# Responsibilities of the Data Subjects

Associated to the exercise of rights are responsibilities to respect the privacy of others and comply with Gordon College’s regulations in its administration of data privacy.

Data subjects shall have the following responsibilities:

1. Respect the data privacy rights of others;
2. Report any suspected security incident or personal data breach to Gordon College Data Protection Officer by sending an email or accomplishing OCP-DPU Form No. 4;
3. Provide Gordon College with true and accurate personal data and other information. Before submitting personal d
label: responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects/]
don College with true and accurate personal data and other information. Before submitting personal data of different people to Gordon College, obtain the consent of such people.
4. Not disclose to any unauthorized party any non-public confidential, sensitive or personal information obtained or learned in confidence from Gordon College; and
5. Abide by the policies, guidelines and rules of Gordon College on data privacy, information security, records management, research and ethical conduct. From time to time, check for and comply with updates on these policies, guidelines and rules. Students m
label: responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects/]
 time to time, check for and comply with updates on these policies, guidelines and rules. Students may also refer to their Student Handbook.

# Final presentation of Project METRO

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, April 17, 2026**

April 16, 2026 | Final presentation of Project METRO: Monitoring Enforcement Tracking Resource Online with DTI Zambales and Gordon College – College of Computer Studies.

Present during the final presentation are DTI3 CPD Acting Division Chief Jose Antonio Toledo, DTI Zambales CPD Division Chief Neil John M. Fabay, Gordon College CCS Dean Ronnie D. Luy, Gordon College CCS Asst Dean Armilyn T. Martinez, and Gordon College CCS Prof. Reynaldo G. Bautista. The panelist provide
label: final-presentation-of-project-metro

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/final-presentation-of-project-metro/]
st Dean Armilyn T. Martinez, and Gordon College CCS Prof. Reynaldo G. Bautista. The panelist provided expert guidance and valuable insights to further refining the system.

Project METRO is intended to make market inspections and fair trade monitoring more efficient, transparent, and fully digitized. In practical terms, the system aims to help government personnel carry out inspections more systematically while improving documentation, reporting, and coordination in the field.

The use of a digital platform for inspections will also help strengthen public trust by making enforcement more trans
label: final-presentation-of-project-metro

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/final-presentation-of-project-metro/]
tal platform for inspections will also help strengthen public trust by making enforcement more transparent and easier to document. In many cases, digitized systems can support more consistent monitoring while reducing gaps in manual processes.

# Empowering Solo Parents: Gordon College Launches ‘Weave of Strength’ Initiative

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, April 8, 2026**

Empowering communities through sustainable livelihood and inclusive development, Weave of Strength: Turning Scraps into Sustainable Livelihood was conducted on April 7, 2026 at the Gordon College Function Hall. The initiative provided solo parents and economically disadvantaged individuals with practical skills in transforming recycled fabric scraps into sustainable sources of income.

The program featured an inspiring message
label: empowering-solo-parents-gordon-college-launches-weave-of-strength-initiative

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/empowering-solo-parents-gordon-college-launches-weave-of-strength-initiative/]
cycled fabric scraps into sustainable sources of income.

The program featured an inspiring message from College President Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano and the support of Vice President for RDCES, Dr. Ediric D. Gadia, reinforcing the college’s commitment to community service and development.

With the support of the Rotary Club of Downtown Olongapo, and through the coordinated efforts of RDCES and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management as the implementing unit, the initiative reflects Gordon College’s core values of Character, Excellence, and Service.

To ensure long-term sustainability,
label: empowering-solo-parents-gordon-college-launches-weave-of-strength-initiative

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/empowering-solo-parents-gordon-college-launches-weave-of-strength-initiative/]
n College’s core values of Character, Excellence, and Service.

To ensure long-term sustainability, the initiative adopts a capacity-building approach by equipping administrative personnel to serve as future facilitators, enabling continuous implementation and expanded community reach.

# Website Policy

### Website Committee

Gordon College (GC) Website Committee was formally established by Interoffice Memorandum No. 38 Re: Website Committee. The Interoffice Memorandum states the following; composition, duties, and responsibilities of Website Committee, Guidelines in Website Content Development and Management, and Procedure for Posting/Uploading Web Content.

---

### Composition

GC Website Committee will be composed of a Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Website Administrator, and Members.  
  
For the members, it will be the Head of the Information and Communication Services
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
and Members.  
  
For the members, it will be the Head of the Information and Communication Services Unit (ICSU), one (1) faculty member from each department designated by the College Dean, and one (1) staff from each office/unit assigned by the Unit Head.

---

### Duties and Responsibilities of Website Committee

Based on Interoffice Memo No. 38, the following are the duties and responsibilities of the committee:

1. Develop and recommend web related policies and standards
2. Oversee the design and underlying technology
3. Ensure the website stays current with new technology/trends
4. Implem
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
n and underlying technology
3. Ensure the website stays current with new technology/trends
4. Implement regular data collection methods
5. Use data collected to guide the framework and infrastructure of the college website
6. Create guiding principles to steer the framework and infrastructure decisions of the college website
7. Work with the Management Information System Unit (MISU) to set timelines for web engine upgrade and new website deployments.
8. Regularly assess the design of the website and make recommendations for improvement
9. Serve as the liaison between the College President and
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
 and make recommendations for improvement
9. Serve as the liaison between the College President and the different department regards content
10. Identify and advocate for web needs and services for the college community.

---

### Guidelines in Website Content Development and Management

1. Interoffice Memo No. 38, clearly states the guidelines to be observed by the committee in terms of the contents for the website.
2. All news, updates and all other content to be posted on the official website should have clearance from concerned departments/ units and approval from the College President.
3.
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
 should have clearance from concerned departments/ units and approval from the College President.
3. The Website Committee is tasked to manage the postings on the official website in coordination with the Information and Communication Services Unit (ICSU). The committee shall formulate posting guidelines and ensure proper dissemination.
4. Queries, concerns or issues aired through the official website must be communicated to concerned departments through ICSU who shall also responsible for posting official responses to such queries, concerns or issues whether through online or offline as the s
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
ng official responses to such queries, concerns or issues whether through online or offline as the situation warrants.

---

### Procedure for Posting/Uploading Web Content

The following are the guidelines stated in Interoffice Memo No. 38 in terms of posting/uploading content to the college website:

1. Requesting department should submit a copy of the post to ICSU for approval of the College President.
2. After approval, ICSU shall transmit the content to the Website Committee Chairperson and Web Administrator.

---

### Website Content Submission

The GC Website Committee is pleased to off
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
 Web Administrator.

---

### Website Content Submission

The GC Website Committee is pleased to offer the opportunity to share knowledge and expertise by submitting content to the college website with the following guidelines:

* **Guiding Principles**  
  Gordon College is one of the institutions that promote excellent education in Region III and the country. As such, the committee would like to publish and post content, materials, and ideas that support the vision, mission, and goals of the college. The committee also encourages each individual from different colleges and departments to sha
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
ollege. The committee also encourages each individual from different colleges and departments to share their experience and insights as one of the people of Gordon College.
* **Style**  
  The Website committee encourages each and everyone to use the current best practices for web writing. These includes:
  + Begin with the conclusion by focusing on the main message and be clear on how you want the reader to feel and respond.
  + Add specific details, facts, figures, quotes, etc.
  + Submit at least one (1) graphs, graphics, or photos that are copyright free with a size of;
    - 1280 pixels b
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
east one (1) graphs, graphics, or photos that are copyright free with a size of;
    - 1280 pixels by 720 pixels for Hero Banners (Congratulatory Posters)
    - 1200 pixels by 630 pixels for News, Announcement and Notice
    - 250 pixels by 250 pixels for Website Banner
    - 250 pixels by 100 pixels for Logo (Rectangle)
    - 100 pixels by 100 pixels for Logo (Square)
  + Graphs, graphics or photos should be in .jpeg, .png or .svg format
  + Use subheadings that reflect the content and captures the interests of readers
  + Use listed points with ten (10) words or less per item
  + Search the
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
 the interests of readers
  + Use listed points with ten (10) words or less per item
  + Search the web for other pro tips on best practices
* **Publishing**  
  The committee does not guarantee that it will post your submissions automatically. All published contents shall be well-guided and must receive the approval of concerned departments, as stated on Interoffice Memorandum No. 38.It does guarantee, however, that the article will be read and explore whether or not it is a good fit for the college and the readership. In reviewing submissions, the committee is looking for content that is nec
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
llege and the readership. In reviewing submissions, the committee is looking for content that is necessary and relevant to the needs of the stakeholders, especially with the faculty and students. Articles reflecting the inspiring, powerful, responsible, productive, and connected nature of the academe are the ones that we are looking for.
* **Copyright**  
  The committee will only post copyright-free materials submitted by the author and will not pay for articles posted on the website. The college reserves the right to use the submitted article or any part of it depending on the committee’s di
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
ge reserves the right to use the submitted article or any part of it depending on the committee’s discretion.
* **Response Time**  
  The goal of the committee is to respond to the requestor as quickly as possible. Depending on the situation, the committee will do its best to respond to each submission in 1 to 3 days.
* **Requestor**  
  The following groups and/or individuals are allowed to submit content for posting:
  + Department Heads
  + Vice Presidents
  + Faculty Members and
  + Administrative Staff
  + Recognized Student Organizations
  + Alumni

### Content Administration

All web content submitted must be approved prior to posting. The following individuals retain the right to
label: website-policy

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/website-policy/]
b content submitted must be approved prior to posting. The following individuals retain the right to edit or request changes:

* College President
* Website Administrator
* Head of ICSU
* Vice Presidents
* Requestor/Author of the Content

---

### Website Content Update/ Revision

Thou postings are checked for spelling, grammar and factual accuracy, there will be some instances that can result unwanted errors.

The GC Website Committee will allow update or revision of articles posted on the website for the following reasons;

* Missing or Duplicate Titles
* Misspelled names
* Overlooked misspe

ite for the following reasons;

* Missing or Duplicate Titles
* Misspelled names
* Overlooked misspelled words or grammatical errors
* Missing images or links
* Missing details or information

---

### Website Content Removal

The GC Website Committee reserves the right to remove any posted content that contains copyrighted materials which include, but not limited to: text, images, graphics, sound recordings, video recordings or software programs.

# Rights of the Data Subjects

The stakeholders are the primary data subjects of Gordon College. They are the students, their parents or guardians, the employees (regardless of their employment status), officials, visitors, donors/ sponsors, service providers, and the like are entitled to the following rights with regards to their personal data:

---

## Rights to be Informed

* The data subject has a right to be informed whether personal data pertaining to him or her shall be, are being, or have been processed, including the existence of automated decision-making and profiling.
* The data sub
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects
rights-of-the-data-subjects
rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
cance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject;
  + The identity and contact details of Gordon College or its representative;
  + The period for which the information will be stored; and
  + The existence of their rights as data subjects, including the right to access, correction, and object to the processing, as well as the right to lodge a complaint before the National Privacy Commission.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/the-right-to-be-informed/>

---

## Right to Object

The data subject shall have the right to object to the proce
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
be-informed/>

---

## Right to Object

The data subject shall have the right to object to the processing of his or her personal data, including processing for direct marketing, automated processing or profiling. The data subject shall also be notified and given an opportunity to withhold consent to the processing in case of changes or any amendment to the information supplied or declared to the data subject in the preceding paragraph.  
  
When a data subject objects or withholds consent, Gordon College may not be able to conduct academic, administrative and other functions or services relate
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
n College may not be able to conduct academic, administrative and other functions or services related to the data subject.  
  
When a data subject objects or withholds consent, Gordon College shall no longer process the personal data, unless:

* The personal data is needed pursuant to a subpoena;
* The collection and processing are for obvious purposes, including, when it is necessary for the performance of or in relation to a contract or service to which the data subject is a party, or when necessary or desirable in the context of an employer-employee relationship between the collector and t
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
cessary or desirable in the context of an employer-employee relationship between the collector and the data subject;
* The information is being collected and processed as a result of a legal obligation; or
* Gordon College’s functions as the local college or as an instrumentality of the government will be impaired.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-object/>

---

## Right to Access

The data subject has the right to reasonable access to, upon demand providing sufficient time for preparation, the following:

* Contents of his or her personal data that were
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects
rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
data subject;
  + Date when his or her personal data concerning the data subject were last accessed and modified; and
  + The description of Gordon College under Olongapo City Ordinance 07 Series of 2018.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-access/>

---

## Right to Correct/ Rectification

The data subject has the right to dispute the inaccuracy or error in the personal data and have Gordon College correct it within a reasonable period and accordingly, unless the request is vexatious or otherwise unreasonable. If the personal data has been corrected, Gordon
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
 the request is vexatious or otherwise unreasonable. If the personal data has been corrected, Gordon College shall ensure the accessibility of both the new and the retracted information and the simultaneous receipt of the new and the retracted information by the intended recipients thereof: provided, that recipients or third parties who have previously received such processed personal data shall be informed of its inaccuracy and its rectification, upon reasonable request of the Data Subject.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-rectify/>

---

## Right to Blo
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
r more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-rectify/>

---

## Right to Block/ Remove/ Erasure/ Be Forgotten

These rights of erasure and blocking do not apply to personal data, documents, records and accounts which are part of Gordon College public records as an instrumentality of the government or as the local college.

The data subject shall have the right to suspend, withdraw or order the blocking, removal or destruction of his or her personal data from Gordon College’s filing system.

* This right may be exercised upon discovery and substantial proof of any of the
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects
rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
itimate interest for the processing;
  + The processing is unlawful;
  + Gordon College personal information processor violated the rights of the data subject.
* Gordon College may notify third parties who have previously received such  
  processed personal data.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-erasure-or-blocking/>

---

## Right to File a Complaint

You have the right to lodge a complaint if you are the subject of a data privacy violation.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-file-a-complaint/>

---

## Right to be I
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
ormation you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-file-a-complaint/>

---

## Right to be Indemnified/ to Damages

This right to damages is subordinate to:

* The presumption of Gordon College regularity in the performance of government functions;
* As applicable, the principle that the State may not be sued without its consent; and
* The non-liability of Gordon College arising from the incidental damages due to Gordon College’s pursuance of its mandates or compliance with its legal obligations.

The data subject shall be indemnified for any damages sustained due to such inaccurate, i
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
igations.

The data subject shall be indemnified for any damages sustained due to such inaccurate, incomplete, outdated, false, unlawfully obtained or unauthorized use of personal data, taking into account any violation of his or her rights and freedoms as data subject.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-damages/>

---

## Right to Portability

As a data subject, you have the right to obtain from the PIC a copy of your personal data and/or have the same transmitted from one PIC to another, in an electronic or structured format that is commonly used.

For mo
label: rights-of-the-data-subjects

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects/]
mitted from one PIC to another, in an electronic or structured format that is commonly used.

For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-data-portability/>

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
# Gordon College Data Privacy General Statement

This is Gordon College’s general statement on its data processing activities to notify data subjects of categories of personal data processed and the purpose and extent of processing. This is not a consent form but an announcement how Gordon College processes personal data.

---

### Types of Information Collected

As an educational institution, Gordon College operates in an environment where it collects information from multiple stakeholders. It is done in many ways that best serve its legitimate interests and purposes for processing and managi
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
is done in many ways that best serve its legitimate interests and purposes for processing and managing personal information. The college wants that the stakeholders understand the types of information it collects as they engage with it. If the stakeholders choose to withhold personal data that Gordon College needs to process, it may be unable, in some circumstances, to comply with its obligations. The college will inform them about the implications of the decision.

1. Information Collected from Students
   1. Information Collected from Prospective Applicants
   2. Information Collected from E
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
 Students
   1. Information Collected from Prospective Applicants
   2. Information Collected from Enrolled Students
   3. Information Collected from Alumni
2. Information Collected from Personnel
   1. Information Collected from Job Applicants
   2. Information From Current Employees
   3. Academic Freedom and Privacy for Faculty
3. Information Collected from Parents/ Legal Guardians
4. Information Collected from Visitors and Guests
5. Information Collected from Vendors, Suppliers, and Service Providers
6. Information Collected from Donors, Benefactors, and Sponsors
7. Information Collected d
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement
gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
ormation such as grades, course and academic standing;
* Employment information such as government-issued numbers, position and functions;
* Applicant information such as academic background and previous employments;
* Medical information such as physical, psychiatric and psychological information.

---

### Processing of Personal Information

Gordon College expects that the following uses will fall within the category of its “legitimate interests”:

1. For Delivery of Education
   1. For Provision of Educational Support and Related Services
   2. For the Exercise of the College’s Pastoral/ Gu
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
ion of Educational Support and Related Services
   2. For the Exercise of the College’s Pastoral/ Guidance Responsibilities
   3. For Communication and Documentation
   4. For Statistical Research, Other Research Studies and Archival Purposes
   5. For Ensuring the Safety and Security of Stakeholders
   6. For Complying with Legal and Contractual Obligations
   7. For the Administration of Human Resources
2. Legal Bases on Processing Personal Information
   1. The Higher Education Act of 1994 (Republic Act No. 7722)
   2. The Family Code of the Philippines (Executive Order No. 209)
   3. The C
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
epublic Act No. 7722)
   2. The Family Code of the Philippines (Executive Order No. 209)
   3. The Commission on Higher Education’s (CHED) Manual of Regulation for Private Higher Education
   4. Olongapo City Ordinance No. 07 Series of 2018 or the “Revised Charter of Gordon College of 2018”
   5. Laws or regulations which amend or repeal the foregoing.
3. Other Bases of Authorized Processing of Information
   1. Consent
   2. Contractual Obligations
   3. Legal Obligations
   4. Protection of Vital Interest
   5. Medical Treatment
   6. Other Lawful and Non-commercial Objectives
   7. Public O
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
tal Interest
   5. Medical Treatment
   6. Other Lawful and Non-commercial Objectives
   7. Public Order and Safety
   8. By Virtue of Public Authority

---

### Ways to Collect Personal Information

Gordon College collects Personal Data physically through printed forms, attachments, and other documents required by the college, its academic units, and its administrative offices. Gordon College collects Personal Data electronically through electronic forms, via email, or inputting of information directly by the data subject or by the concerned faculty, personnel, and other stakeholders.

---

#
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
directly by the data subject or by the concerned faculty, personnel, and other stakeholders.

---

### Accuracy of Information

Gordon College prioritizes the correctness of the personal information of its stakeholders. With this, the college implements;

1. Verification of Personal Information
2. Correction, or Update of Personal Information

---

### Disclosure, Transfer or Sharing of Personal Information

As part of Gordon College’s legitimate interests as an educational institution, it needs to transfer and/or share personal information within and across college departments and offices rel
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
 to transfer and/or share personal information within and across college departments and offices relevant to the processes involved.

1. In doing so, the college makes sure it adheres to the following guidelines;
   * The transfer or sharing has the consent of the stakeholder, when needed
   * The transfer or sharing is governed by approved contractual clauses or an arrangement providing an adequate standard of data protection and third parties performing functions for them are informed regarding appropriate handling of their personal information.
   * The transfer or sharing is necessary for
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
g appropriate handling of their personal information.
   * The transfer or sharing is necessary for the performance of a contract with another person or company/service provider, which is in the interests of the college
   * The transfer or sharing is necessary for the performance of a contract with the stakeholders or take steps requested by the college to entering into that contract

Gordon College abstains from renting, selling, or sharing personal information about its stakeholder with other non-affiliated people, entities or third parties.

In general, the college will disclose or share i
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
 non-affiliated people, entities or third parties.

In general, the college will disclose or share its stakeholders to non-affiliates only with their consent or under the following circumstances:

1. Legal Obligation as Educational Institution and Reportorial Obligations as Employer
2. Contractual Obligations
   * Industry Partners
   * Benefactors, Donors and Sponsors
   * Essential Service Provider
   * Accreditation and Quality Assurance
   * Legitimate Interests of the College in Sports Activities
   * Parents and Legal Guardians *(for minor students)*
   * Public Consumption
   * Law Enfo
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
vities
   * Parents and Legal Guardians *(for minor students)*
   * Public Consumption
   * Law Enforcement and Local Authorities

---

### Store, Retain Personal Information

Gordon College makes it a practice to store and transmit data securely in a number of ways, including manual paper and electronic formats, including databases that are shared between and among the different units or offices of the college. Access to personal data is limited to the respective college personnel who have legitimate interest for the purpose of carrying out contractual duties. It shall only collect and store
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
timate interest for the purpose of carrying out contractual duties. It shall only collect and store information that is necessary to achieve its legitimate purposes and/or when permitted by law.

Unless otherwise provided by law or by appropriate college policies, the basic academic records for individual students are kept permanently and in perpetuity by the college, with more detailed records kept for defined retention periods. Each unit or office processing personal data have their respective retention policies, after which, all affected records will be securely disposed of.

There are also
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
 retention policies, after which, all affected records will be securely disposed of.

There are also some categories of data that the college retain for historical, archival and statistical purposes, unless otherwise provided by law or by applicable college policies.

---

### Information Security Measures

Gordon College shall exercise every practicable and reasonable means to protect personal information and to ensure the security of personal data about individuals through appropriate organizational, physical and technical measures. This includes policies around the use of technology and dev
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
tional, physical and technical measures. This includes policies around the use of technology and devices and the access to college systems. All personnel and faculty will be made aware of these policies and their duties under the Data Privacy Act of 2012 and receive relevant training at least once a year.

The college implements security measures in the following aspects;

1. Organizational Security Measures
2. Physical Security Measures
3. Technical Security Measures

---

### Participation of Stakeholders

Gordon College encourages that its stakeholders should be aware on the importance of t
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement
gordon-college-general-privacy-statement
gordon-college-general-privacy-statement
gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
* Abide by the policies, guidelines and rules of Gordon College on data privacy, information security, records management, research and ethical conduct.

Other rights and responsibilities of stakeholders are in the **Gordon College Data Privacy Manual**.

---

### Breach and Security Incidents

The mitigation, management and resolution of Security Incidents and Personal Data Breaches requires the coordination of various stakeholders. All concerned should be vigilant in their responsibilities to enable an effective security incident management process.

Gordon College forms a Data Breach Respon
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
enable an effective security incident management process.

Gordon College forms a Data Breach Response Team that will assess and evaluate the Security Incidents, which includes Personal Data Breaches, restore integrity to the information and communication systems, mitigate and remedy resulting damages, and comply with reportorial requirements.

The following incident management and notification procedure is established in the Gordon College Security Incident Management Policy, this is divided into three major steps;

1. **Incident Response Procedure**  
   • Step 1 – Reporting  
   • Step 2 –
label: gordon-college-general-privacy-statement

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-general-privacy-statement/]
to three major steps;

1. **Incident Response Procedure**  
   • Step 1 – Reporting  
   • Step 2 – Categorization  
   • Step 3 – Investigation
2. **Breach Notification**  
   • Step 4 – Reporting and Notification
3. **Mitigation Response Plan**  
   • Step 5 – Containment  
   • Step 6 – Recovery  
   • Step 7 – Feedback  
   • Step 8 – Learning

# GC holds mental health seminar, boosts self-harm awareness among students

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, March 23, 2026**

To raise awareness on self-harm, the Gordon College – Office of Student Welfare and Services (GC-OSWS), in collaboration with GC – Guidance Counseling Unit (GC-GCU), conducted the “Nature of Self-Harm: Awareness, Compassion, and Prevention” seminar last March 13 at the Function Hall, GC – Main Building.

Graced by Resource Speaker Erin RiegodeDios Asio, a Master of Arts in Education Major in Behavioral Sciences graduate, the seminar fe
label: gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students/]
iegodeDios Asio, a Master of Arts in Education Major in Behavioral Sciences graduate, the seminar featured how Non-Suicidal Self-Injury (NSSI), also known as self-harm, is different from suicide.

According to Asio, self-harm is a way of coping intense feelings by hurting oneself on purpose. The intention of doing self-harm is to feel alive and escape emotional pain.

Additionally, she stated that self-harm used to be listed as a symptom of Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD), a pervasive disorder that affects the whole personality. Most people who self-harm do not have BPD, but people who h
label: gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students/]
rder that affects the whole personality. Most people who self-harm do not have BPD, but people who have BPD may have these tendencies.

The seminar went further and discussed the different types of self-harm: cutting, burning, and hitting. If NSSI is done occasionally, there is a risk of making an unintentional mistake that could lead to accidental death.

Asio explained the phases of taking action when someone is afflicted with self-harm. Where the first phase is listening to someone when they open up about hurting themselves. Then, offer support toward counseling.

Then followed by the cause
label: gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-holds-mental-health-seminar-boosts-self-harm-awareness-among-students/]
open up about hurting themselves. Then, offer support toward counseling.

Then followed by the causes, myths, and how to cope with self-harm in a healthy way. She also told students that they should check up on each other afterwards.

The keynote speaker also underscored the importance of promoting a stigma-free, healthy, and supportive environment within the school.

Students are welcome at the GCU office for free counseling services to those struggling with their mental health. | via Jasmine Zeah Llagas/The FOREFRONT

Photos by Lheeon Owen Dela Rosa and Marian Sualibio

**Saturday, August 19, 2023**

The Reading Literacy Program expanded its scope to include learners within the DepEd system facing reading challenges, such as non-readers and those with difficulties in English and Filipino. The program held sessions on June 9 and 22, as well as August 12 and 19, attracting voluntary participation. Despite the short duration, significant impro
label: mrps-reading-program

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mrps-reading-program/]
 August 12 and 19, attracting voluntary participation. Despite the short duration, significant improvements were observed in the majority of learners. The culminating activity, conducted simultaneously across 17 barangays in Olongapo City, was highly successful.

The program’s success was achieved through collaboration among student facilitators, immersion instructors, CESU coordinators, local barangay representatives, the mayor’s office, and the Rotary Club of Downtown Olongapo City. The Rotary Club played a crucial role in printing the booklets used during the sessions.

# Enhancing Reading Literacy Training

**Saturday, June 24, 2023**

A successful whole-day training was conducted through a collaborative effort involving the GC CESU, Academic Affairs, CEAS immersion instructors, and dedicated volunteers from the DepEd teaching staff. The training’s success can be attributed to the contributions of Mr. Willy R. Antigo, Ms. Enggelyn Yandoc, Ms. Meryl Bustos, and Ms.
label: enhancing-reading-literacy-training

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/enhancing-reading-literacy-training/]
tributed to the contributions of Mr. Willy R. Antigo, Ms. Enggelyn Yandoc, Ms. Meryl Bustos, and Ms. Mara Grace Inocencio, who served as resource trainers and played crucial roles in its smooth execution. The training saw enthusiastic participation from a diverse group of student facilitators, representing ten different programs within the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences. There were 117 trainees in the morning session and 117 in the afternoon session.

**Saturday, September 9, 2023**

The GC Community Extension Services Unit enthusiastically responded to the PAO’s call for volunteers for the MRP: Rivereth Possible Tree Planting Activity at Banka-Bangkaan, Gordon Heights. The event was successfully conducted with active participation from students, faculty members, and administrative personnel from the GC community. Our strong
label: mrps-tree-planting

content:

ation from students, faculty members, and administrative personnel from the GC community. Our strong commitment to environmental sustainability and community engagement drove our involvement in this initiative. We firmly believe that by fostering the active engagement of our employees and volunteers in such endeavors, we exemplify our organizational values and reinforce our dedication to corporate social responsibility.

# Cosme Y. Sering Library is Now Open

[LIMC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/limc/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, January 18, 2025**

Today marks a significant milestone for Gordon College, as we celebrate the blessing of the newly renovated Cosme Y. Sering Library. Made possible through the generous donation of the Acevedo Family, this library serves as a vital resource for students, faculty, and the Olongapo City community, promoting learning, collaboration, and access to knowledge.

The Acevedo Family’s generosity exemplifies their commitment to ed
label: cosme-y-sering-library-is-now-open

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/cosme-y-sering-library-is-now-open/]
ration, and access to knowledge.

The Acevedo Family’s generosity exemplifies their commitment to education and community development. This improved facility enhances academic opportunities and enriches the educational experience for students and stakeholders alike.

We extend our deepest gratitude to the Acevedo Family, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino, Former City Mayor, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., Olongapo City Mayor, Hon/ Cristabelle Marie “Tata” Paulino, Committee on Education Chairperson, Hon. Sarah Lugerna Lipumano-Garcia, City Councilor, Hon. Benjamin G. **Sunday, September 18, 2022**

Welcome Back to School at Gordon College is phenomenal as students spend their spare time inside the library reading books. Students from different departments utilize the library in doing their homework or completing their reading materials. This is highly unexpected because of the recent pandemic.

Since the recent pandemic, Gordon College has implemented a lot of new rules for students to follow in ord
label: limc-packed-with-students-as-new-school-year-opens

the recent pandemic, Gordon College has implemented a lot of new rules for students to follow in order for them to be safe and healthy. The same goes with the library since it is a place where students get to spend some time studying for their upcoming exams and projects. In fact, students are not allowed to bring food inside the library since not all food are safe to eat anymore because of lack of proper sanitation and hygiene practices during meal preparation.

Despite these new rules being implemented by Gordon College, many students still prefer staying inside the library because it gives
label: limc-packed-with-students-as-new-school-year-opens

plemented by Gordon College, many students still prefer staying inside the library because it gives them an opportunity to study without any distractions from other people or places around campus.

# GC Library Renovation: 40% Done

[LIMC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/limc/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, August 19, 2024**

The Gordon College library is currently undergoing renovation project which aims at transforming the space into a more dynamic and student-friendly environment.

In an interview with The Forefront, Mr. Marlon Atencio, the college librarian, provided detailed updates and valuable insights into the objectives, current developments, and anticipated benefits of the project for students and faculty alike.

According to Mr. Atencio,
label: gc-library-renovation-40-done

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-library-renovation-40-done/]
, and anticipated benefits of the project for students and faculty alike.

According to Mr. Atencio, the upgraded library is shifting towards a “learning commons” setup.

“Kasi yung college library natin ang mangyayari ay parang learning commons na siya, kumbaga parang café setup,” he shared.

In addition, the college librarian announced plans to enhance the library by installing vending machines, similar to those at ground floor, allowing students to enjoy snacks—though only solid foods, other snacks, will be permitted, with liquids excluded.

He further mentioned that the timeline for the re
label: gc-library-renovation-40-done

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-library-renovation-40-done/]
snacks, will be permitted, with liquids excluded.

He further mentioned that the timeline for the renovation’s completion remains flexible.

“Wala naman binigay na timeline, ang gusto lang manyari is as soon as possible matapos siya para siyempre magamit na ito ng mga estudyante.” he added

As of now, the renovation is around 40% complete. Key areas such as the computer section and audiovisual room are being redesigned with high-quality materials.

Moreover, he also shared that various areas, such as the flooring are already underway and the renovation is focusing on upgrading both the structu
label: gc-library-renovation-40-done

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-library-renovation-40-done/]
ch as the flooring are already underway and the renovation is focusing on upgrading both the structural and aesthetic aspects of the library to ensure a premium experience for GCians.

Students and faculty can look forward to an enhanced library experience. The space are expected to be more conducive to learning with upgraded seating, including sofas and modern furniture.

Furthermore, the college librarian also mentioned that they should not only expect the library to be well-maintained but also that its services will be improved to ensure that students can use it effectively.

In addressing
label: gc-library-renovation-40-done

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-library-renovation-40-done/]
o that its services will be improved to ensure that students can use it effectively.

In addressing the specific needs of students and faculty, the library will host separate orientation sessions to familiarize students with the new setup.

“Kaya hindi kami nakisabay sa student orientation kasi magkakaroon kami ng separate orientation sa library, wherein ang gagawin namin ay mag-kakaroon ng library tour ang bawat section, para alam nila kung paano gamitin lahat ng area, para maiwasan din natin [na] nahihirapan kung paano i-access [ang] mga gamit, pati mga electronics resources lahat ‘yon it-ta
label: gc-library-renovation-40-done

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-library-renovation-40-done/]
na] nahihirapan kung paano i-access [ang] mga gamit, pati mga electronics resources lahat ‘yon it-tackle natin during orientation,” he explains.

Next, he also discussed changes in the physical layout, such as new glass doors and relocating the lockers outside the library.

Additionally, he encouraged students to reach out through the library’s Facebook page for online inquiries.

Once completed, the renovated Gordon College library promises to be a welcoming and efficient learning space for all that fosters both learning and collaboration.

**Thursday, May 29, 2025**

The Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) in partnership with the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) and Public Employment Service Office (PESO), conducted their annual Labor Education for Graduating Students (LEGS) 2025 yesterday, May 28, at the SMX Convention Center Olongapo.

The morning session included students from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and the College of Hospitality and Tourism
label: %f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%83%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%84-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a4

dents from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) assisted by the Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).

Meanwhile, the event continued until afternoon for the graduating students of College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS), College of Computer Studies (CCS), and College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS).

Started by Mr. Arvin Fabian, Senior Labor Employment Officer of DOLE, the seminar aims to equip its participants with sufficient knowledge regarding labor standards.

“Ang mandate po kasi ng DOLE is to e
label: %f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%83%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%84-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a4

ticipants with sufficient knowledge regarding labor standards.

“Ang mandate po kasi ng DOLE is to ensure that everyone receive[s] what is due to them,” assured Mr. Fabian.

Additionally, this event was led by Ms. Jehan Doblado, Career Education and Job Placement Services Unit (CEJPSU) Coordinator, with the goal of preparing graduating studentsfor the corporate world.

“Para hindi sila zero knowledge. [Para] pagka-graduate nila alam nila ang gagawin nila,” the CEJPSU Coordinator said.

In her opening remarks, Ms. Lovelyn Ceralde, the Vice President of the Office of Student Welfare and Services
label: %f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%83%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%84-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a4

ening remarks, Ms. Lovelyn Ceralde, the Vice President of the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS), stressed how necessary it is to study these standards.

“It is important that you, our future leaders and contributors, are well-versed in these fundamental principles,” said Ms. Ceralde.

She also highlighted the upcoming Commencement Exercises in July for the graduating batch 2025.

“See you on July 9 with your black togas!” the OSWS Vice President noted.

In an interview with The Forefront, some participants from different departments shared their insights on how the LEGS 2025 helped
label: %f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%83%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%84-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a4

ront, some participants from different departments shared their insights on how the LEGS 2025 helped them as a graduating student of Gordon College.

“Ang laking tulong nitong seminar saken as a graduating student para maging prepared sa mga kakailanganin ko as a first time job seeker,” Jordan Magbanua, CCS Graduating Student claimed.

“Very helpful para sa mga graduating students lalo na sa akin kasi sa personal experience ko na naging employee na nag-resign para magpatuloy ang pag-aaral. Na-enlighten ako sa seminar na ito,” CEAS Graduating Student said.

label: %f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%83%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%84-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a4

lighten ako sa seminar na ito,” CEAS Graduating Student said.

Moreover, this event eyes recurrence every year in face-to-face set-up to ensure that graduating students remain well-informed regarding their rights as current or future employees.

**Thursday, February 13, 2025**

SAN FERNANDO, Pampanga – The Forefront, the official student publication of Gordon College, garnered five awards at the 5th Assumption Outstanding Campus Press Awards (AOCPA) held at the University of the Assumption on January 27, 2025.

# New Supreme Student Council proclaimed

[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/) [SSC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ssc/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, May 6, 2025**

FRONTLINES | 𝑰𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝒄𝒂𝒍𝒍 𝒕𝒐 𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒏𝒌 𝒘𝒊𝒔𝒆 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝒗𝒐𝒕𝒆 𝒔𝒎𝒂𝒓𝒕, 𝒄𝒂𝒏 𝒂𝒏𝒚𝒐𝒏𝒆 𝒓𝒆𝒂𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒔𝒂𝒚, ‘𝑰 𝒕𝒉𝒐𝒖𝒈𝒉𝒕 𝒘𝒊𝒔𝒆𝒍𝒚 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝒗𝒐𝒕𝒆𝒅 𝒔𝒎𝒂𝒓𝒕’?

As the election results unfold, the following were proclaimed the new Supreme Student Council for A.Y. 2025-2026:

𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐧: 𝐒𝐚𝐦𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞, 𝐉𝐮𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐫

𝐕𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐧: 𝐏𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐚𝐬, 𝐃𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚 𝐃𝐞𝐥

𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐲: 𝐒𝐚𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐚𝐩, 𝐀𝐧𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐲 𝐀.

𝐓𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐫: 𝐃𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐝, 𝐏𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐜𝐞

𝐀𝐮𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐨𝐫: 𝐃𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐨, 𝐏𝐡𝐨𝐞𝐛𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧𝐞
label: new-supreme-student-council-proclaimed

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/new-supreme-student-council-proclaimed/]
, 𝐃𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚 𝐃𝐞𝐥

𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐲: 𝐒𝐚𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐚𝐩, 𝐀𝐧𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐲 𝐀.

𝐓𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐫: 𝐃𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐝, 𝐏𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐜𝐞

𝐀𝐮𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐨𝐫: 𝐃𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐨, 𝐏𝐡𝐨𝐞𝐛𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧𝐞 𝐒.

𝐏𝐈𝐎: 𝐄𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐚, 𝐉𝐨𝐡𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐢 𝐄.

𝐂𝐀𝐇𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐔𝐦𝐚𝐝𝐡𝐚𝐲, 𝐑𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐫 𝐓𝐫𝐨𝐬

𝐂𝐁𝐀 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐚𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐧, 𝐍𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧 𝐃.

𝐂𝐂𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐀𝐥𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐨, 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐃𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐝

𝐂𝐄𝐀𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐄𝐛𝐚𝐥𝐨, 𝐋𝐲𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐝 𝐉𝐚𝐲 𝐏.

𝐂𝐇𝐓𝐌 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐘𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐲𝐚𝐦𝐚, 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐘𝐨𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐤𝐨 𝐃.

As the students answer the call to vote wisely—which did not and will not end there—echoes collective raps to this new set of leaders about the accountability and responsibility of their respective positions.

Nothing much was asked by the student

ountability and responsibility of their respective positions.

# PRC On-site Caravan 2025 Lands with a Bang

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [IPDEA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ipdea/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, May 9, 2025**

𝐏𝐑𝐎𝐅𝐄𝐒𝐒𝐈𝐎𝐍𝐀𝐋 𝐑𝐄𝐆𝐔𝐋𝐀𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍 𝐂𝐎𝐌𝐌𝐈𝐒𝐒𝐈𝐎𝐍 (𝐏𝐑𝐂) 𝐎𝐍-𝐒𝐈𝐓𝐄 𝐒𝐄𝐑𝐕𝐈𝐂𝐄 𝐂𝐀𝐑𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐍

GORDON COLLEGE | OLONGAPO CITY, ZAMBALES

May 9, 2025

It’s a wrap!

The PRC On-Site Service Caravan at Gordon College was a resounding success, delivering a total of 549 services to Professionals in a single day. This initiative significantly improved
label: prc-on-site-caravan-2025-was-launched

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/prc-on-site-caravan-2025-was-launched/]
ng a total of 549 services to Professionals in a single day. This initiative significantly improved access to essential PRC services within the local community—and is part of our ongoing commitment to support not only our Alumni but also to all Professionals.

Breakdown of Services Rendered:

• Renewal – 138

• Initial Registration – 60

• Duplicate – 11

• Certification – 170

• Authentication – 162

• Certificate of Special Order (COS) – 8

TOTAL – 549

A special acknowledgment to the dedicated PRC Committee for their exemplary service and leadership:

Ms. Elaine Perez Espela, Ms. Glenda B.
label: prc-on-site-caravan-2025-was-launched

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/prc-on-site-caravan-2025-was-launched/]
d PRC Committee for their exemplary service and leadership:

Ms. Elaine Perez Espela, Ms. Glenda B. Sapon (Team Leader), Ms. Joana Benigna Pabelonia, Mr. Joselito Dulay, and Mr. Angelo Pedro Hernandez.

We are also grateful to the student organizations for their invaluable support and active participation:

We are thrilled to announce that our Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap Project, led by Founding Chair Michael P. Tuyay and Special Project Chair Eduardo B. Velasco, has been honored with the Education Stakeholders Award by SDO Olongapo.

# GCian Alumni Makes Name on OSLP 2023

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 11, 2023**

Mr. Marko L. Aratea and Mr. Nieki E. Garigo, both alumni from Gordon College’s College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), stand out as finalists during the award ceremony for Outstanding Student Leaders of the Philippines (OSLP) 2023 held at Teacher’s Camp, Baguio City, on October 1, 2023.

In an interview with The Forefront, Aratea and Garigo claimed that the Office of Student Welfare and Service (OSWS) and
label: gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023/]
h The Forefront, Aratea and Garigo claimed that the Office of Student Welfare and Service (OSWS) and GC-CEAS had announced that they would represent the institution in the aforementioned competition and directed all requirements to Transforming Advocates and Champions (TRAC).

“I was astonished and exhilarated when it was announced that I was one of the top 20 finalists. Knowing that out of 135 applicants across our nation and at various colleges and universities, I was chosen. For me, I consider that one of the great achievements that I had,” Aratea said, reflecting on how he felt when he kne
label: gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023/]
r that one of the great achievements that I had,” Aratea said, reflecting on how he felt when he knew that he was one of the finalists.

While Garigo stated, “Syempre napasigaw ako that time, because bago pa dumating sa point na yan ay marami kaming dinaanang proseso at screening. I still remember the day where they conducted our final interview and sakto pang nakaduty ako sa Cooperating School ko for my practice teaching, and as in, medyo mahina ang signal niyan kaya during my 1-hour interview, ay naroroon ako sa ilalim ng manga ng Regional Science High School para maging stable ang internet,
label: gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023/]
 ay naroroon ako sa ilalim ng manga ng Regional Science High School para maging stable ang internet, and sobrang naging successful naman ng interview dahil every time na may mga ganyang screening, kasi ako I always put in my mind na parang “kachikahan” ko lang ang interviewer at sobrang ganda ng discussion namin, especially yung point na may SDG pa sa question, like I am not ready that time dahil ang nasa isip ko talaga niyan ang lesson sa susunod na subject na tuturuan ko. But thank God because everything went well.”

They expressed their deepest gratitude to TRAC for the opportunity they att
label: gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023/]
 everything went well.”

They expressed their deepest gratitude to TRAC for the opportunity they attained and to their co-finalists from different schools. The representatives would also like to thank Gordon College, the GC Alumni Association, Inc., GC-RDCES, GC-OSWS, GC-CEAS, their families, and lastly, the Almighty God.

“Be who you are, be true to yourself, and always know your advocacy by heart and mind. Think of this competition as a way for you to raise your voice as a student leader, an advocate, and a champion,” Aratea said, while Garigo stated, “Be a leader na hindi tuta; be a leader
label: gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-alumni-makes-name-on-oslp-2023/]
dvocate, and a champion,” Aratea said, while Garigo stated, “Be a leader na hindi tuta; be a leader na boses ng mag-aaral; be a leader who is pro-people, pro-student, and pro-community,” as their advice for those aspiring student leaders of the institution.

Aratea was also hailed as the champion for the Student Research Paper Presentation at the said event.

# Gordon College Celebrates 26th Founding Anniversary with Successful FUNdation Run 2025

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [GCFAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/gcfas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, February 28, 2025**

Gordon College launched its 26th Founding Anniversary festivities through FUNdation Run 2025, which took place on February 23rd.

Gordon College Faculty Association (GCFAS), in partnership with Gordon College Alumni Association Inc. (GCAAI), successfully organize
label: gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025/]
n (GCFAS), in partnership with Gordon College Alumni Association Inc. (GCAAI), successfully organized the event as many students, faculty members, alumni, and community members attended the celebration.

Among the distinguished guests present were Hon. Sarah Lugerna Lipumano-Garcia, Olongapo City Councilor; Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, College President; Ms. Agnes L. Melo, GCFAS President; and Mr. Joseph Angelo B. Pusing, GCAAI President.

They joined GCFAS and GCAAI officers, college officials, administrative staff, faculty, students, alumni, and numerous college supporters in making the event a
label: gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025/]
inistrative staff, faculty, students, alumni, and numerous college supporters in making the event a resounding success.

Jeian Espiritu emerged as the winner in the Boys Student Category, completing the race in an impressive 10 minutes and 44 seconds.

“I was motivated to run by my teammates in the Gordon College athletics program and my girlfriend,” Espiritu shared. “Winning feels great but also adds pressure for future years.”

In the women’s Student Category, Susie May Mistica Ariño took first place with a time of 14 minutes and 43 seconds.

Ariño credited her partner’s encouragement and he
label: gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025/]
t place with a time of 14 minutes and 43 seconds.

Ariño credited her partner’s encouragement and her focus on mental health for her success. “It started when my partner encouraged me to run,” Ariño explained.

“While running, I realized I wanted more than just a casual run for the sake of my mental health. I started joining his training plans until he gifted me a running watch.”

Furthermore, Nicole Bernal Marcial, an alumna of Gordon College, won the Alumni Category with a time of 14 minutes and 32 seconds.

“The FUNdation Run isn’t just about promoting fitness and health—it’s a way to celeb
label: gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025/]
nd 32 seconds.

“The FUNdation Run isn’t just about promoting fitness and health—it’s a way to celebrate and commemorate Gordon College’s 26th founding anniversary,” Marcial said.

“As an alumnus, I wanted to be part of this special occasion.”

He also emphasized the importance of community and the joy of running for his participation.

The winners also offered advice to aspiring runners.

Espiritu encouraged them to persevere even when facing challenges.

Meanwhile, Ariño stressed the importance of preparation and a winner’swinner’s mindset.

Lastly, Marcial advised runners to enjoy the proce
label: gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-26th-founding-anniversary-with-successful-fundation-run-2025/]
e of preparation and a winner’swinner’s mindset.

Lastly, Marcial advised runners to enjoy the process and find their motivation. “The hardest part of joining fun runs or any running event is finding the motivation to start. But once you understand why you’re doing it and learn to love the experience, everything else follows, ” he said.

He added, “One foot in front of the other, and always be proud of yourself for taking that step.”

The FUNdation Run 2025 served as a fitting start to Gordon College’s 26th Founding Anniversary, as it brought together the college community and promoted health

# GC Rise for Freedom in Global Campaign Against Gender-Based Violence

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [GAD](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/gad/) [IPDEA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ipdea/) [NSTP](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/nstp/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, February 13, 2025**

Gordon College joined the global movement “One Billion Rising: Rise for Freedom” on February 14, 2025 at the Olongapo City Tapinac Oval Sports Complex as the students, faculty, and
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
on February 14, 2025 at the Olongapo City Tapinac Oval Sports Complex as the students, faculty, and alumni united in dance and solidarity to raise awareness about violence against women and girls.

One Billion Rising is the most extensive mass action to end violence against women in human history which began in 2012 worldwide.

This advocacy campaign was further adapted by the Philippines on February 10, 2025.

Additionally, the campaign was based on the statistics from the United Nations that one in three women globally will experience violence in their lifetime, and with a world population o
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
ne in three women globally will experience violence in their lifetime, and with a world population of 7 billion, this translates to over one billion women and girls.

The event, organized by the Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) through the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Unit, in partnership with the National Service Training Program (NSTP) and the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc., saw enthusiastic participation from the entire college community.

Dr. Imelda OP. Soriano, OIC-College President, emphasized the institution’s commitment to ending all forms
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
lda OP. Soriano, OIC-College President, emphasized the institution’s commitment to ending all forms of violence against women and girls. “As a woman, we need to rise and rise for what is right for us,” the newly-pledged college president stated.

“In this activity, we will dance, we will rise because we want to demand justice. Together, we will break this silence through dancing because this is not just an act of resistance, but it is our expression of our unyielding spirit, our joy, and our collective power to fight injustices against women.”

Furthermore, the event featured a mass dance to t
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
lective power to fight injustices against women.”

Furthermore, the event featured a mass dance to the One Billion Rising anthem, “Break the Chain.”

Harvey Bolfango, a first-year BSED English student, highlighted the importance of student involvement. “It is important to every student of Gordon College to be part of this event because it embodies a lot of respect, dignity, transparency, and more awareness of certain causes like violence against women and other girls.”

Additionally, John Andrei Facultad, a first-year BSED English student, emphasized the importance of speaking up against viole
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
 Facultad, a first-year BSED English student, emphasized the importance of speaking up against violence. “Don’t be afraid to speak up if you have ever been attacked. Kahit pa sabihin natin na verbal ‘yan or hindi gano’n kalala. But violence is still violence, and it should be addressed.”

Sir Joseph Atencio, OIC Head of the DEI Unit, explained the partnership between NSTP, the GC Alumni, and the DEI Unit.

“Gusto natin ipakita na si Gordon College is for the protection of women’s rights, and especially ‘yung mga children, mga batang babae. Kasi ang usaping women empowerment (and) women’s right
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
ecially ‘yung mga children, mga batang babae. Kasi ang usaping women empowerment (and) women’s rights is not only for women alone. Bagkus buong society, buong sector is tumutulong, and nag-participate sa pagtaguyod and pagpapaunladng karapatan ng kababaihan,” he stated.

He also affirmed Gordon College’s ongoing commitment to diversity through the DEI unit and its pursuit of inclusive education.

As the event concluded, Dr. Darwin Paguio, Vice President of Academic Affairs, echoed the call to action, stating, “Rising is not an option. It is our duty. Let us rise for ourselves, for each other,
label: gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-rise-for-freedom-in-global-campaign-against-gender-based-violence/]
tion, stating, “Rising is not an option. It is our duty. Let us rise for ourselves, for each other, and for generations to come. Rise up, stand firm, and let our collective courage illuminate the path for a brighter tomorrow. The time is now. Let us rise, Gordon College.”

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

: Princess Alliyah Danday | News Correspondent

# Gordon College Joins National Simultaneous Earthquake Drill

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 12, 2026**

LOOK | Students and personnel of Gordon College (GC) gathered earlier this afternoon, March 12, at Tapinac Oval Track as part of the National Simultaneous Earthquake Drill (NSED).

The earthquake drill simultaneously occurred at 3:30pm today in public and private institutions across the country to strengthen community resilience against a possible disaster.

NSED is practiced every quarter — totaling four times in a year — to establish nationwide
label: gordon-college-joins-national-simultaneous-earthquake-drill

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-joins-national-simultaneous-earthquake-drill/]
isaster.

NSED is practiced every quarter — totaling four times in a year — to establish nationwide earthquake preparedness. This is in preparation for possible earthquakes in the future including the “Big One”, a projected magnitude 7.2 earthquake predicted to hit Metro Manila and affect nearby provinces as the West Valley Fault is ripe for movement. | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT

# TF’s Luto, among top newsletters at 2026 AOCPA; scribes bag 4 individual awards

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, March 7, 2026**

he Forefront, the official student publication of Gordon College, took home the 8th Most Outstanding Campus Newsletter and four individual awards at the 6th Assumption Outstanding Campus Press Awards (AOCPA).

The event, hosted by the University of the Assumption’s Regina, took place in San Fernando, Pampanga, on February 27.

The first semestral issue of The Forefront, Luto, was one of this year’s recipients of Most Outstanding
label: tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards/]
 first semestral issue of The Forefront, Luto, was one of this year’s recipients of Most Outstanding Campus Newsletter.

During the awards ceremony, the publication’s Editor-in-Chief John Patrick Mateo earned the 2nd Most Outstanding Campus Journalist award.

Opinion-Editorial Editor Stephanie Estrella’s piece, “Pundido ang ilaw sa susunod na kalsada”, made her the 4th Most Outstanding Editorial Writer and Punto Central Luzon’s Choice for Most Outstanding Editorial Writer.

Meanwhile, Head of Visual Arts Maryisobelle Arcilla was named the 10th Most Outstanding Editorial Cartoonist in another L
label: tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards/]
sual Arts Maryisobelle Arcilla was named the 10th Most Outstanding Editorial Cartoonist in another Luzon-wide stint.

The annual awards, featuring 46 publications from various regions in the north, saw The Forefront emerge as one of Luzon’s best.

# TF marks history at 21st LHEPC; 3 scribes snatch indiv awards

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, March 22, 2026**

The Forefront (TF) delegates secured three individual awards, first time in the publication’s Luzon-wide stint, at the 21st Luzon-Wide Higher Education Press Conference (LHEPC) from March 17-19, at NGN Grand Hotel in Tuguegarao City.

Representing Central Luzon, the individual category participants bagged awards in: Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English), 3rd place, from Senior Layout Artist Angelique Jose; Head of Traditional Arts M
label: tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards/]
ustration (English), 3rd place, from Senior Layout Artist Angelique Jose; Head of Traditional Arts Mary Isobelle Arcilla secured 7th Place in Traditional Comic Strip Drawing (English); and John Christian Rocero, former social media manager, took home 9th place in Photojournalism (English).

At the same time, John Patrick Mateo, Editor-in-Chief, participated in Devcomm Writing (Filipino) while Lance Isaac Leon, Associate Editor, participated in Editorial Writing (English) and Column Writing (English).

Moreover, Region III was named the Best Performing Region for the Individual and Group catego
label: tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards/]
ish).

Moreover, Region III was named the Best Performing Region for the Individual and Group categories.

Announced on the same day, LHEPC will be replaced next year by the 1st National Higher Education Press Conference (NHEPC), to be hosted by Region I at Vigan City.

Similar to LHEPC rules, the top 5 placers in each category from the respective regional presscons will advance to the NHEPC. | via Roi Chester B. Manio/ THE FOREFRONT

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, March 6, 2026**

Alumna Alexis Molon Penuliar of the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) ranked ninth in the February 2026 Philippine Nursing Licensure Examination (PNLE), as announced by the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) today, March 6.

Graduated cum laude, the former Gordon College – Nursing Student Organization (GC-NSO) president earned an 89.80% rating in the recent board examination.

The institution recorded 55 passers out of 60 tota
label: ex-nso-prexy-secures-rank-9-spot-gc-among-top-schools-in-feb-2026-pnle

n 89.80% rating in the recent board examination.

The institution recorded 55 passers out of 60 total takers, posting an overall passing rate of 91.67%. Comprising this are 55 out of 57 first-time passers while none of the three repeat examinees passed.

Moreover, GC was among the country’s top-performing schools at 4th rank in the recent licensure exam with 50 to 99 examinees and at least 85% passing percentage, far surpassing the national passing rate of 44.24%.

label: ex-nso-prexy-secures-rank-9-spot-gc-among-top-schools-in-feb-2026-pnle

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
