# SUCs still face budget cuts in proposed 2026 national budget – Kabataan

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 18, 2025**

“Sa ibang institusyon o unibersidad [man], ang matapyasan ng budget para sa sektor ng edukasyon ay isang paraan upang mawalan ng karapatan ang mga magaaral na magkaroon ng kalidad na edukasyon,” a 4th Year BACOMM Student exclaimed.

Twenty-five State Universities and Colleges (SUCs) are still facing budget cuts amounting to Php 6.88 billion after Congress rejected amendments by Kabataan Partylist in its second-to-last hearing of the
label: sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan/]
llion after Congress rejected amendments by Kabataan Partylist in its second-to-last hearing of the proposed 2026 national budget.

In a Facebook Post, youth-led Kabataan partylist condemns the House of Representative’s rejection of proposed amendments to House Bill (HB) No. 4058 or the proposed 2026 national budget that would have alleviated deficits to SUCs.

“Mariing tinututulan ng Kabataan Partylist ang pahayag ng Kongreso na sapat na raw ang dagdag-badyet sa SUCs at ang pagpondo sa 2022-2025 Free Higher Education (FHE) deficiencies. Danas ng bawat Iskolar ng Bayan ang hirap na magpatuloy
label: sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan/]
5 Free Higher Education (FHE) deficiencies. Danas ng bawat Iskolar ng Bayan ang hirap na magpatuloy sa kanilang pag-aaral dahil sa kakulangan ng pondo at suporta mula sa gobyerno,” the youth-led party-list said in a Facebook post.

Furthermore, in a report by Krixia Subingsubing for Philippine Daily Inquirer, twenty-five SUCs will be affected by budget cuts amounting to Php 6.88 billion. Three of these institutions are from Central Luzon including Aurora State College of Technology, Bulacan Agricultural State College, Central Luzon State University in Nueva Ecija and Philippine Merchant Marine
label: sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan/]
cultural State College, Central Luzon State University in Nueva Ecija and Philippine Merchant Marine Academy in Zambales.

Meanwhile, a group of school presidents and student regents from these institutions, led by Kabataan Party-list, has since issued a unity statement calling for Congress to restore budget cuts from the proposed national budget next year, citing “chronic underfunding” of the country’s education system.

According to the group, budget cuts compel SUCs to restrict admissions and compensate with “internally generated income from business ventures [and on] direct fee collection
label: sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/sucs-still-face-budget-cuts-in-proposed-2026-national-budget-kabataan/]
 compensate with “internally generated income from business ventures [and on] direct fee collection from students”.

As of October 10, the House has approved the proposed Php 6.7 trillion 2026 budget bill for 2026 following amendments. The budget is scheduled for a final reading in Congress on October 13 before congressional break. | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT

Sources: Kabataan Partylist/via FACEBOOK, Krixia Subingsubing/PHILIPPINE DAILY INQUIRER

# CEAS Rep crowned as the First-Ever Mr. Tourism Olongapo 2025

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 15, 2025**

“Lord, hindi ako magtatampo kung hindi mo ibibigay sa akin.”

This is the silent cry of Chester Pislaan, a 4th-year student from the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) of Gordon College, before he was crowned as Mr. Tourism Olongapo 2025 during the grand coronation on October 10, 2025, held at the SMX Olongapo Convention Center, SM City Olongapo Central.

Pislaan expressed his plans to use his platform to promote sustainable fashi
label: ceas-rep-crowned-as-the-first-ever-mr-tourism-olongapo-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-rep-crowned-as-the-first-ever-mr-tourism-olongapo-2025/]
City Olongapo Central.

Pislaan expressed his plans to use his platform to promote sustainable fashion and empower student workers and breadwinners as the newly crowned Mr. Tourism Olongapo.

“I will focus on informing everyone to refuse fast fashion while also sharing my story, especially with student workers,” he highlighted his advocacy for both environmental and social awareness.

The CEAS Student has a strong will to win, but admitted that things turned upside down when he saw how well the other candidates performed, saying that he’s just like the other contestants— with a heart full of h
label: ceas-rep-crowned-as-the-first-ever-mr-tourism-olongapo-2025

other candidates performed, saying that he’s just like the other contestants— with a heart full of hope and dreams.

“He [Lord] gave me these titles not just for recognition but to expand my reach,” his sentiment after being announced as the winner. He described the moment as both surreal and purposeful, as he connected his earlier achievements as Ginoong Linggo ng Kabataan – Olongapo and Mr. Gordon College Siklab 2025 to a broader mission of service and inspiration.

In addition, Candidate #6 Marc Anthony Acula from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA), earned the title of 2nd Runner

# World University Rankings 2026: GC Recognized as Reporter Institution

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 18, 2025**

Gordon College has officially been granted Reporter Status in the recent Times Higher Education (THE) World University Rankings 2026, released last October 9.

The college is among six Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) in Central Luzon that have been granted this recognition, including:  
– Bulacan State University  
– Central Luzon State University  
– Nueva Ecija University of Science and Technology  
– Tarlac Agricultural Univers
label: world-university-rankings-2026-gc-recognized-as-reporter-institution

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/world-university-rankings-2026-gc-recognized-as-reporter-institution/]
tate University  
– Nueva Ecija University of Science and Technology  
– Tarlac Agricultural University, and  
– City College of San Fernando Pampanga

The Times Higher Education annually releases their World University Rankings that measure the performance of universities in eighteen (18) performance indicators across five core pillars: teaching, research environment, research quality, international outlook, and industry. A reporter status is granted to HEIs who have submitted relevant data but did not meet eligibility criteria to receive a rank. Inclusion in the reporter status allows HEIs t
label: world-university-rankings-2026-gc-recognized-as-reporter-institution

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/world-university-rankings-2026-gc-recognized-as-reporter-institution/]
 did not meet eligibility criteria to receive a rank. Inclusion in the reporter status allows HEIs to receive feedback in their performance across the metrics to support future decision making.

Meanwhile, Ateneo de Manila University is the highest ranking university in the Philippines with a banded rank of 1001-1200, followed by the University of the Philippines (1201-1500), De la Salle University (1501+), Mapua University (1501+). Mindanao State University – Iligan Institute of Technology (1501+), and University of Santo Tomas (1501+). The University of Oxford remains to be the top global ra
label: world-university-rankings-2026-gc-recognized-as-reporter-institution

content:

1+), and University of Santo Tomas (1501+). The University of Oxford remains to be the top global ranking university for the tenth consecutive year. | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT

**Sunday, October 19, 2025**

The eagerly anticipated week-long 14th LCUAA National Games officially kicked off today at the City of San Jose del Monte (CSJDM) Sports Complex.

Cheers and applause filled the venue as the opening ceremony began with a parade featuring all 27 participating colleges, each led by their institution’s gold medalists.

Following the parade was the introduction of the LCUAA 2025 representative officers, a doxology by
label: lcuaa-2025-welcomes-rainy-sunday-afternoon

City College of San Jose del Monte (CCSJDM) College President Dr. Roger S. Ambida delivered the opening remarks, warmly welcoming delegates to the institution.

Hon. Arturo B. Robes then gave the keynote address, encouraging athletes to build a brighter and more empowered future.

A

The competitive events officially begin tomorrow, October 20, 2025.

# Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 18, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE MAIN CAMPUS — Coinciding with PAGLALAYAG 2025 (more commonly known as Apertura 2025), the newly acquired Gordon College Annex Building, located at Lower Kalaklan, Olongapo, was formally inaugurated on August 11.

In an interview with The Forefront, Olongapo City Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. “wanted to be practical” in expanding Gordon College (GC), citing several important considerations.

Paulino believe
label: paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready/]
actical” in expanding Gordon College (GC), citing several important considerations.

Paulino believes that students applied to Gordon College for quality education, a main reason to attract 9,500 students this Academic Year (A.Y).

As the A.Y. 2025-2026 begins, the local chief executive highlighted their desire to make ongoing improvements to the current building and structure, ensuring new equipment is added as needed.

“We want to make sure that everything goes accordingly. Gusto talaga naming mag-expand,” the mayor said.

The back-to-back mayor of Olongapo City also considered improving and
label: paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready/]
 mag-expand,” the mayor said.

The back-to-back mayor of Olongapo City also considered improving and adding programs that would need more equipment and facilities.

“Tinitingnan din natin ‘yong possibility na mag-expand pa tayo kasi may clamor din to add additional [programs], whether more medical courses or engineering, lahat ‘yan pinag-iisipan namin,” the mayor added.

Now, the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) has officially moved to the newly inaugurated annex building of the institution.

When asked about the institution’s readiness for university status, the Board of Trustees Cha
label: paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready/]
tion.

When asked about the institution’s readiness for university status, the Board of Trustees Chairperson gave the institution a rating of 8 to 9 out of 10, stressing that while “the qualifications [are] there and personnel [are] there”, there remains the challenge of “accumulating enough resources to qualify.”

Moreover, the chairperson spoke of the institution being already at that point as “the quality of education [that Gordon College] provides is at par with some of the universities.”

“Sa results ng [board] exams natin, we’re always competing with the best universities,” he added.

Fu
label: paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready/]
Sa results ng [board] exams natin, we’re always competing with the best universities,” he added.

Furthermore, the mayor underscored the need to instill professionalism and discipline among students through the institution, aiming to make them employable and ready to become future managers. He also nurtured and wished for the students to apply values of professionalism and intelligence to work hand-in-hand when enrolled within the institution.

“[Please] know that the city government is here to support the institution and the students. The city is always proud of Gordon College. It’s the home
label: paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

support the institution and the students. The city is always proud of Gordon College. It’s the home of many top-notchers, many successful community leaders, both in business and society. Mataas ang accreditation natin, we are internationally ranked, [and] we are very proud of the school and very proud of its students,” he concluded. | via Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**

TRICKSHOT BADMINTON COURT—Flora Amor Francisco and Vherna Lei Castañada of the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) made a powerful smash to victory in the Women’s Double category against the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) with a score of 21-10.

The Trickshot Badminton Court echoed with loud smashes and cheers as Francisco and Castañada dominated the game, leaving the CHTM Sharks with no chance at victory.
label: ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win/]
rs as Francisco and Castañada dominated the game, leaving the CHTM Sharks with no chance at victory. The first set ended decisively, 21-4.

Francisco expressed her thrill and happiness during the interview as they were the first to win gold for the team.

“We know na ‘yon ‘yung way para lumakas ‘yung loob ng ibang ka-team namin, para lumaban at manalo para maibalik sa CEAS Badminton ang overall. Also, I promised before I graduated na maibalik ang overall champ sa CEAS Badminton,” the victor shared.

Francisco also expressed the challenges they faced in synchronizing their movements during matc
label: ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win/]
ed.

Francisco also expressed the challenges they faced in synchronizing their movements during matches, emphasizing how it is one of the most important aspects of playing doubles—where victory depends on two players moving as one.

In the elimination round, the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks battled fiercely with the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards, with the Sharks taking the win.

In the semifinals, the College of Computer Studies (CCS) Phoenix faced the CEAS Mentors, while the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers battled the CHTM Shar
label: ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-womens-doubles-clear-past-chtm-sharks-for-the-win/]
ced the CEAS Mentors, while the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers battled the CHTM Sharks, setting up a championship showdown between CHTM and CEAS.

For the final standings, CAHS Tigers secured third place, CCS Phoenix finished in fourth place, and CBA Wizards settled into fifth place. | via Jeliza Tadena/The FOREFRONT

**Friday, September 19, 2025**

The College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors brought home the championship for Men’s Futsal against the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks with a score of 1-0.

Only in the first seven the battle of the two teams has become a center, thrilled to bring home the gold in their respective departments.

Everyone held their breath in the opposition of the two teams to secure the championship.

Although it wasn’t easy

# CHTM Sharks sealed the set against CEAS Mentors in Men’s Singles

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**

TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—The College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks bagged the gold again during the men’s badminton singles championship match, 21-6, defeating the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors.

With his outstanding performance, Kryztofer Matias Basa of CHTM Sharks ended the finals with two straight sets, leaving no chance for his opponent to recover.

In an interview, Basa shared that he al
label: chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles/]
raight sets, leaving no chance for his opponent to recover.

In an interview, Basa shared that he already expected the victory as he had once played with a stronger player before.

“‘Yu‘n pa yung ginamit ko na motivation para magchampion ulit yung CHTM,” he said when asked about being pressured to defend the previous win of CHTM last SIKLAB.

He also expressed his joy in playing with passion and humility. “Iniisip ko lang is maging humble, and…sportsmanship,” he added.

Moreover, the College of Computer Studies secured the bronze medal, while the College of Business and Accountancy and the Col

# Beauty Meets Sportsmanship: Pislaan and Pradas Crowned as Mr. and Ms. Sportsfest 2025

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, September 15, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE, OLONGAPO CITY— Cheers filled the East Tapinac Oval Grounds as the perfect blend of charm and athletic spirit came to life with representatives from various colleges showcasing their poise and sportsmanship, on the recently concluded Mr. and Ms. Sportsfest 2025 on Monday, September 15.

Kick-starting off the event with a bang, representatives wowed the crowd with their charisma and creative display of ath
label: beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025/]
e event with a bang, representatives wowed the crowd with their charisma and creative display of athletic-themed fashion for the Best in Sportswear award, which went to Mr. Chester Pislaan and Ms. Bhavjeet Kaur L. Singh from the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors.

But the night didn’t end there—the battle for the ultimate title pushed the candidates to bring out their best poise, personality, and confidence. As the event drew to a close, the stage erupted in cheers and anticipation as the overall winners were finally announced.

After an intense showdown, Ms. Dhana Del C.
label: beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025/]
ipation as the overall winners were finally announced.

After an intense showdown, Ms. Dhana Del C. Pradas from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards and Mr. Chester Pislaan from the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) emerged as Mr. and Ms. Sportsfest—proving that talent, wit, and stage presence shine brightest when matched with determination and sportsmanship.

Furthermore, hot on their heels, the 1st Runner-Up titles went to Mr. Marc Anthony Acula (CBA) Wizards and Ms. Bhavjeet Kaur L. Singh (CEAS) Mentors, whose outstanding performances made them strong contender
label: beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025/]
Ms. Bhavjeet Kaur L. Singh (CEAS) Mentors, whose outstanding performances made them strong contenders throughout the competition.

Meanwhile, Mr. Krizztan Vic A. Dominico and Ms. Ron Eriele Joi P. Agustin from College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers secured the 2nd Runner-Up spot, earning admiration for their dynamic partnership.

In 3rd place, Mr. Charles De Leon from College of Computer Studies (CCS) Phoenix and Ms. Alexandra Macky Alisbo from College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) claimed their rightful spot. At the same time, Mr. Jack Francis P. Meran (CHTM) Sharks and
label: beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/beauty-meets-sportsmanship-pislaan-and-pradas-crowned-as-mr-and-ms-sportsfest-2025/]
nt (CHTM) claimed their rightful spot. At the same time, Mr. Jack Francis P. Meran (CHTM) Sharks and Ms. Janna Lyn Rolls, CCS Phoenix, completed the roster as the 4th place finishers. | via Love Lorainne Venus Acedo/The FOREFRONT

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, September 19, 2025**

A story of five teams told across five days, this year’s SIKLAB 2025 championship was a tooth and nail fight for gold, silver, and bronze.

The Tigers clawed their way through the stage.

The Wizards wavered their wands high.

The Phoenix rose with flaming feathers up high

The Sharks splashed onto the podium.

Yet, only one team pulled through.

Clinching forty-four gold, twenty-six silver, and fourteen bronze medals, the Mentors of the College of Education,

# Ang Paghatak ni Apolo ng Karwahe sa Dulo ng PaSIKLABan 2025

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, September 30, 2025**

OLONGAPO CITY—Ang pagtaklob ng ulap sa nakapapasong araw ay naging tanda ng pagtatapos ng SIKLAB 2025. Sa pagkalansing ng medalya at pagkinang ng tropeyo, umalingawngaw ang sigawan at palakpakan. Kagat-labi ang mga manonood sa antisipasyon kung sino ang magkakamit ng inaasam na pagkapanalo.

Bago parangalan ang magkakampeon sa sagupaan ng mga pating, tigre, wizards, phoenix, at mentor, nagbigay ng natatanging mensahe si Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano n
label: ang-paghatak-ni-apolo-ng-karwahe-sa-dulo-ng-pasiklaban-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ang-paghatak-ni-apolo-ng-karwahe-sa-dulo-ng-pasiklaban-2025/]
ing, tigre, wizards, phoenix, at mentor, nagbigay ng natatanging mensahe si Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano na tunay na nagbigay konklusyon sa limang araw na bakbakan sa palarong pampalakasan.

Sa huli, isa lang ang tanong na namutawi: mananatili bang asul ang kulay ng kampeon o mamamayagpag ang bandila ng ibang kolehiyo? | via Kana Maye Dueñas/The FOREFRONT

# CEAS Mentors Duo Reclaims Gold, Overpowers CBA in Mixed Doubles Badminton Clash

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**

TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—In a smashing display of speed, precision, and teamwork, the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors reclaimed badminton glory, dominating the mixed doubles championship against the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards on September 16, 2025, at the Trickshot Badminton Court.

Right from the opening serve, the dynamic duo of Daniel Ruzzel Bernal and Mary Anne Lafuente wast
label: ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash/]
.

Right from the opening serve, the dynamic duo of Daniel Ruzzel Bernal and Mary Anne Lafuente wasted no time asserting their dominance with lightning-quick reflexes and razor-sharp shot placements.

Consequently, their rapid-fire rallies and clever net play kept the CBA Wizards scrambling across the court, ending the first set in convincing fashion at 21-9, with CEAS clearly in control.

Building on that first-set momentum, Bernal and Lafuente came out swinging in the second set, smashing their way to an early lead through seamless communication and smart positioning.

Meanwhile, although th
label: ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash/]
r way to an early lead through seamless communication and smart positioning.

Meanwhile, although the Wizards attempted a comeback with aggressive clears and pushes, every move was countered by the Mentors’ calculated drop shots, steady defense, and powerful smashes, closing the set at 21-1 to secure the championship crown.

After the final rally, Lafuente emphasized that the victory was not only a triumph of skill but also a testament to their trust in each other, both on and off the court.

Similarly, Bernal expressed his pride and excitement after clinching the gold, saying, “Sobrang saya p
label: ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash/]
milarly, Bernal expressed his pride and excitement after clinching the gold, saying, “Sobrang saya po and super proud po ako sa partner ko.”

Before reaching the finals, the duo battled past the CHTM Sharks in the elimination round, overcoming sharp smashes and relentless returns to secure a 30–10 win and a spot in the championship stage.

Furthermore, the CEAS pair showed that age and experience were no barriers, as their first match as college students turned into a golden opportunity to prove their fire and determination. | via Lovely Mae M. Doroin/The FOREFRONTFOREFRONT

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**

TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—In an intense showdown between the two departments, the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) clinched the men’s doubles title in three sets against the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) at the Trickshot Badminton Court yesterday, September 16, 2025.

The CBA Wizards took the first set, 15–12, but the CEAS Mentors roared back with a 21–10 win in the second. In the decider, Xyrus Russ
label: ceas-mentors-mens-double-secured-the-championship-over-the-cba-wizards-21-6

, 15–12, but the CEAS Mentors roared back with a 21–10 win in the second. In the decider, Xyrus Russell S. Santos and Cyvon Aristoteles of CEAS left no room for a comeback, sealing the match and denying the Wizards victory.

After placing first runner-up last SIKLAB, Santos shared his joy and nervousness with today’s game, “During game po, s’yempre po, nakaramdam din po kami ng kaba. Pero, sa huli po, pag nananalo po, masaya naman po,” he stated.

Meanwhile, his teammate Aristoteles celebrated his second championship this SIKLAB 2025.

label: ceas-mentors-mens-double-secured-the-championship-over-the-cba-wizards-21-6

is second championship this SIKLAB 2025.

# CCS Phoenix Clinches Gold at SIKLAB 2025 E-Games

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**

FUNCTION HALL, GORDON COLLEGE MAIN BLDG.—The Gordon College Main Building Function Hall buzzed with excitement as SIKLAB 2025 E-Games came to a spectacular close, featuring intense battles for gold, silver, and bronze.

In the Battle for Bronze, the College of Education, Arts, and Science (CEAS) Mentors defeated the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers, 22-8, securing third place and bringing pride to the CEAS community.

Meanwhile, the champi
label: ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games/]
 Tigers, 22-8, securing third place and bringing pride to the CEAS community.

Meanwhile, the championship series between the CCS Phoenix and the CHTM Sharks was a nail-biter in a best-of-three format. CCS edged CHTM in Game 1, 21-20, followed by a 17-win in Game 2. In the final showdown, the Phoenix dominated, 12-1, clinching the gold medal with an overall 2-1 victory.

The CHTM Sharks earned the silver medal, demonstrating remarkable perseverance and strategy throughout the finals.

SIKLAB 2025 E-Games concluded as a remarkable showcase of determination, strategy, and camaraderie, crowning t
label: ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games/]
5 E-Games concluded as a remarkable showcase of determination, strategy, and camaraderie, crowning the CCS Phoenix as champions while bringing the excitement of competitive E-sports to Gordon College—and leaving fans eagerly anticipating next year’s edition. | via Frances Marie Farne/The FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Celebrates World Teachers’ Day

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, October 7, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE MAIN BLDG—The Office of the Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) hosted a World Teachers’ Day Celebration, together with various student organizations, which aims to honor the dedicated instructors of the institution on October 6, 2025, at Gordon College Main Bldg—Function Hall.

The event began with an opening prayer and remarks from Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs.
label: gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day/]
 Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs.

“We cannot have a lavish lifestyle in teaching. But your dedication and passion will definitely [inaudible] through saying our students’ success— changing their lives, especially those who are underprivileged students, and you are making them a story,” said Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, showing her gratitude for the effort of Gordon College’s faculty.

Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also expressed to the faculty that the celebration isn’t just about their perseverance, but also to take care of all the hard work
label: gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day/]
that the celebration isn’t just about their perseverance, but also to take care of all the hard work in the classrooms as they invest in nurturing curiosity that will nourish them for a lifetime.

After the opening ceremony, Performing Arts Students Ensemble (PASE) Voci tributed a chorale performance that honors the educators.

To spark joy with the event, the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Student Council, Midwifery Student Organization (MSO), and the Nursing Student Organization (NSO) hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.

Furthermore, the PASE Ballo sparked warmth an
label: gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day/]
 hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.

Furthermore, the PASE Ballo sparked warmth and embrace with a performance number.

Moreover, the Supreme Student Council (SSC) and Human Resource Management Unit (HRMU) conducted an awarding ceremony to appreciate the faculty’s skills with admiration and humor.

Meanwhile, Hon. Rolen Paulino Jr., City Mayor of Olongapo, held a speech and cash raffle for the faculty.

“Salamat sa sacrifices niyo, sa mahabang pasensiya ninyo, tiyaka sa lahat ng ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stated.

An Audio-Visual Presentation (AVP) vid
label: gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day/]
g ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stated.

An Audio-Visual Presentation (AVP) video was shown to the faculty, featuring greetings and messages from the students, followed by the students who presented gifts to their beloved tutors.

Lastly, another raffle event was held with prizes for the faculty, which filled the room with joy and laughter.

The said event was supposed to be celebrated on October 3rd but was rescheduled due to Typhoon Paolo.| via Jasmine Zeah Llagas/The FOREFRONT

# CEAS Women’s Historic Smash Hit: Another Gold in Futsal Match

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, September 19, 2025**

What a thrilling clash between pink and blue.

The College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors defeated College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks after a long showdown that ended in 3-0, and finished as champions in the Women’s Futsal match on the last day of SIKLAB 2025, held at New Asinan covered court.

The Mentors did not let their guard down ever since their first game, making sure everything is coordinated to
label: ceas-womens-historic-smash-hit-another-gold-in-futsal-match

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-womens-historic-smash-hit-another-gold-in-futsal-match/]
s did not let their guard down ever since their first game, making sure everything is coordinated to one another until the final match.

Cheers roared through the court as the intensifying moments of the game went on, with Mentors maintaining their momentum throughout the match.

Despite the Sharks’ efforts to lessen the pressure, CEAS women clocked the game with a winning score of 3-0. | via Mariah Llave/The FOREFRONT

# PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, August 29, 2025**

Gordon College warmly welcomed the Public Employment Service Office – Tarlac Center for Learning and Skills Success (PESO-TCLASS) under the Provincial Government of Tarlac last August 26, 2025, for a benchmarking visit.

As part of their preparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC), the PESO-TCLASS delegation, headed by Dr. Maria Lira Reyes –
label: peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation/]
tificate of Program Compliance (COPC), the PESO-TCLASS delegation, headed by Dr. Maria Lira Reyes – PESO Manager and School Administrator, engaged with Gordon College to learn from our best practices in curriculum development, faculty qualifications, library and physical facilities, equipment, support services, and quality assurance systems. The visit provided them with valuable insights and guidance on meeting the minimum standards required for program compliance.

This knowledge-sharing initiative reflects Gordon College’s continued commitment to collaboration and capacity-building among aca

# CHTM Sharks Roar at 2nd CHTM Rally 2025

[CHTM](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/chtm/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, September 11, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE — The College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) filled the East Tapinac Covered Court with cheers and chants as the 2nd CHTM Rally unfolded last September 9, 2025, igniting the spirit of the CHTM Sharks in preparation for the much-anticipated Siklab 2025 Sportsfest on September 15–19, 2025.

The event showcased the final lineup of the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) o
label: chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025/]


The event showcased the final lineup of the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) official teams and players.

Furthermore, athletes from the CHTM Sharks introduced themselves through lively intermission numbers performed by each sports team, adding excitement and entertainment to the rally.

Player representatives from each team also participated in a brief interview with the Masters of Ceremony, where they shared their thoughts and enthusiasm for the upcoming Siklab competition.

Moreover, the event was organized in collaboration with the College of Hospitality and Tourism M
label: chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025/]
n.

Moreover, the event was organized in collaboration with the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management Student Council (CHTM-SC), Arapaap Productions, the Junior Association of Hospitality Management Students (JAHMS), and the League of Tourism Students of the Philippines (LTSP), who worked together to ensure the program’s success.

The College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Dean, Dr. Mark Alvin Lazaro, Assistant Dean Vincent Dela Cruz, Bachelor of Science and Tourism Management (BSTM) Program Coordinator Ma’am Lizette Ann P. Sia, Bachelor of Science in Hospitality Manageme
label: chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-roar-at-2nd-chtm-rally-2025/]
ent (BSTM) Program Coordinator Ma’am Lizette Ann P. Sia, Bachelor of Science in Hospitality Management (BSHM) Coordinator Vince Richard Gomez, and ARAPAAP Adviser Ms. Jenny Mae M. Lagman also extended strong support and leadership, which greatly guided the preparations for the rally.

To cap off the event, the atmosphere reached its peak as the CHTM Sharks filled the covered court with their department’s official Siklab cheer, embodying their spirit of unity and readiness for the upcoming sportsfest.| via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT

# PENCIL PASSED: TF Ushers in New Leaders, Journos

[ForeFront](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/forefront/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, September 13, 2025**

In a time-honored tradition of the passing of the pencil, the outgoing editorial board of The Forefront for academic year 2024-2025 has turned over their duties to the new set of editorial board for the incoming academic year in a turnover ceremony and culminating activity held last Thursday, September 11, 2025, at the Physical Education Hall, Gordon College – Main Campus.

After 53 days of deli
label: pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos/]
ember 11, 2025, at the Physical Education Hall, Gordon College – Main Campus.

After 53 days of deliberation, fourth-year Bachelor of Arts in Communication (BACOMM) student and outgoing managing editor (Man-Ed) Mx. John Patrick Mateo was hailed as the new Editor-in-Chief (EIC) and OIC news editor of The Forefront for Academic Year 2025-2026, replacing outgoing editor-in-chief and BACOMM alumna, Ms. Bealyn Demanalata.

“Out of all the speeches I have delivered, this one took three years in the making—three years of service to the community, to the people, and most especially, to the marginalize
label: pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos/]
king—three years of service to the community, to the people, and most especially, to the marginalized and oppressed,” Ms. Demanalata said in her step-down speech.

The outgoing EIC further emphasized the role of campus journalists as torchbearers of the truth. “As you carry this legacy forward, may you remember that every article written, every truth defended, and every voice amplified is part of a greater fight—a fight not just for recognition, but for democracy, justice, and the people. This is the mark we leave behind: that we dared, that we endured, and that we surpassed the limits.”

Form
label: pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos/]
s the mark we leave behind: that we dared, that we endured, and that we surpassed the limits.”

Formally assuming their new role, Mx. Mateo stressed that, despite their imperfection, they will remain to be an empathetic and transformational leader for the publication. “Bilang huling pagkakataon na maging student journalist, wala akong planong perpekto. Ngunit, pangako, hindi ako magiging makasarili’t hahatiin ko ang aking puso para lamang maiabot ang pagmamahal sa bawat isa sa inyo,” the new EIC said.

Together with publication alumni Mr. John Carlo Bermudez (outgoing Associate Editor), Mr. I-
label: pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos/]
 said.

Together with publication alumni Mr. John Carlo Bermudez (outgoing Associate Editor), Mr. I-man Anthonie Klay Garcia (outgoing Circulations Manager), Mr. Emar Kristian Aranda (outgoing OIC Opinion-Editorial Editor), Ms. Patrisha Marie Pineda (outgoing DevComm Editor), and Ms. Jessica Dimatulac (outgoing Head Layout Artist), their duties and responsibilities were officially turned over to the new editorial board, who each gave their respective turnover speeches.

Moreover, forming Mx. Mateo’s executive committee are: Mx. Lance Isaac Leon, outgoing head of visual arts, as associate edito
label: pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pencil-passed-tf-ushers-in-new-leaders-journos/]
eo’s executive committee are: Mx. Lance Isaac Leon, outgoing head of visual arts, as associate editor; Ms. Mikhaela Sagun as managing editor who remains as editor of the literary and culture section, and; Mr. Rishen David Belza as circulations manager and head broadcaster.

Meanwhile, the editorial board was appointed based on the results of the editorial board exam last July 7, 2025 as well as performance during the previous academic year. Seated on the board are:

– News Editor: Mx. John Patrick Mateo

– Opinion and Editorial Editor: Ms. Stephanie Estrella

– Feature Editor: Ms. Shermel Carl

# Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan, pinasinayaan sa Gordon College

[CEAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ceas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, September 7, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE—Inukit na ang mga bagong lakan at lakambini ng kasaysayan kasabay ng panapos na selebrasyon ng Buwan ng Kasaysayan 2025 na may temang “Diwa ng Kasaysayan, Kabilin sa Kabataan” na idinaos noong Agosto 30, sa Gordon College Physical Education (PE) Hall sa pangunguna ng Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).

Nagwagi sina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales mula sa BSE
label: bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college/]
an ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).

Nagwagi sina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales mula sa BSEd Soc 3-A at Bb. Angel Mae Deseo mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A para sa Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan 2025.

Nasungkit naman nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A ang korona bilang Ginoong Unang Gantimpala at Binibining Unang Gantimpala.

Namayagpag din bilang Ginoong Ikalawang Gantimpala at Binibining Ikalawang Gantimpala sina G. Rafael Ellado mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A at Bb. Gervy Sarmiento mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A.

Bukod sa Lakan at Lakambini 20
label: bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college/]
do mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A at Bb. Gervy Sarmiento mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A.

Bukod sa Lakan at Lakambini 2025, iginawad din ang mga espesyal na parangal tulad ng Lunting Diwa at Best in Talent na parehong nakuha nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho.

Nakamit naman nina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho ang mga titulong Best in Formal Attire at People’s Choice Award.

Samantala, inuwi naman nina Bb. Angel Mae Deseo at G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales ang karangalang Binibini at Ginoo ng Nakaraan.

“Nung sinabi na ako yung panalo, naging proud ako kasi nagbunga yung su
label: bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college/]
ibini at Ginoo ng Nakaraan.

“Nung sinabi na ako yung panalo, naging proud ako kasi nagbunga yung suporta ng pamilya at mga kaibigan, higit sa lahat, nagbunga yung effort at tiwala ko sa sarili” saad ng Lakan 2025.

“In-expect ko talaga na may chance akong manalo.” Ibinahagi din ni Lorenzen na pinaghirapan niya ang kanyang preparasyon, mula ensayo hanggang pagpapakilala at pagsagot sa tanong ng mga hurado.

Samantala, labis naman ang pasasalamat ni Bb. Angel Mae Deseo, Lakambini 2025, sa lahat ng tumulong sa kanya lalo na at noong panahong iyon ay masama pa ang kanyang karamdaman.

“Tinrangkas
label: bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college/]
g tumulong sa kanya lalo na at noong panahong iyon ay masama pa ang kanyang karamdaman.

“Tinrangkaso po ako hanggang sa mismong laban, sa kabila nun ay ipinagpatuloy ko ang laban sapagkat nakikita ko ang aking mga kamag-aral (SOC 1-A) na nagtutulong-tulong upang matapos ang lahat ng aming kakailanganin,” saad ni Angel Mae Deseo.

“Lahat ng pagod at puyat ay naging makabuluhan sapagkat nasungkit namin ang korona at karangalan,” aniya. | via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT

# NAME PROTECTED: Agaton and Cerilles Among the Philippines’ Most Outstanding

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, August 27, 2025**

Two students from Aduana – Gordon College (GC) Chapter were recognized as the Most Outstanding BSCA Students of the Philippines for 2025 at an awards ceremony held by the Philippine Society of Customs Administration Students (PSCAS) Nationals on August 16 at Wind Residences, Tagaytay City.

Kris Rean Agaton was named the Top 1 Most Outstanding BSCA Student, while Renmmelle Kyle Cerilles secured the Top 3 position.

AGATON’S CUSTO
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
 Outstanding BSCA Student, while Renmmelle Kyle Cerilles secured the Top 3 position.

AGATON’S CUSTOMized JOURNEY

For Agaton, receiving the top honor was a moment of profound shock and validation.

She acknowledged experiencing personal doubt at the moment when she was nominated and considered her lack of extracurricular activities as a drawback compared to her fellow nominees across the country.

“I initially thought my chances of making it into the Top 10 were slim. That’s why, when I received the message that I ranked number one, I was overwhelmed with happiness and disbelief,” she added.
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
d the message that I ranked number one, I was overwhelmed with happiness and disbelief,” she added.

Furthermore, Agaton shared that her mother has always been the biggest source of motivation, as she’s been pushing her to move outside her comfort zone.

She revealed that, in her second year, she consistently declined opportunities for national competitions due to a “fear of failure.”

“Why not give it a try? It’s your last chance. I know you don’t regret turning down the chances before, but at least take this one as something you can look back on in the future,” her mother assured.

This enco
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
least take this one as something you can look back on in the future,” her mother assured.

This encouragement helped her overcome her fear of failure.

CE-REALEST AND DEDICATION

On the other hand, Cerilles’s journey to the Top 3 position was a story of significant personal transformation. He admitted that he was far from what many would call ‘outstanding’ and did not always take academics as seriously as he should have.

“Honestly, if you look back at past years, I was far from what many would call ‘outstanding.’ I used to be the type of student who would rather stand outside the classroom,”
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
call ‘outstanding.’ I used to be the type of student who would rather stand outside the classroom,” Cerilles shared.

It was deliberate of him to give his best in whatever he did, and this eventually resulted in a leadership position at PSCAS-GC that took him to positions he never expected.

“This award is not just a recognition of what I achieved, but a reflection of how far I’ve come. From someone unsure of their place to someone willing to go beyond their borders,” he reflected.

Similarly, Cerilles attributed his success to the lessons and sacrifices of his parents.

He sees them as his “g
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
erilles attributed his success to the lessons and sacrifices of his parents.

He sees them as his “greatest mentors,” with his mother’s resilience and his father’s resourcefulness shaping his character and approach to challenges.

“They reminded me that success is never about individual effort alone, it is built upon the values, discipline, and character that we learn from those who raised us,” he emphasized.

When the two were asked what they felt was the most valuable piece of advice to give to other aspiring students of the Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration, both Agaton and Ceri
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
o other aspiring students of the Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration, both Agaton and Cerilles returned with bits of wisdom based primarily on their own experiences.

The advice given by Agaton focused on being aware of oneself and being focused.

The cluster I valedictorian urged students to remember their “why”—the reason they started—and to make that their anchor.

“Your journey is your own. Stay focused, be patient with yourself, and trust that your efforts—no matter how big or small—are leading you somewhere meaningful,” she reminded.

Meanwhile, Cerilles’ message was a call to
label: name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/name-protected-agaton-and-cerilles-among-the-philippines-most-outstanding/]
ll—are leading you somewhere meaningful,” she reminded.

Meanwhile, Cerilles’ message was a call to “move beyond borders.”

Using the metaphor of an “aduana” (customs), he encouraged students to be brave enough to step out of their comfort zones.

Whether it is joining a student organization or taking academics more seriously, it brings one closer to discovering one’s true potential, he advised the students that growth only happens when you dare to cross those borders. | via Princess Alliyah Danday/The FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Faculty Join Basic FSL Training to Promote Inclusive Education

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, August 29, 2025**

Faculty members from the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) with Mr. Joseph Atencio-OIC, Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Unit proudly took part in a three-day Basic Filipino Sign Language (FSL) Training held last August 26–28, 2025 at the SMX Convention Center, Olongapo City. The event was organized by the Olongapo City Persons with Disability
label: gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education/]
vention Center, Olongapo City. The event was organized by the Olongapo City Persons with Disability Affairs Office (PDAO) in collaboration with the Department of Education – Olongapo City Division Office.

Throughout the training, participants engaged in interactive and hands-on activities designed to equip educators with the skills to communicate effectively with the Deaf—nurturing a more inclusive, and compassionate learning environment.

This initiative not only strengthened our instructors’ professional competencies but also reaffirmed their dedication to accessibility, diversity, and incl
label: gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education/]
professional competencies but also reaffirmed their dedication to accessibility, diversity, and inclusivity in education—a true reflection of Gordon College’s commitment to ensuring that no learner is left behind.

**Sunday, August 17, 2025**

Gordon College is proud to announce that the Philippine Society of Customs Administration Students (PSCAS) Gordon College Chapter soared to new heights at the PSCAS National Awards on August 16, 2025, at Wind Residences, Tagaytay City!

PSCAS GC Chapter was honored with the most prestigious award: Top Performing PSCAS Chapter!

This incredible achievement is a testament to our dedication to excellence and unwavering commitment to our community.

# Galaxies of Unity: CCS Students Explore New Worlds Together

[CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [ForeFront](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/forefront/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, September 14, 2025**

ASINAN COVERED COURT, OLONGAPO CITY—The College of Computer Studies (CCS) of Gordon College launched its academic year with a universe of energy at the annual General Orientation and Acquaintance Party, “Galaxies of Unity: Exploring New Worlds Together,” held on September 13, 2025, at the Asinan Covered Court.

The event aims to transform
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
ds Together,” held on September 13, 2025, at the Asinan Covered Court.

The event aims to transform into a constellation of colors, performances, advocacies, and camaraderie, welcoming both new and returning students into a community built on diversity, inclusion, and shared growth.

Furthermore, carrying the banner “Innovate, Include, Inspire: Pioneering Equality and Diversity in the AI-Driven World,” the orientation extended beyond the usual acquaintance party.

The event, spearheaded by Dean Ronnie Luy and Assistant Dean Armilyn Martinez with the support of the CCS faculty and coordinators,
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
Ronnie Luy and Assistant Dean Armilyn Martinez with the support of the CCS faculty and coordinators, united students from the three CCS undergraduate programs—Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (BSCS), Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT), and Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Multimedia Computing (BSEMC)—in celebrating inclusivity, teamwork, and talent.

“This event is not just for acquaintances, but also for orientation and presentation of our organizations and sportsfest participants. We want freshmen to feel welcome and informed, and for everyone to know the CCS
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
tsfest participants. We want freshmen to feel welcome and informed, and for everyone to know the CCS community they belong to,” shared Ms. Yheng Miel Sanchez, CCS faculty.

At the heart of the program was the introduction of eight planetary teams, each representing a social cause: Lumora for Accessibility and Compassion (Persons with Disabilities), Veltraxis for Collaboration (Ethnicity and Race), Aurelia for Empathy (Senior Citizens), Cryonex for Innovation (AI and Robotics), Floraxis for Sustainability and Culture (Cultural Heritage), Kahelion for Resilience and Advocacy (Frontline Workers),
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
inability and Culture (Cultural Heritage), Kahelion for Resilience and Advocacy (Frontline Workers), Nyxara for Identity (LGBTQIA+), and Zenthara for Equity (Women Empowerment and Gender Equity).

Moreover, the day opened with an invocation, welcome remarks, and the general assembly before teams were formally revealed. Excitement built as each segment unfolded: cosplay presentations highlighted student creativity and confidence; production numbers filled the venue with music and dance; interactive games such as Constellation Connection, Bring Me, Inclusion Mosaic, One Word Explains All, Voices
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
ve games such as Constellation Connection, Bring Me, Inclusion Mosaic, One Word Explains All, Voices of the Galaxy, and The Matrix sparked laughter and teamwork; entrepreneurial booths offered handmade crochets, stickers, badges, and keychains; Echoes of Unity, the team chant presentations, raised school spirit; and raffle draws added surprise and fun throughout the day.

“Okay lang naman yung event, nakakapagod pero enjoyable. Pinakagusto ko yung cosplay, because makikita mo yung confidence and effort ng mga sumali,” said Jerry Andrew Famanila, CCS Student.

The cheers peaked with the introdu
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
 effort ng mga sumali,” said Jerry Andrew Famanila, CCS Student.

The cheers peaked with the introduction of players for CCS Sportsfest 2025, closing with the fiery chant, “Go CCS Phoenix!”. Student organizations, including the CCS Student Council, Society of Programming Enthusiasts in Computer Science (SPECS), Empowered League of Information Technology Education Students (ELITES), and Innovative Multimedia Artists Guild of Empowered Students (IMAGES), ensured the program’s smooth flow by managing venue setup, technical aspects, and coordination.

Between lively intermissions, the spotlight tu
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
ng venue setup, technical aspects, and coordination.

Between lively intermissions, the spotlight turned to academic achievements. The Dean’s Lister Awarding Ceremony honored the Top 10 achievers per program (BSCS, BSIT, BSEMC–Digital Animation, BSEMC–Game Development) and the Top 50 overall Dean’s Listers for A.Y. 2024–2025. Special recognitions, including Top Cosplayers, Best Production Number, and Winning Team Planet, added a celebratory flair, affirming that CCS nurtures both artistry and innovation.

In addition, John Derick De Leon, BSIT Student, shared that everything went according to
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
novation.

In addition, John Derick De Leon, BSIT Student, shared that everything went according to the plan—which makes it very satisfying.

“Medyo mainit at may mga segment na biglaan pero doon lumalabas yung creativity ng students. Kitang-kita rin yung teamwork. Favorite ko yung part na bigla kaming pinag-drawing sa Inclusion Mosaic,” Mateo B. Bandilio, BSEMC student, shared.

Moreover, while the day brimmed with laughter, its deeper purpose was clear: to introduce freshmen to the CCS culture, strengthen bonds among students, and celebrate diversity as a source of strength. By merging advoc
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
, strengthen bonds among students, and celebrate diversity as a source of strength. By merging advocacies with artistry and recognition with entertainment, the event became more than a kick-off celebration. It reaffirmed CCS’s mission to innovate, include, and inspire while promoting equality across differences.

As acknowledgments and final remarks drew the day to a close, ‘Galaxies of Unity’ stood as more than an acquaintance party. Guided by their advocacies, CCS students are set to carry the spirit of unity into the rest of the academic year, a reminder that while they may belong to differ
label: galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together/]
 spirit of unity into the rest of the academic year, a reminder that while they may belong to different ‘planets’ of identity, talent, and cause, together they can form a universe of their own | Featured by Charyll Mae Cabungcal/The FOREFRONT

# Hindi pula’t dilaw ang tunay na magkalaban

**Sunday, September 7, 2025**

Gordon College, OLONGAPO CITY—Ibinandera nina Lourd Cedric Alejo ng College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) at Pauline Batinga ng College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) ang kani-kanilang kolehiyo matapos k
label: hindi-pulat-dilaw-ang-tunay-na-magkalaban

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/hindi-pulat-dilaw-ang-tunay-na-magkalaban/]
Batinga ng College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) ang kani-kanilang kolehiyo matapos koronahan bilang bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Buwan ng Wika 2025 noong ika-4 ng Sityembre.

Bukod sa prestihiyosong korona, itinanghal ding Lakan ng Larawan 2025 si Alejo habang nasungkit naman ni Rowena Ablian mula sa College of Allied Health Studies ang titulo ng Lakambini ng Larawan 2025.

Kinilala namang Lakan at Lakambini ng Pormal na Kasuotan sina Matthew Navarro (CHTM) at Shanelle Ferrer ng College of Computer Studies (CCS).

Batay sa kanilang ranggo, hinirang ang mga sumusunod:

Lakan at Lakambini ng SamFil (4th Runner-up)

Clark Justin Pambid (CCS) at Luisa May Manlapat (CBA)

# CHTM Sharks, sinaliwan ang ritmo tungong panalo

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, September 15, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE, OLONGAPO CITY—Nagliyab sa entablado ang College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks sa Dancesports Competition—Latin Category nang ipamalas ang kanilang husay sa Cha-cha na sumubok sa indayog ng mga ritmo, Rumba na nagbigay-diin sa senswalidad, at Jive na nagpaigting ng sigla at enerhiya ng SIKLAB 2025.

Matapos ang tatlong antas ng laban, nangibabaw sina John Michael Apan at Cathlyn Heart Del Rosario ng CHTM Sharks. Ipinamal
label: chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo/]
as ng laban, nangibabaw sina John Michael Apan at Cathlyn Heart Del Rosario ng CHTM Sharks. Ipinamalas nila ang matinding dedikasyon, tiyaga, at koordinasyon sa bawat hakbang at bawat himig ng musika. Tinanghal silang kampeon ng gabi sa kategoryang Latin.

Sinundan naman ito ng College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors na sumungkit sa ikalawang pwesto at College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards sa ikatlong pwesto.

“Nagpapasalamat po ako sa Diyos sa guidance, also sa lakas. Unexpected po yung nangyari, aminado naman po kami na lahat ng department malalakas, also dahil ng
label: chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo/]
. Unexpected po yung nangyari, aminado naman po kami na lahat ng department malalakas, also dahil nga po last year hindi namin nakuha ‘yong gold. Mas pinag-igihan na lang po namin yung training namin. Sobrang saya po namin na champion po kami,” ani ni John Michael, sa naramdamang galak matapos magwagi.

Dagdag pa ni Cathlyn, natural lang sa mag-partner ang mag-away. Hindi man daw nagkakasundo sa gustong steps; mga routines, mga gustong minsa’y hindi nauunawan. Ngunit, nagagawan naman ng paraan at naaayos sa usapan.

Hindi lamang sa tropeyo at ranggo umikot ang laban. Sa huli, ipinakita ng Lati
label: chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sinaliwan-ang-ritmo-tungong-panalo/]
t naaayos sa usapan.

Hindi lamang sa tropeyo at ranggo umikot ang laban. Sa huli, ipinakita ng Latin Category ang sigasig at diwa ng komunidad ng Gordon College—kung saan ang bawat indak ay larawan ng determinasyon, pagkakaisa, at pagmamahal sa sining ng sayaw ng Gordon College Community. | via Lianne Malazarte/The FOREFRONT

**Friday, August 29, 2025**

On August 27, 2025, Gordon College had the privilege of hosting Colegio de Masinloc for a benchmarking visit as they gear up for their application for Institutional Recognition, a crucial step toward qualifying for the Free Higher Education program under the Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act (Republic Act 10931).

Their delegation, headed by Dr. Feliciano M. Rosete Jr. – College President, explored G

Gordon College is honored to share its experiences and extend its support to partner institutions in advancing the quality and accessibility of higher education.

# CEAS Mentors, ibinida ang ginto sa Standard Category

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, September 15, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE, OLONGAPO CITY – Umangat ang kahusayan ng College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors sa taunang Dance Sports Competition Standard Category, matapos matagumpay na hinataw ang dalawang katunggali sa entablado sa pamamagitan ng tatlong klasikong sayaw.

Sinubukan ang kanilang husay at indak sa Waltz na nagpakita ng pino at walang-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa matinding ekspresyon, at Quickstep na sumubok sa bilis, koo
label: ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category/]
-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa matinding ekspresyon, at Quickstep na sumubok sa bilis, koordinasyon, at tibay ng bawat pares.

Sa kabila ng dikit na laban, naging kapansin-pansin ang stage presence, chemistry, kontrolado ngunit makapangyarihang galaw ni Jieann Cahanding at Ivan Russel Bahala mula sa CEAS Mentors na nagpakita ng malinis na footwork hanggang sa makabagbag na emosyon sa entablado. Sinundan ito ng CHTM (College of Hospitality and Tourism Management) Sharks sa ikalawang pwesto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health Studies) Tigers sa ikatlong pwesto.

“Aside from being stu
label: ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category/]
sto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health Studies) Tigers sa ikatlong pwesto.

“Aside from being student athletes, of course. Kami kasi ng partner ko first time partner, yung adjustment doon na tinuturuan ko siya kasi hindi rin siya talaga dance sport athlete, pero yung nakakamangha sa kaniya ay madali siya turuan, saad ni Jieann ukol sa hinarap nilang problema bago tumungtong sa entablado.

“Yung struggle namin ay ‘yong time management medyo mahirap kasi tuwing hapon lang kami nakakapag practice,” dagdag pa niya.

“Sobrang saya po na nakakaiyak. As a first-timer malaking challenge sa akin yun
label: ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category/]
e,” dagdag pa niya.

“Sobrang saya po na nakakaiyak. As a first-timer malaking challenge sa akin yun at malaking achievement,” ayon kay Bahala patungkol sa naramdaman matapos sungkitin ang kampeyonato.

Sa pagtatapos ng patimpalak, hindi lamang talento sa pagsasayaw ang naipakita ng mga kalahok. Bawat kolehiyo ay nagbigay ng kani-kaniyang tatak—mula sa makukulay na costume, malinis na footwork, hanggang sa mga koreograpiyang may hatid na emosyon at kwentong Gcians. | via Lianne Malazarte/The FOREFRONT

# GC presents one thousand students in graduation rites

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, July 10, 2025**

Gordon College’s (GC) 23rd Commencement Exercises saw a total of 1,016 passionate graduates walk in the aisle of Subic Bay Exhibition and Convention Center (SBECC) this year, donning their respective togas and sablays.

Amidst heavy rains, Cluster I filled the morning ceremony with 487 graduates from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM).

Meanwhil
label: gc-presents-one-thousand-students-in-graduation-rites

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-presents-one-thousand-students-in-graduation-rites/]
e College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM).

Meanwhile, 529 students from Cluster II’s College of Allied Health Sciences (CAHS), College of Computer Studies (CCS), and College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) graced the stage during the afternoon rites.

The graduates are products of Free Higher Education (FHE) from the institution and taxpayers, who enjoyed it during their tenure.

# Gordon College GAD Focal Point System Conducts Benchmarking Visit at PLM

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, August 11, 2025**

Representatives from various departments of Gordon College, serving as members of the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System, successfully concluded a benchmarking visit to Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Maynila (PLM) last August 08, 2025. The activity aimed to strengthen the College’s GAD programs by learning from PLM’s best practices in promoting gender equality, inclusivity, and institutional development.

The delegation was warm
label: gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm/]
s in promoting gender equality, inclusivity, and institutional development.

The delegation was warmly welcomed by PLM officials and staff. The visit featured comprehensive presentations on PLM’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) framework, gender-responsive policies, and student-centered initiatives. Delegates also toured PLM’s academic and administrative facilities, including specialized units dedicated to personnel and student support and welfare.

An open forum provided an opportunity for Gordon College representatives from different units to engage with PLM counterparts, exchanging s
label: gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm/]
or Gordon College representatives from different units to engage with PLM counterparts, exchanging strategies on implementing GAD-related projects, integrating gender perspectives in policies.

This activity reinforces Gordon College’s commitment to fostering a gender-responsive, inclusive, and equitable academic environment through continuous learning and partnership with other higher education institutions.

# 𝐏𝐚𝐠𝐥𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐩𝐚𝐭 𝐧𝐠 𝐤𝐚𝐩𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐡𝐚𝐧 𝐬𝐚 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐨, 𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐥 𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐧𝐚𝐢𝐬𝐚𝐤𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐧

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, July 18, 2025**

GORDON COLLEGE — Isinalin na ang mga posisyon at kapangyarihan sa mga bagong opisyales, gayundin ang pagpapakilala sa mga bagong kakatawan sa mga GCians sa katatapos na Supreme Student Council Turnover Ceremony na ginanap sa PE Hall nitong ika-14 ng Hulyo.

Nasaksihan din ng mga lider-estudyante mula sa iba’t ibang organisasyon sa loob ng institusyon ang nabanggit na seremonya.

Bilang panimula, binigyang diin ng noo’y SSC Chairperson F
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a/]
 institusyon ang nabanggit na seremonya.

Bilang panimula, binigyang diin ng noo’y SSC Chairperson Frances Daphne Mobo ang seremonya sa paglalahad ng Budget at Accomplishment Reports ng kanyang termino.

Samantala, inamin ni Mobo na nabigo nilang tuparin ang ilang mga pangakong proyekto para sa mga mag-aaral.

“Failures make us better individuals. Leadership teaches you how to be accountable [for] your own actions and how to accept constructive criticism. To our student leaders present here today, do remember that leadership is not about positions or titles because those are temporary. Leaders
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a/]
y, do remember that leadership is not about positions or titles because those are temporary. Leadership is about passion and dedication,” aniya.

Bukod dito, binigyan ng pagkakataon ang bagong termino upang talakayin at bigyang linaw ang kanilang mga inihandang proyekto at aktibidades sa harap ng kanilang mga kapwa lider-estudyante.

Sumatutal, mayroong 12 na proyekto at 17 na aktibidades na gustong ilunsad ang naturang termino sa pangunguna ni ngayo’y SSC Chairperson Julier Samonte. Ilan dito ay ang GCLAMP 2.0: Enhancing Access in the GCLAMP, CODE 200: Tech Industry Partnership Found, BIN-nov
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a/]
y ang GCLAMP 2.0: Enhancing Access in the GCLAMP, CODE 200: Tech Industry Partnership Found, BIN-novation: Classroom for a Cleaner Future para sa kanilang proyekto, samantala ay inahain naman ang Student First Starter Packs: College Life Unboxed, Think Green: Green Revolutions for GCians, Beyond the Myths: Understanding HIV/AIDS & Prevention para sa kanilang mga aktibidades.

Ngunit ang lahat ng kanilang programa ay pawang mga panukala pa lamang at posibleng mayroong ibang hindi mapayagang mailunsad, paglilinaw ng bagong konseho.

Bilang karagdagan sa mga pangako ni Samonte, bagong kinatawan n
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a

nsad, paglilinaw ng bagong konseho.

Bilang karagdagan sa mga pangako ni Samonte, bagong kinatawan ng konseho, nais niya at ng kaniyang konseho na palawakin ang komunikasyon at transparency sa pagsasagawa ng regular student consultations, open forums, at online suggestion platforms para marinig ang tunay na saloobin ng mga estudyante. Kasama rin dito ang paglabas ng general plan of actions, budget breakdowns, at accomplishment reports upang mas mapanatili ang tiwala ng bawat estudyante sa konseho.

“Pangatlo, mas makabuluhang events para sa lahat. Gagawin nating inclusive at meaningful ang baw
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad-%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a4%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%9a

o.

“Pangatlo, mas makabuluhang events para sa lahat. Gagawin nating inclusive at meaningful ang bawat SSC-led event. Hindi lang ito basta kasiyahan dahil gagawa rin po tayo ng mga aktibidad na magtataguyod ng leadership, advocacy, at student development.”

“Pang apat, pagpapalakas ng bawat student organization sa bawat departamento. Bilang inyong chairperson, sisiguraduhin kong makakaramdam ng buong suporta ang bawat departamental organization,” dagdag pa niya.

Paalala naman ng bagong SSC Chairperson na ang kaniyang pagkapanalo noong nakaraang eleksyon ay hindi lamang tagumpay ng isang tao k
