# 𝐈𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐍𝐨𝐰 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐚𝐦𝐩𝐮𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐒𝐎𝐆𝐈𝐄-𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐏𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, August 5, 2025**

The Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) released an advisory on July 31, 2025, regarding the implementation of a new uniform policy that formally recognizes the right of students to wear components of the existing prescribed uniform that align with their Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics (SOGIESC).

This initiative aims to provide a space for members of the LGBTQIA+ community to
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2/]
istics (SOGIESC).

This initiative aims to provide a space for members of the LGBTQIA+ community to express their authentic selves freely within the campus.

According to the advisory, the implementation of the proper wearing of uniforms, including this new provision, will start on September 8, 2025, for freshmen and transferees.

Meanwhile, for continuing students, the change will take effect on August 18, 2025. The wearing of the prescribed uniform will remain compulsory for all students from Monday to Thursday.

“In recognition of the spectrum of gender identities within our student body, t
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2/]
Monday to Thursday.

“In recognition of the spectrum of gender identities within our student body, the institution affirms the right of individuals to express their authentic selves through their attire,” the institution affirmed in its official statement.

Furthermore, on “wash days” (Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Mid-Year Term), students have the right to choose their attire, as long as it adheres to the College’s prescribed school attire and maintains decency.

This means the students are now allowed to choose from the established guidelines for male and female clothing, specifically, the p
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2/]
 allowed to choose from the established guidelines for male and female clothing, specifically, the pants or skirts that are already part of the school’s approved uniform, to express their gender identity best, provided the chosen attire still adheres to the College’s overall dress code and decency standards.

The said office encourages the entire Gordon College community to ensure the implementation of this new policy.

**Friday, July 18, 2025**

Gordon College welcomed delegates from Norzagaray College (NC) for a benchmarking visit focused on best practices in student-developed systems, Computer Studies and Tourism and Hospitality program deliveries, and institutional innovations.

# 𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨 𝐚𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐤𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨, 𝐛𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐨

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, July 18, 2025**

Pinaglaanan ng panibagong pananaw ang mga kasalukuyang student leader ng Dalubhasaan Gordon patungkol sa paksa ng pamumuno bilang lider sa katatapos na Capacity Building and Personality Development Program for Student Organizations 2025 na ginanap sa Function 1, SMX Olongapo City Convention Center.

Ang naturang seminar na may temang: “Leadership Rooted in Purpose: Strengthening Self, Serving Others, Shaping Tomorrow” ay dinaluhan ng dal
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a/]
dership Rooted in Purpose: Strengthening Self, Serving Others, Shaping Tomorrow” ay dinaluhan ng dalawang resource speaker mula Central Luzon State University (CLSU) na sina Dr. Irene G. Bustos, CLSU Student Affairs and Services Dean at ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser.

Ang nabanggit na talakayan ay pinasimulan ni Dr. Bustos sa kanyang presentasyon tungkol sa kung paano nga ba ang tamang pamumuno ng isang lider sa kanyang nasasakupan at mga kalidad na dapat niyang tinataglay. Binigyang pansin din ni Dr. Bustos kung paano gawing reyalidad ang bisyon ng isang lider.

Sa kab
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a/]
y. Binigyang pansin din ni Dr. Bustos kung paano gawing reyalidad ang bisyon ng isang lider.

Sa kabilang banda, binuksan naman ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser, ang diskusyong hinggil sa pagiging gender-responsive at pagbuo ng isang inklusibong kapaligiran at sa loob ng isang institusyon.

Ayon kay Ms. Pelagio, sa pamamagitan ng gender-responsiveness at inklusibong pakikitungo, mas makakabuo tayo ng pagkakaisa, respeto, at ligtas kapaligiran.

“Nag-start kami with few so with support and respect na hindi kami hinahayaan kung hindi sinusuportahan kami na gawin kung ano yun
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a

content:

th support and respect na hindi kami hinahayaan kung hindi sinusuportahan kami na gawin kung ano yung mga activities na kailangan, ‘yun yung isa sa nagmo-motivate sa group o sa org para ituloy yung advocacy.” Saad ni Ms. Pelagio ukol sa lagay ng Paruparong Bukid sa kanilang institusyon.

Si Ms. Pelagio ang tagapayo ng Paruparong Bukid, isang organisasyong nabuo sa isang obserbasyon at pagsasaliksik nina Dr. Bustos patungkol sa homophobia at transphobia na siyang naging pasimundan ng MAYARI na organisayon naman ng LGBTQIA+ ng Dalubhasaang Gordon.

Sa kabuuan ng nasabing seminar, “If you have pa
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a

ganisayon naman ng LGBTQIA+ ng Dalubhasaang Gordon.

Sa kabuuan ng nasabing seminar, “If you have passion for change, keep that passion fired, ika nga, [and] lighted, and pursue your vision and make it a reality. Be aware of everything that happens within and outside yourself. And kung anuman ang mga challenges, mga issues, problems ‘no parte lahat ito ng pagiging leader kaya hold on and keep the fire burning — yung passion mo to lead and to make change for the betterment of your studentry, the community, and siyempre the nation as a whole.” Ani Dr. Bustos sa mga kasalukuyang student leader at
label: %f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a

content:

community, and siyempre the nation as a whole.” Ani Dr. Bustos sa mga kasalukuyang student leader at nagnanais na maging lider sa hinaharap.

GC APERTURA | Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. warmly addressed more than 9,000 Gordon College students during the opening of the First Semester for Academic Year 2025–2026 held at the Tapinac Oval Grounds.

**Saturday, July 5, 2025**

Congratulations to our College President, Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, for being recognized by the CHED Regional Office III for her visionary and innovative leadership, as well as for her commitment to internationalization among local colleges and universities. # Blessing of Gordon College Annex Building

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, August 11, 2025**

Pormal na binasbasan ang bagong Annex Building ng Gordon College ngayong Agosto 11, 2025, sa pangunguna ni Rev. Fr. Roque Villanueva.

Dumalo sa makabuluhang seremonya sina Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., BCDA Director Rolen C. Paulino, Gordon College President Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, mga Konsehal ng Lungsod, at ilan pang opisyal, kasama ang mga estudyante, guro, at kawani ng Gordon College bilang pagsuporta sa makasaysayang pagbubukas ng bagong gusali.

Mahigit
label: blessing-of-gordon-college-annex-building

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/blessing-of-gordon-college-annex-building/]
t kawani ng Gordon College bilang pagsuporta sa makasaysayang pagbubukas ng bagong gusali.

Mahigit sa tatlong libong karagdagang estudyante ang makikinabang sa bagong gusali.

Itinakda ang pagbubukas ng Annex Building kasabay ng pasimula ng klase ngayong Academic Year 2025–2026, bilang bahagi ng patuloy na pagtugon ng lokal na pamahalaan at pamunuan ng kolehiyo sa lumalaking pangangailangan para sa dekalidad at makabagong pasilidad sa edukasyon.

# CHED validates the MAN program of Institute of Graduate Studies

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, June 19, 2025**

The CHED Technical Panel for Nursing, Technical Evaluator for Nursing, and Regional Quality Assessment Team (RQAT) conducted onsite validation at Gordon College, Institute of Graduate Studies, last May 29-30, 2025.

The Gordon College welcomed evaluators from the Commission on Higher Education (CHED)—Regional Quality Assessment Team (RQAT) for an on-site validation aimed at securing a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) for the Master of A
label: ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies/]
 on-site validation aimed at securing a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) for the Master of Arts in Nursing (MAN) program.

Program Chair for MAN, Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., introduced the CHED-RQAT evaluators: Dr. Maria Martina Geraldine Dimalibot, Dr. Al D. Biag, Dr. Mary Ann Gienette D. Medina, and Ms. Niña Jessa Angeli Palaganas.

The evaluators conducted thorough assessments of program administration, faculty credentials, curriculum, student services, facilities, and resources. Their findings and recommendations, discussed in a post-evaluation conference, will guide Gordon College,
label: ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies/]
findings and recommendations, discussed in a post-evaluation conference, will guide Gordon College, Institute of Graduate Studies continuing efforts to enhance academic quality.

Preparations were led by Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Dean of the Institute of Graduate Studies; Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs; Dr. Narcisa R. Figuerres, Assistant Dean of the Institute of Graduate Studies; and Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr. Program Chair for MAN, Ms. Joan Joy A. De Vera; Executive Assistant of the Institute of Graduate Studies, Ms. Diana Grace M. Vallar; Executive Assistant of the Ac
label: ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ched-validates-the-man-program-of-institute-of-graduate-studies/]
stant of the Institute of Graduate Studies, Ms. Diana Grace M. Vallar; Executive Assistant of the Academic Affairs Office; and strong support from other college officials and faculty.

Gordon College, Institute of Graduate Studies, conveyed its sincere gratitude to the CHED-RQAT team for their invaluable insights and steadfast support, recognizing the visit as a major step in the institution’s ongoing journey toward academic excellence.

**Saturday, June 7, 2025**

Gordon College (GC) College of Business and Accountrancy (CBA) records 9 new Certified Public Accountant (CPA) in the recently held May 2025 Certified Public Accountant Licensure Exam (CPALE).

The institution aimed an overall perfomance of 56.25%, which surpassed the national rating of 33.11%, with a total of 9 out of 16 examinees joining 3,156 new CPAs in the country.

Out of 13 first time examinees,

Moreover, Dr. Darwin Paguio, Vice President of Academic Affairs, declared his pride for GC’s achievement.

“The first local college na magkaka-Mac laboratory. [The] first in zambales, [the] first in region 3,” Dr. Paguio stated.

With GC’s climb towards innovation, the institution also eyes international deployment of its students in Macau.

# 𝐌𝐀𝐘𝐀𝐑𝐈 𝐑𝐢𝐬𝐞𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬 𝐟𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐋𝐆𝐁𝐓𝐐𝐈𝐀+ 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐎𝐫𝐠𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, June 18, 2025**

Gordon College established its first LGBTQIA+ Student Organization named “Mga Anak ng Yugtong Apo para sa Respeto at Inklusyon (MAYARI),” in line with the celebration of Pride Month this June.

MAYARI aims to champion the rights and well-being of LGBTQIA+ students within the college and beyond.

Furthermore, Mr. Marko Aratea, organization adviser of MAYARI, confirmed that the organization has developed rapidly and has profound objectives.

“We,
label: %f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae/]
of MAYARI, confirmed that the organization has developed rapidly and has profound objectives.

“We, at MAYARI, are currently in the process of becoming a formally recognized civic organization at Gordon College, but we will get there very soon!” Mr. Aratea stated.

Moreover, MAYARI was formed with both students and the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) in mind as a dedicated community for LGBTQIA+ students and their allies. Both Mr. Aratea and co-adviser Mr. Christian Loid Valenzuela started as the founding members who led the creation of the constitution, set clear objectives, and
label: %f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae/]
 started as the founding members who led the creation of the constitution, set clear objectives, and met institutional standards.

The central aim of MAYARI is to establish a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQIA+ students. Mr. Aratea outlined the organization’s primary objectives: “MAYARI aims to provide a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQIA+ students and allies in our humble institution. Our main goals are to promote visibility, inclusivity, respect, empowerment, and solidarity.”

MAYARI intends to accomplish these objectives through education by providing LGBTQIA+ seminars, webinars, gender
label: %f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae/]
ds to accomplish these objectives through education by providing LGBTQIA+ seminars, webinars, gender sensitivity programs, and opportunities for creativity and leadership.

Furthermore, one of the important goals of MAYARI is to handle issues affecting LGBTQIA+ individuals, mainly discrimination, mental health challenges, and lack of community understanding.

“MAYARI is a civic organization for LGBTQIA+ and allies in GC, also serving as a platform to address catalysts related to gender-based concerns through formal dialogues programs and activities that will equip relevant stakeholders regardi
label: %f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae/]
cerns through formal dialogues programs and activities that will equip relevant stakeholders regarding said concerns and practical solutions,” MAYARI’s adviser affirmed.

Meanwhile, the organization will implement peer support initiatives, explore partnerships with the college’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) office, and launch extensive awareness campaigns on SOGIESC (Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics).

Looking beyond symbolic gestures, MAYARI envisions a future where “total acceptance” is deeply ingrained in the institution’s policies an
label: %f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae/]
ARI envisions a future where “total acceptance” is deeply ingrained in the institution’s policies and culture.

“Total acceptance means more than symbolic gestures—it involves policy and culture change,” Mr. Aratea emphasized and further discussed that the organization aims to integrate inclusivity into the college’s systemic operations rather than limiting it to mere statements.

# Gordon College Joins the 2025 Times Higher Education Impact Rankings

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, June 18, 2025**

We are proud to announce that Gordon College has officially earned a place in the prestigious Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Rankings 2025! Despite being a newcomer to the global stage, GC was ranked among the top 1,500+ out of 2,526 higher education institutions (HEIs) from 130 countries, assessed for their contributions to achieving the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs)—a recognition based on universities’ soci
label: gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings/]
the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs)—a recognition based on universities’ social impact through research, stewardship, outreach, and teaching.

Gordon College participated in four SDG categories and earned the following standings:

• SDG 3 – Good Health and Well-being: Ranked 1001–1500

• SDG 4 – Quality Education: Ranked 1501+

• SDG 10 – Reduced Inequalities: Ranked 1001+

• SDG 17 – Partnerships for the Goals: Ranked 1501+

More than just recognition, this milestone reflects our evolving vision—to become a globally recognized local institution making meaningful contrib
label: gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings/]
ects our evolving vision—to become a globally recognized local institution making meaningful contributions to global progress through inclusive, equitable, and sustainable development in higher education.

# 10 HEIs sa Central Luzon, pasok sa Times Higher Education Impact Rankings 2025

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, June 23, 2025**

Muling pinatunayan ng Central Luzon ang galing nito sa larangan ng edukasyon matapos mapasama ang 10 pampublikong Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) sa Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Ranking 2025.

Batay sa social media post ng Commission on Higher Education (CHED) Regional Office III, kabilang sa prestihiyosong listahan ang mga sumusunod na institusyon:

1. Central Luzon State University (801-1000)

2. Tarlac Agricultural U
label: 10-heis-sa-central-luzon-pasok-sa-times-higher-education-impact-rankings-2025

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/10-heis-sa-central-luzon-pasok-sa-times-higher-education-impact-rankings-2025/]
ga sumusunod na institusyon:

1. Central Luzon State University (801-1000)

2. Tarlac Agricultural University (1001-1500)

3. Bataan Peninsula State University (1501+)

4. Bulacan State University (1501+)

5. Don Honorio Ventura State University (1501+)

6. Nueva Ecija State University of Science and Technology (1501+)

7. Tarlac State University (1501+)

8. Gordon College (1501+)

9. Mabalacat City College (1501+)

10. City College of San Fernando, Pampanga (1501+)

Ang THE Impact Rankings ay sumusukat sa kontribusyon ng mga kolehiyo at unibersidad sa pag-abot ng United Nations Sustainable De
label: 10-heis-sa-central-luzon-pasok-sa-times-higher-education-impact-rankings-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/10-heis-sa-central-luzon-pasok-sa-times-higher-education-impact-rankings-2025/]
umusukat sa kontribusyon ng mga kolehiyo at unibersidad sa pag-abot ng United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), gaya ng dekalidad na edukasyon, gender equality, climate action, at iba pa.

Layunin nitong kilalanin at ipagdiwang ang mga institusyong aktibong nakikibahagi sa pagsusulong ng mas maayos at mas inklusibong mundo.

Ngayong taon, higit 2,000 universities mula sa 125 na bansa at teritoryo ang lumahok sa ranking. Sa bilang na ito, nasa 121 na institusyon sa Pilipinas ang kinilala, patunay ng lumlawak na global recognition pagdating sa kalidad ng edukasyon sa bansa.

# “2,000 change-makers” attend NSTP grad ‘25

[NSTP](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/nstp/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, June 8, 2025**

Two thousand Gordon College (GC) students enrolled in their National Service and Training Program (NSTP) courses filled the SMX Convention Center, Olongapo City, on Wednesday, for this year’s NSTP Graduation Ceremony.

With the theme, “Volunteerism in Action: Shaping Leaders, Strengthening Community”, this year’s batch of NSTP graduates was graced by Miss Manila 2014 Dr. Krischelle D. Halili.

Dr. Halili emphasized tha
label: 2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25/]
f NSTP graduates was graced by Miss Manila 2014 Dr. Krischelle D. Halili.

Dr. Halili emphasized that leadership is an “act not for yourself, but for others” in her address. “Serve something greater than yourself and take your friends with you.”

Moreover, she called on students to love the country and God while also channeling ‘tech natives’ to participate in online community initiatives as well as community services and university council activities on-site.

Leading the initial confirmation of the graduates, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also delivered his special
label: 2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25/]
f the graduates, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also delivered his special message, focusing on the impact of genuine leadership.

“True leadership is not defined merely by title or accolades but by the impact you make in the lives of others,” he said as he congratulated the “two thousand change-makers” afterward.

This was followed by the awarding of certificates to Literary Training Service (LTS) and Civic Welfare Training Service (CWTS) completers from the different departments of the institution.

NSTP Instructor Mr. Hubert Lew Magrata delivered his closing remark
label: 2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25/]
departments of the institution.

NSTP Instructor Mr. Hubert Lew Magrata delivered his closing remarks to officially close the program.

# Gordon College Launches State-of-the-Art Mac Laboratory

[CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, April 28, 2025**

Gordon College (GC) proudly announces the official launch of its new Mac Laboratory, a significant enhancement to the technological resources available to its students. The laboratory is equipped with 25 advanced iMac M4 units, representing a substantial investment in providing students with industry-standard technology to support their academic pursuits and skill development.

[CAHS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/cahs/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, May 14, 2025**

Gordon College (GC) secures third spot among top performing schools in the May 2025 Philippine Nurses Licensure Examination (PNLE), earning a rating of 98.29%, as 115 out of 117 examinees successfully made the cut.

Moreover, two GC Alumni; Martin Jorge Bernal Alcantara and Loren Mirador Mistica, emerged as top 9, both acing a rate of 88.20%.

𝗮𝗴𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗮, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗠𝘀. 𝗟𝗼𝘃𝗲 𝗟𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗻𝘂𝘀 𝗣. 𝗔𝗰𝗲𝗱𝗼, as well as their coaches 𝗠𝘀. 𝗝𝗲𝗵𝗮𝗻 𝗗𝗼𝗯𝗹𝗮𝗱𝗼 and 𝗠𝗿. 𝗖𝗵𝗿𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗮𝗻 𝗟𝗼𝗶𝗱 𝗩𝗮𝗹𝗲𝗻𝘇𝘂𝗲𝗹𝗮, for being 𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝗻 𝗞𝗢𝗟𝗘𝗛𝗜𝗬𝗔𝗪 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿-𝘀𝗰𝗵𝗼𝗼𝗹 𝗗𝗲𝗯𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 𝗮𝘁 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝘂𝗺𝗯𝗮𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲.

They have also won and received the following awards:

2𝒏𝒅 𝑩𝒆𝒔𝒕 𝑫𝒆𝒃𝒂𝒕𝒆𝒓 — 𝐋𝐨𝐯𝐞 𝐋𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞 𝐕𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐬 𝐏. 𝐀𝐜𝐞𝐝𝐨

𝑷𝒆𝒐𝒑𝒍𝒆’𝒔 𝑪𝒉𝒐𝒊𝒄𝒆 𝑨𝒘𝒂𝒓𝒅 — 𝐋𝐮𝐜𝐤𝐲 𝐔𝐞𝐥 𝐌𝐚𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐚

𝘖𝘯 𝘣𝘦𝘩𝘢𝘭𝘧 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘊𝘌𝘈𝘚 𝘧𝘢𝘮𝘪𝘭𝘺, 𝙬𝙚 𝙖𝙧𝙚 𝙖𝙡𝙡 𝙨𝙤 𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙪𝙙 𝙤𝙛 𝙮𝙤𝙪. Baka 𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗔𝗞 𝗚𝗖𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝘆𝗮𝗻!

# Gordon College Registration to National Privacy Commission

Gordon College, as a Personal Information Controller aims to faithfully complies to Republic Act 10173 or also known as the Data Privacy Act of 2012. Wherein the institution promotes the protection of the privacy of its stakeholders by registering its Data Privacy Officer and Data Processing Systems (both manual and computerized/online) to the National Privacy Commission (NPC).

---

You can verify our registration to NPC by scanning the code.

We would like to remind our Dear Students and Parents to ask this kind of seal to other organization(s) that will process your personal information.

---
label: gordon-college-registrations-to-npc

# GC smashers bag gold, silver medals at LCUAA 2023

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 13, 2023**

Glenn Ian Amancio, Krisha Maeh Estocapio, Janea Galindez, and Rose Ann Mesias of Gordon College Men’s and Women’s Badminton team made a splash after squeaking past the gritty Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa (PLMun) and getting the gold in mixed doubles event and silver in women’s doubles at Laguna Sports Complex, October 11, 2023.

Amancio and Estocapio made sure that they can match the wild and roaring crowd as they shoved their opponent in the badmint
label: gc-smashers-bag-gold-silver-medals-at-lcuaa-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-smashers-bag-gold-silver-medals-at-lcuaa-2023/]
ade sure that they can match the wild and roaring crowd as they shoved their opponent in the badminton mix doubles to earn the gold with a score of 21-11, 22-20. Meanwhile, Galindez and Mesias tried to survive the game, but fell short of winning the gold against PLMun and getting the silver medal.

“I think deserve naman namin manalo dahil marami kaming pinagdaanan at binigay talaga namin ‘yung best namin. Mahirap siya pero since nakalaban na namin sila (PLMun), alam na namin ‘yung dapat naming gawin”, Estocapio said.

Meanwhile, the badminton team are now focused to keep themselves on track a
label: gc-smashers-bag-gold-silver-medals-at-lcuaa-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-smashers-bag-gold-silver-medals-at-lcuaa-2023/]
gawin”, Estocapio said.

Meanwhile, the badminton team are now focused to keep themselves on track as they prepare for the upcoming LCUAA 2024.

# Breakthrough Research by Secondary Education Major in English Students Published in International Journal

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, October 17, 2023**

October 11, 2023 – In a significant achievement for the Secondary Education Major in the English program, four individuals have earned recognition for their groundbreaking research. Ms. Myrnalyn Garachico, Ms. Queeney Fortaleza, Ms. Ma. Elisa Depison, and their esteemed instructor, Ms. Madeleine Marcial, have seen their research article titled “Pre-Service Teachers’ Level of Cognition in Teaching Gram
label: breakthrough-research-by-secondary-education-major-in-english-students-published-in-international-journal

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/breakthrough-research-by-secondary-education-major-in-english-students-published-in-international-journal/]
, have seen their research article titled “Pre-Service Teachers’ Level of Cognition in Teaching Grammar: Basis for Skills Refinement Plan” published in the prestigious International Journal of Academic and Applied Research.

According to Ms. Depison, “This study is about how pre-service teachers’ level of cognition can affect teaching and how it can be beneficial to learners and educational providers. They conduct this study to find out what things make future teachers think and learn about teaching grammar; and how pre-service teachers perceive and instruct grammar within the classroom.”

The
label: breakthrough-research-by-secondary-education-major-in-english-students-published-in-international-journal

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/breakthrough-research-by-secondary-education-major-in-english-students-published-in-international-journal/]
hing grammar; and how pre-service teachers perceive and instruct grammar within the classroom.”

The students were overjoyed when they learned that their research had been published. “We’re on top of the world after reading the email,” Ms. Fortaleza said. “Although unexpected since this is our first time, we felt fulfilled.”

The study was not without its challenges, however. The most challenging part, according to Ms. Garachico, was the data collection phase. “It’s like trying to start a conversation with someone who just won’t talk to you – you’re left feeling stuck and frustrated. You must

# GCians “in-game in selling again”

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, February 19, 2024**

TAPINAC OVAL — Gordon College students-turned-entrepreneurs set up over forty stores in the 2-day “CBA Entrepreneurship Fair 2024” held yearly by the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) in celebration of the week-long GC 25th Founding Anniversary amidst 38-degree Celsius heat today, February 20.

The College of Allied Health and Sciences (CAHS), College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), College of Computer Sciences (CCS), and College of Hospitality and Touris
label: gcians-in-game-in-selling-again

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-in-game-in-selling-again/]
Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), College of Computer Sciences (CCS), and College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) also made an appearance with their respective stores in the fair.

CBA-GC wants students in BSBA in Financial Management, HR Management, and Marketing Management to promote collaborative and competitive skills, and give importance not only to their majors but also to entrepreneurship, especially to the first-time goers, BS in Accountancy (BSA) and Customs Administration (BSCA) students.

“As business and (accountancy) students, we want to nurture the entrepreneurial skills of
label: gcians-in-game-in-selling-again

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-in-game-in-selling-again/]
students.

“As business and (accountancy) students, we want to nurture the entrepreneurial skills of the CBA students, this (fair) could be the trigger,” said Project Manager, Nigel N. Silvestre.

Among the stores, the Facebook favorites “Sisig Ni Nigel” of BSBA – FM 2B and “Mas Pinasarap Na Sisig Ni Nigel” of BSBA – HRM 2A all set up at the middle left and front right of the oval respectively to take over the “Sisig war.”

“Marketing (strategy) nila ‘yan, (ang) gamitin ang aking pangalan, go ahead,” added Silvestre.

Moreover, school organizations including the League of English Language and
label: gcians-in-game-in-selling-again

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-in-game-in-selling-again/]
ead,” added Silvestre.

Moreover, school organizations including the League of English Language and Literature Students (LELLS) and The Forefront put in place their respective stores to join the wars.

Olongapo City Agriculture and Eco-Tourism Office and the United Farmers and Fisherfolks Association also made their presence felt with their fresh fruits and vegetables at the fair.

🏻: Keane Abdon | Sports Correspondent

: Cindy Soriano | Photojournalist

: Isaac Leon | Multimedia Editor

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, July 22, 2024**

BACOLOR, Pampanga – As part of its ongoing pursuit of excellence and vision to become a premier local college, [Gordon Colleg](https://www.facebook.com/GordonCollegeOfficial?__cft__[0]=AZWeOHPLp0d7HU_EaEokQN48EMJ_TFnBWfxXx6Ygbg93Q5r3cra5p6rKqXN6Rg1Na-GItxpaiufaXVExEX4xdqaguUGY4WcBEF-qCL1R6-xvbkWkOq_QZDd6nRYgX41Y08NnMIdTULQIBHcmORyKNnYjCNHlDQNiAO_XRcfM4d63xgIuk0Czxqt48PJlnwas9mqpYJMUE1fGq7SE9WwESi6fX2OUukO1IjaZPlfYogIqz1LZEBK_VEGeqPrJtsI50

GC aims to improve its academic programs and administrative operations by learning from the experiences, models, and best practices of other higher education institutions like DHVSU.

The visi
label: gordon-college-benchmarks-dhvsu-for-gad-public-administration-programs

Quiambao, Vice President for Student Affairs and Services, and concurrent Dean of the Graduate School and Director of the GAD Focal Point System, facilitated the visit. Other DHVSU officials who played a key role in the benchmarking event were Mr. Luisito B. Reyes, Dean of the College of Business Studies, Ms. Maria Liberty Isip, Program Chairperson of the Bachelor in Public Administration Program

The delegation from GC, led by their OIC-College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, included members of the Executive Management and Academic Council Officials.

“With my team, [I would like to] thank you very much. Your knowledge and wisdom today are appreciated. They will help improve our efforts, especially for a local college. We believe that DHVSU has strong governance and administration, and of c
label: gordon-college-benchmarks-dhvsu-for-gad-public-administration-programs

# SINGSON ON WINNING MS. GC 2024: “I feel very honored and empowered.”

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, February 26, 2024**

TAPINAC SPORTS COMPLEX – Ma. Francesca Simone Singson, student from Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN), defended the crown to the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) as she became the Ms. Gordon College (GC) 2024 last February 21.

According to an interview with The Forefront, she felt very honored and empowered when she realized that she won against eight aspiring Ms. GC candidates in the said competition.

“I feel very honored an
label: singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered/]
t she won against eight aspiring Ms. GC candidates in the said competition.

“I feel very honored and empowered kasi finally po mayroon na po akong posisyon to empower other people and influence them,” she stated.

Furthermore, she quoted that she had concluded that the question in the Q&A portion was fooling her but standing still from what she believed and advocated made her a queen.

“Sabi ko ay inuuto ako nito so by the second question nag iisip na ako ng sagot, bakit ako papayag na hindi nila suportahan ang aking advocacy? So by the said question meron na po akong sagot nun,” the Ms. GC 2
label: singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered/]
la suportahan ang aking advocacy? So by the said question meron na po akong sagot nun,” the Ms. GC 2024 revealed.

Moreover, the BSN student also expressed her experiences with her co-female and male candidates during their pageant journey.

“Nagkaroon ako ng mga new friends talaga, no competition sa amin and all support, that is why I love women and not just the female (candidates) but also the male (candidates), no toxicity at all,” the CAHS representative discussed.

Meanwhile, she gave her gratitude to all who supported her along with her promise to be a great role model to the GCians.

“S
label: singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/singson-on-winning-ms-gc-2024-i-feel-very-honored-and-empowered/]
ratitude to all who supported her along with her promise to be a great role model to the GCians.

“Salamat po sa pagsuporta, salamat po sa inyong lahat maasahan niyo po ako as a great role model,” the new GC Queen insisted.

On the other hand, Singson will also represent the institution at the upcoming Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) 2024.

: I-man Anthonie Klay Garcia | News Editor

: John Patrick Mina | Photojournalist

: Isaac Leon | Multimedia Editor & Angelique Jose | Graphics Editor

# Clearing the Coast, One Trash at a Time

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, September 16, 2023**

Different student organizations from Gordon College and other colleges of Olongapo City, along with volunteers, local offices, and the Local Government Unit of Olongapo City have participated in the “Making Riverth Possible 2023”, a citywide cleanup drive held at Kalaklan Parola and Driftwood Beach earlier today, September 16, 2023.

According to the Olongapo City Information Center, there are an estimated 3,000 volunteers for the coastal clean-up, which made it
label: clearing-the-coast-one-trash-at-a-time

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/clearing-the-coast-one-trash-at-a-time/]
Information Center, there are an estimated 3,000 volunteers for the coastal clean-up, which made it successful once again. The volunteers of the event were given checklists in which they were required to categorize and count the trash that they had collected. The checklists will be used to gain information about the most common trash found in the coastal areas.

The event was graced by the Olongapo City Council which gave away several souvenir shirts as memorabilia for the event.

# IONS Conducts Research Seminar for Graduating GCians

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, November 21, 2023**

Gordon College’s Interactive Organization of Natural Science (IONS) officiated a research seminar for graduating students with the theme, “IONS-IN-ACTION: Igniting Outstanding Narratives for Scholarly and Successful Journal Publications,” on November 17, 2023.

Meanwhile, in an interview with The Forefront, John Diezel Mama-o, President of IONS, said that the activity was proposed by Mr. Kristoffer Paul C. Obispo, Adviser of IONS. He also mentioned tha
label: ions-conducts-research-seminar-for-graduating-gcians

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ions-conducts-research-seminar-for-graduating-gcians/]
t the activity was proposed by Mr. Kristoffer Paul C. Obispo, Adviser of IONS. He also mentioned that this was the first seminar their organization had ever held.

Furthermore, the said seminar helped the participants on the process of making their thesis studies into a publishable research journal. “The seminar workshop covers key topics such as selecting the right journal, structuring the article, crafting a compelling abstract, adhering to journal guidelines, and addressing peer reviewer feedback,” Mama-o added.

Moreover, participants look into techniques for making engaging introductions,
label: ions-conducts-research-seminar-for-graduating-gcians

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ions-conducts-research-seminar-for-graduating-gcians/]
back,” Mama-o added.

Moreover, participants look into techniques for making engaging introductions, arranging research findings, improving impactful results, and weaving together discussions that attracts readers and journal editors.

In addition, the event had 50 participants from BSEd-Science, Math, Social Studies, BA Communication, and BS Tourism Management of the institution.

# Nursing Student Reigns as MX. GC PRIDE 2024

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, February 25, 2024**

OVAL TAPINAC GROUNDS — In the first-day celebration of Gordon College’s Foundation anniversary, Five delegates from the LGBTQ+ community representing their respective colleges were crowned as Queens of the Institution’s first-ever MX. GC Pride 2024 on Tuesday, February 20, 2024.

With reference to the competition’s placements, four candidates were given the title of runner-ups, ranking from 1st-4th:

•Mx. Vench Paje- 1ST RUNNER-UP

•Mx. Josh Allen Bermudez- 2ND
label: nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024/]
of runner-ups, ranking from 1st-4th:

•Mx. Vench Paje- 1ST RUNNER-UP

•Mx. Josh Allen Bermudez- 2ND RUNNER-UP

•Mx. Joshua Salgado- 3RD RUNNER-UP

•Mx. John Michael Arcelao- 4TH RUNNER-UP

Ultimately, the competition crowned Mx. Albert Panotes, a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) student from the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS), as the winner of Mx. GC Pride 2024.

Beyond the highest title given to Mx. Panotes, she was also awarded Mx. Congeniality, Mx. Beautiful Body, Best in Pride Attire, and Best in Formal Wear.

Amplifying her winning answer on the Q&A portion, Mx. Panotes proud
label: nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024/]
ttire, and Best in Formal Wear.

Amplifying her winning answer on the Q&A portion, Mx. Panotes proudly answered that she must lead by example when asked by the chosen judge regarding her contribution to beautifying the image of the LGBTQ+ community in a world where members struggle to achieve unimpaired acceptance.

“For me, as a student, I know that I don’t have that much resources to contribute to society, but as someone from the medical field, and also as a student leader, I must lead by example. And by that alone, I know that will be the greatest contribution,” she said.

Mx. Panotes elabo
label: nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/nursing-student-reigns-as-mx-gc-pride-2024/]
ple. And by that alone, I know that will be the greatest contribution,” she said.

Mx. Panotes elaborated on her answer, believing that we may be informed, empowered, and inclusive by becoming an example to the younger generation and her other transwomen. She also feels that making a difference is far greater than making vital contributions to our society.

Moreover, Mx. Panotes, being the winner of the competition, is expected to represent Pride Week events as well as incoming LGBTQ+ pageants.

: Lauren Louise | Photojournalist

CHED Memorandum Order No. 5 Series of 1998 Associate in Computer Technology (ACT) Program is a two (2) year non-degree program leading to a diploma in Associate in Computer Technology

The Associate in Computer Technology program is useful for students who wish to gain a foundational knowledge in computer science and information technology. As it is intended to lead to a baccalaureate program, those who wish to take the ACT should also plan to get an undergraduate degree in any computer science and information technology related programs.

# CCS CELEBRATES MULTIPLE VICTORIES IN THE MR. & MS. GORDON COLLEGE 2024 PAGEANT

* Posted on
  February 24, 2024

In a heartwarming display of outstanding talent and dedication, CCS students at Gordon College have left an indelible mark on the prestigious Mr. & Ms. Gordon College 2024 pageant. It is with immense pride and joy that we, the CCS Student Council, extend our heartfelt congratulations to Mr. Carl Wilkin Barrera for securing the title of Mr. Gordon College 2024, Best in Thematic Wear, Best in Casual Wear, and Best in Formal Wear, showcasing his exceptional prowess in the field
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs mr-and-ms

[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/mr-and-ms/]
 Wear, Best in Casual Wear, and Best in Formal Wear, showcasing his exceptional prowess in the field of Information Technology (BSIT).

Adding to the glory of CCS, Ms. Jeanwil Julie Espinocilla (BSCS) has earned the esteemed position of the first runner-up, Best in Thematic Wear, and Best in Casual Wear, while Mr. Bryan Mascariña (BSEMC) has achieved the title of the 4th runner-up and Best in Swimwear. These remarkable achievements not only highlight the individual excellence of these students but also reflect the collective spirit of dedication and commitment within the College of Computer St
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs mr-and-ms

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/mr-and-ms/]
ut also reflect the collective spirit of dedication and commitment within the College of Computer Studies. We applaud each participant for their hard work and passion, contributing to the thriving academic community at Gordon College.

Congratulations once again to Mr. Carl Wilkin Barrera, Ms. Jeanwil Julie Espinocilla, Mr. Bryan Mascariña, and all the participants for their outstanding accomplishments!

The program prepares graduates to address various user needs involving the selection, development, application, integration and management of computing technologies within an organization.

BSIT graduates are expected to become globally competent, innovative, a

BSIT graduates are expected to become globally competent, innovative, and socially and ethically responsible computing professionals engaged in life-long learning endeavors. They are capable of contributing to the country’s national development goals.

The BS in Computer Science (BSCS) program includes the study of computing concepts and theories, algorithms foundations and new development in computing. The program prepares students to design and create algorithmically complex software and develop new and effective algorithms for solving computing problems.

The program also includes the study of the standards and practices in Software Engineering. It prepares students to acquire skills and disciplines required for designing, writing and modifying software components, modules and applications that comprise software solutions.

The BSCS
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs program-offerings bscs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/bscs/]
 modifying software components, modules and applications that comprise software solutions.

The BSCS graduates are expected to become globally competent, innovative, and socially and ethically responsible computing professionals engaged in life-long learning endeavors. They are capable of contributing to the country’s national development goals.

## PROGRAM OFFERINGS

## Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

A Bachelor of Science in Computer Science is a comprehensive program tailored for students intrigued by the inner workings of technology and its applications. Delving into foundational concepts such as algorithms, data structures, and software engineering, this degree equips students with the knowledge and skills needed to tackle complex computational problems across diverse domains. From developing cutting-edge software solutions to optimizing systems for efficiency and security, students explore a wide range of topics includin
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs program-offerings

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/]
 to optimizing systems for efficiency and security, students explore a wide range of topics including artificial intelligence, computer networking, and database management.  
 [View Course](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/bscs)

## Bachelor of Science in Information Technology

A Bachelor of Science in Information Technology is a dynamic program designed for students fascinated by the intersection of technology and business. It offers a comprehensive understanding of IT systems, infrastructure, and applications essential for modern organizations. Through coursework covering
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs program-offerings

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/]
s, infrastructure, and applications essential for modern organizations. Through coursework covering topics such as database management, network administration, cybersecurity, and project management, students gain practical skills to design, implement, and manage information systems effectively. Emphasizing real-world applications and industry-relevant technologies, this degree prepares graduates for diverse career paths, including IT consulting, system analysis, web development and IT project management.  
 [View Course](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/bsit)

## Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Multimedia Computing

A Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Multimedia Computing is a dynamic interdisciplinary program designed for students passionate about merging technology with creativity. Through a blend of computer science fundamentals and multimedia production techniques, this program equips students with the skills to develop innovative digital content across various platforms including gaming, animation, virtual reality, and interactive media. From co
label: https:  gordoncollege.edu.ph ccs program-offerings

cross various platforms including gaming, animation, virtual reality, and interactive media. From coding and programming to graphic design and storytelling, students explore the convergence of technology and entertainment, preparing them for exciting careers in industries such as game development, film production, advertising, and digital media.  
 [View Course](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/ccs/program-offerings/bsemc)

The Associate in Computer Technology (ACT) is a 2-year degree program that offers specialized computing and information technology tracks.

# Lunch for a Cause

[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)

**Saturday, February 18, 2023**

The Office of Student Welfare and Services orchestrated a Lunch for a Cause on February 18 in collaboration with the GC Community Extension Services Unit. With the invaluable assistance of the CESU Coordinators and the Marahuyo Events Team, comprised of dedicated fourth-year BSHM student volunteers, this event was effectively executed. Among the
label: lunch-for-a-cause

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/lunch-for-a-cause/]
ed of dedicated fourth-year BSHM student volunteers, this event was effectively executed. Among the 200 available tickets, 154 were successfully sold. A portion of the funds generated was allocated towards covering the expenses associated with the “GC Job Fair” and other foundation celebration costs. Additionally, these funds will contribute to sustaining future community extension initiatives of OSWS and GC-CESU.

# Beyond Boundaries: GCAAI’s Voluntary Visions Flourish at Kamp Kalinga

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, August 5, 2023**

Gordon College Alumni Association Inc. Community Extension Program Update

Dear Gordon College stakeholders, we are delighted to share the resounding success of our participation in the Kamp Kalinga event on August 5, 2023. Led by the unwavering initiative of our dedicated President Michael Tuyay, VP Abraham Dionson Jr., and Trustee John Erder Mora, the GCAAI community united to make
label: %f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%b2%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%b2%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac/]
hael Tuyay, VP Abraham Dionson Jr., and Trustee John Erder Mora, the GCAAI community united to make a profound impact.

We had a fulfilling day at Kamp Kalinga, engaging in various activities that brought radiant smiles to the faces of the children.  We would also like to extend credit to all GCAAI officers, whose generous financial contributions and voluntary services made this endeavor possible. Together, we exemplified the power of unity and the transformative potential of even the smallest acts of kindness in the community.

Let us joyfully celebrate the spirit of generosity and compassion
label: %f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%b2%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%b2%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%81%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac/]
cts of kindness in the community.

Let us joyfully celebrate the spirit of generosity and compassion within our GCAAI community. Stay tuned for more updates on our future projects, and we wholeheartedly invite you to join us in our efforts to create a lasting impact. Together, we can achieve remarkable milestones! # University of Eastern Pangasinan Visits GC

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [IPDEA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ipdea/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, April 8, 2025**

The University of Eastern Pangasinan successfully conducted a benchmarking visit at Gordon College today in preparation for its Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) accreditation, which is scheduled for September 3–5, 2025.

Dr. Evelyn Abalos-Tomboc, University President, and her team personally visited the college
label: university-of-eastern-pangasinan-visits-gc

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/university-of-eastern-pangasinan-visits-gc/]
, 2025.

Dr. Evelyn Abalos-Tomboc, University President, and her team personally visited the college to gain insights and learn from the institution’s best practices in quality assurance and accreditation.

Gordon College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano and other GC officials and personnel warmly welcomed the UEP personnel and shared her institution’s experiences and strategies for navigating the accreditation process.

This benchmarking activity forms part of the University of Eastern Pangasinan’s continuous efforts to align its processes with global quality standards. Their unwavering comm
label: university-of-eastern-pangasinan-visits-gc

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/university-of-eastern-pangasinan-visits-gc/]
nan’s continuous efforts to align its processes with global quality standards. Their unwavering commitment to excellence and strong accreditation culture have provided UEP with meaningful guidance as it strives to fulfill its mission and vision.

# BARRERA ON WINNING MR. GC 2024: “Kung kaya ko, kaya rin ng iba.”

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, February 26, 2024**

TAPINAC SPORTS COMPLEX – Carl Wilkin Barrera, Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT) student, hailed as the new king of Gordon College (GC) as he stands out among other male candidates during the Coronation Night on February 21.

In an interview with The Forefront, he revealed that he didn’t expect this kind of achievement and also believed that his opponents were more deserving compared to him.

“Actually hindi ko siya ineexpe
label: barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba/]
so believed that his opponents were more deserving compared to him.

“Actually hindi ko siya ineexpect na mananalo ako kasi sa totoo lang feel ko na mayroon pang ibang candidate na mas deserving na Manalo,” Mr. GC 2024 said.

Furthermore, he also didn’t expect the question since he only focused on what he had reviewed before the competition but his adlibs brought him to the crown he was aiming for.

Moreover, the BSIT student expressed how proud he was of his co-candidates despite the pressure and competition they have while he also gave some advice to the next aspiring Mr. and Ms. GC.

“They
label: barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba/]
nd competition they have while he also gave some advice to the next aspiring Mr. and Ms. GC.

“They did their best naman and did their part, I’m proud of them and sa next na Mr. or Ms. Gordon College, kung kaya ko, kaya rin ng iba,” he stated.

Meanwhile, he discussed that he was open and willing to improve himself more especially now that he already has a title.

“Actually average student lang ako pero since dahil nagkaroon ng title parang nagkaroon ng expectation sa akin so im willing to improve myself more,” the new GC King insisted.

On the other hand, Barrera will also represent the insti
label: barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barrera-on-winning-mr-gc-2024-kung-kaya-ko-kaya-rin-ng-iba/]
ve myself more,” the new GC King insisted.

On the other hand, Barrera will also represent the institution at the upcoming Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) 2024.

: John Patrick Mina | Photojournalist

# GC emerged as Champions in the 28th Philippine Statistic Quiz – Provincial Level

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 30, 2024**

Congratulations to our talented students, Rholly N. Cao Jr., Matthew A. Manalo, and Kurt Michael H. Banaag, for emerging as CHAMPIONS in the 28th Philippine Statistics Quiz – Provincial Level!

We are proud to celebrate this victory for the second consecutive year! Special recognition to our dedicated coaches, Mr. Leby A. Fastidio and Mr. St. Lawrence Abogadie, along with our esteemed Event Coordinator and BSEd-Math Program
label: gc-emerged-as-champions-in-the-28th-philippine-statistic-quiz-provincial-level

Amarille, and Vice President of Academic Affairs, Dr. Darwin P. Paguio for their unwavering support and guidance.

Our heartfelt gratitude goes to the City of Olongapo, especially, Hon. Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. and Councilor, Hon. Cristabelle Marie “Tata” Paulino, and Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, for their invaluable support, and to Gordon College for hosting this remarkable event.

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, April 6, 2024**

GORDON COLLEGE – Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation’s (ALCU-COA) three-day evaluation concluded with an Exit Conference yesterday, April 5, 2024.

The last day began with ALCU-COA Accreditors conducted Classroom Observation while others finalized the findings and recommendations.

Furthermore, the last evaluation day continued as Rev. Fr. Roque Villanueva led a holy mass and prayers fo

rmore, the last evaluation day continued as Rev. Fr. Roque Villanueva led a holy mass and prayers for the institution.

A procession ceremony dedicated for the arrival of the 13 accreditors accompanied by the college officials, the participants welcomed them by a warm greetings and applause.

To formally start the conference, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, College President, gave her welcome remarks to the faculty members, college personnels, guests and students.

The program continued with the sharing of overall impressions for the 13 programs and facilities offered by the Gordon College initiated by
label: %f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%8e-%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%8d-%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%94-%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%80

f overall impressions for the 13 programs and facilities offered by the Gordon College initiated by Prof. Alain Anuevo, ALCU-COA Supervising Technical Assistant.

In line with this, different program coordinators shared their responses from some recommendations and followed by the special performances from Pase Ballo and Pase Voci.

Moreover, the conference ended with the presentation of certificates and tokens for the Accreditation Team and a heartwarming speech by Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College Administrator, as she went emotional in her closing remarks.

“Pahinga muna po kami (and) we will

ge Administrator, as she went emotional in her closing remarks.

“Pahinga muna po kami (and) we will continue our journey as the Apo Warriors keep on fighting until we reach our mission and vision and leave a legacy for Apo ni Apo to become one of the university in Olongapo (City),” the college administrator stated.

Gordon College (GC) Dancesport Team dominated the dancefloor with their on-point grooves in the 12th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) 2023 National Games Dancesport Competition at Laguna University Multipurpose Hall.

# APERTURA 2023: GCians welcome New Academic Year

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, August 15, 2023**

Gordon College officially starts the Academic Year 2023-2024 with its annually conducted Apertura event with the theme “Paglalayag 2023: Navigating towards New Horizons, Sailing Beyond 25 Years.”

Thousands of students gathered at the East Tapinac Oval Track and shout for their college as they welcome a new academic year.

With colorful things that represent their colleges, student enjoyed different booths and games prepared by student-leaders of their respec

eir colleges, student enjoyed different booths and games prepared by student-leaders of their respective programs.

The event suddenly went into silence as a sign of sympathy and condolences for the passing of the father of Prof. Arlida M. Pame, College President.

On the other hand, Olongapo City Mayor, Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. proudly stated in his opening remarks that among almost 5,000 applicants, the institution admitted 2,000 freshmen and said that they were lucky to be accepted due to the high standards of the college.

College Administrator Dr. Imelda DP Soriano, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, and Olongapo City Councilor Cristabelle Marie “Tata” C. Paulino also graced the stage and gave an inspirational speech in front of the students.

As the A.Y. 2023–2024 officially started, the new elected officers from different organizations took their oaths, led by Mayor Paulino Jr. Along with the presentation of the new officials of the Gordon College Supreme Student Council (GC-SSC).

# VICTORIOUS KICKS: GC prevails, clinches LCUAA Futsal Championship

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, October 10, 2023**

𝗟𝗔𝗚𝗨𝗡𝗔, 𝗣𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗽𝗽𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀– 𝙐𝙉𝘿𝙀𝙁𝙀𝘼𝙏𝙀𝘿.

Gordon College (GC) Men’s Futsal Team successfully conquered the court and remained at the top of all teams after defeating Taguig City University (TCU) 8-2, in the championship game of the 12th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) 2023 National Games Men’s Futsal at Laguna University Multipurpose Hall.

After a series of defense in the tumultuous arena, GC pulverized TCU as Dominado
label: %f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%93%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%94%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%97%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%93%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%94%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%97%9a/]
Multipurpose Hall.

After a series of defense in the tumultuous arena, GC pulverized TCU as Dominador Juanito Jimenez led the team with impressive three goals in the finals game.

“Ginawa ko pong advantage ‘yon (tumult) para makapagpahinga. Binuhos ko po lahat [tapos] doon na po ako bumawi,” Jimenez said.

Furthermore, Lemuel John Mendez assisted Jimenez in a sleek-running game, adding two goals for the team.

Meanwhile, the top scorer of the event and the hailed MVP, Mon Ivan Mendoza, added a goal, making TCU far from winning.

Alexander Nash Alvizo and James Gabertan also braced their powers
label: %f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%93%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%94%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%97%9a

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%93%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%94%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8a%f0%9d%90%92%f0%9d%97%9a/]
oal, making TCU far from winning.

Alexander Nash Alvizo and James Gabertan also braced their powers, contributing their respective goals to make them dominant in the finals game.

In the end, the arena was filled with shouts, cheering for the whole team’s victorious finish in the 12th LCUAA.

# BEST MOBILE GAME AWARDS – LOLA’S LUTONG BAHAY

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, December 3, 2024**

BEST MOBILE GAME BAHAY

We are ecstatic to share with you that Lola’s Lutong Bahay recently won the BEST MOBILE GAME AWARD at the GameOn Awards last November 30, 2024 at Red Carpets Cinemas, Shangri-La Mall.

Another milestone for Lola’s Lutong Bahay before this year ends! Thank you to everyone who supported us from the start of development [FrogForge Ent](https://www.facebook.com/FrogForgeEnt?__cft__[0]=AZXU_EnUG63ia0ssiJngZ6fh8kA9C-zW5hEbfhN4NsiEAzruB-AjTg2c

# 𝐂𝐥𝐚𝐢𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐇𝐞𝐫 𝐇𝐚𝐥𝐟 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐒𝐤𝐲

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, March 31, 2024**

This Women’s Month, we celebrate the strong women who inspire and pave the way for others. The President of Gordon College, Professor Arlida Pame, exemplifies leadership and dedication.

Taking the helm in 2006, Prof. Pame has not only steered the college to new heights but has also made significant progress in empowering women within its walls.

Under her leadership, Gordon College has seen many milestones, but one that stands out is the establishment of the Gender and Developme
label: %f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ab-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9f-%f0%9d%90%a8

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ab-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9f-%f0%9d%90%a8/]
e has seen many milestones, but one that stands out is the establishment of the Gender and Development (GAD) Office headed by Dr. Abigail Legaspi, the GAD Office spearheads programs and training sessions focused on women’s empowerment throughout the year, culminating in grander activities during International Women’s Day.

Furthermore, she herself draws inspiration from a remarkable role model: Vice President Leni Robredo.

“Her hands-on approach and unwavering integrity are truly inspiring. The projects she spearheads to uplift the poor sectors of society are commendable,” the college preside
label: %f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ab-%f0%9d%90%87%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9f-%f0%9d%90%a8

 projects she spearheads to uplift the poor sectors of society are commendable,” the college president added as this admiration speaks volumes about her commitment to making a positive difference.

Additionally, when asked for a message for young women, the woman leader’s answer is clear and empowering: “Educate yourself, improve your skills, and explore the world. You can be what you want to be. Claim your right to choose. Half of the sky is yours, claim it,” she declares.

# Dr. Yusi: ‘The best is yet to come’

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [CHED](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ched/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 6, 2025**

In her address, Commission on Higher Education (CHED) Regional Office III Director IV Dr. Lora L. Yusi said Gordon College’s (GC) “much like a 26-year-old” at its exciting age—“strong, confident, and ready to conquer the world.”

“If Gordon College were a person, it would be old enough to rent a car, but still young enough to stay up all night binge-watching Netflix,” s
label: dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come/]
 be old enough to rent a car, but still young enough to stay up all night binge-watching Netflix,” she added.

This speech was after her attendance at the 26th Founding Anniversary celebration of GC at SMX Convention Center, SM Central, Olongapo City last February 25.

“Twenty-six is not just a number—it’s a story of how a dream once nurtured grew into a thriving institution, deeply rooted in the heart of Olongapo…a story of countless lives touched, dreams realized, and communities transformed,” the CHEDRO III director furthered.

Dr. Yusi also expressed her confidence in the institution’s cap
label: dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come/]
 the CHEDRO III director furthered.

Dr. Yusi also expressed her confidence in the institution’s capability to build its students’ futures, “Every graduate who has walked through its halls is a living proof of the institution’s impact. These graduates are like seeds scattered across the land” that while some of them grew their roots in the city and some flew far, they still carry with them the values of the institution.

Moreover, the director spoke of the educators, administrators, and stakeholders in their respective roles in uprooting its students.

“And today, we celebrate those seeds that
label: dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come/]
ders in their respective roles in uprooting its students.

“And today, we celebrate those seeds that have grown and flourished. But we also celebrate the gardeners who, with steady hands and dedicated hearts, nurtured those dreams into reality. Without you, these stories would never have taken flight,” she exclaimed.

She prompted, “Just like the journey of the last 26 years, the next chapter will not be without its challenges” and voiced her certainty that the institution understands this message and confidence in its commitment to its mission while being agile to change.

“That is no small f
label: dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-yusi-the-best-is-yet-to-come/]
ge and confidence in its commitment to its mission while being agile to change.

“That is no small feat.”

As she extended her gratitude and congratulations to the institution, “Cheers to the past 26 years, to the next 26 years, and the many years to come. May Gordon College continue to write even greater stories of transformation, inspiration, and impact,” she also wished the institution to continue to be the builder of dreams and light ways for more generations.

# A Magnitude 6.2 Earthquake Hit the South China Sea

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, June 15, 2023**

BREAKING NEWS: A magnitude 6.2 earthquake hit the South China Sea—42km from Calatagan, Batangas today June 15, 2023

Olongapo City experienced a 5 to 10 seconds of heavy shaking—forcing the students, visitors, and employees inside the Gordon College building to evacuate to the East Tapinac Oval Track.

The College Administrators eventually prompted everyone to leave the college grounds following the announcement of the cancelation of all activities and tran
label: a-magnitude-6-2-earthquake-hit-the-south-china-sea

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/a-magnitude-6-2-earthquake-hit-the-south-china-sea/]
o leave the college grounds following the announcement of the cancelation of all activities and transactions inside Gordon College.

The building is currently being inspected for any damages caused by the said earthquake.

Olongapo City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (OC-DRRMO) lines are open for any report or incident as result of the earthquake.

# MONTELAVA ON MS. GAY ZAMBALES 2023: “Negrang Tatatak at Tatalak”

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, November 27, 2023**

Third-year Bachelor of Education Major in English student Jian Patricia Montelava crowned as the Ms. Gay Zambales 2023 and sat to her throne last November 18, 2023, held at Palauig, Zambales.

Meanwhile, Montelava said in an interview that, in spite of the hesitations she felt, her grandmother motivated and encouraged her to join the said pageant. Her attempt to face the stage was to make her grandmother proud.

“Navigating the financial asp
label: montelava-on-ms-gay-zambales-2023-negrang-tatatak-at-tatalak

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/montelava-on-ms-gay-zambales-2023-negrang-tatatak-at-tatalak/]
ant. Her attempt to face the stage was to make her grandmother proud.

“Navigating the financial aspects of participating in this pageant proves challenging for me. As a student and the primary provider for my family, my resources are limited, posing a hurdle in my journey through this competition,” Montelava stated.

In line with this, she gave advice to aspiring Ms. Gay contestants that are from Gordon College: embrace your authenticity with confidence, let your unique brilliance shine, and also remember to share the beauty of your purpose and advocacy.

On the other hand, according to Monte
label: montelava-on-ms-gay-zambales-2023-negrang-tatatak-at-tatalak

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/montelava-on-ms-gay-zambales-2023-negrang-tatatak-at-tatalak/]
so remember to share the beauty of your purpose and advocacy.

On the other hand, according to Montelava, the goals of the Miss Gay Pageant in the Philippines are to spread love, happiness, and inspiration. “I firmly believe that it goes beyond mere enjoyment, possessing a purpose and advocacy dedicated to addressing various societal issues.”

Moreover, her majesty makes her name remarkable with a tagline “Negrang Tatatak at Tatalak.”

🏻: I-man Anthonie Klay Garcia | News Editor

: Angelique Jose | Graphics Editor

# BOT confirms Soriano’s Appointment as New College President

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, February 11, 2025**

Then Officer-In-Charge College President Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano took office as a Full-Fledged College President last Thursday, February 6.

On a Facebook post by the official Gordon College (GC) page, her appointment as the new president was reviewed by the Search Committee for the New Gordon College President and formally approved by the Board of Trustees (BOT) during the board meeting.

Th
label: bot-confirms-sorianos-appointment-as-new-college-president

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bot-confirms-sorianos-appointment-as-new-college-president/]
College President and formally approved by the Board of Trustees (BOT) during the board meeting.

The said meeting was attended by all BOT members and the confirmation was led by the BOT Chairperson, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., Committee on Education Chairperson, Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, and Director of the Commission on Higher Education Regional Office III (CHEDROIII), Dr. Lora L. Yusi.

Moreover, the now-confirmed-college president was the first president from a local university and college (LUC) in Region III to be appointed through CHED’s Search Committee for President (SCP) proce
label: bot-confirms-sorianos-appointment-as-new-college-president

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bot-confirms-sorianos-appointment-as-new-college-president/]
ollege (LUC) in Region III to be appointed through CHED’s Search Committee for President (SCP) process.

“We extend our heartfelt congratulations to Dr. Soriano and look forward to her visionary leadership in guiding Gordon College toward greater success and excellence,” the Facebook post also read.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
