# Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap: An Adopt-a-Learner Project Year 2 Wave 2

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, September 14, 2023**

𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞 𝐀𝐥𝐮𝐦𝐧𝐢 𝐀𝐬𝐬𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧, 𝐈𝐧𝐜. 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐝𝐥𝐲 𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐰𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐨𝐟 “𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐚𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐢𝐬𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐩: 𝐀𝐝𝐨𝐩𝐭 𝐚 𝐋𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐫 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐣𝐞𝐜𝐭.”

“Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap: Adopt a Learner Project” in San Narciso, Zambales, in collaboration with TGIS ( Thankful Givers; Inspired Servants).

Together, we are lighting up the path to a brighter future for these young minds by distributing essentia
label: isang-daang-pisong-pangarap-an-adopt-a-learner-project-year-2-wave-2

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/isang-daang-pisong-pangarap-an-adopt-a-learner-project-year-2-wave-2/]
her, we are lighting up the path to a brighter future for these young minds by distributing essential school supplies.

Leading this wonderful initiative are Eduardo B. Velasco, Jr., Chair of Sports, Special Events, and Programs, and our dedicated President, Michael P. Tuyay.

Let’s keep the spirit of giving alive!

# Singson reigns as Ms. LCUAA 2024, Barrera finishes 5th

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, October 24, 2024**

Gordon College’s bet, Francesca Singson donned the crown and Carl Wilkin Barrera grabbed 4th runner-up in the 13th Mr. and Ms. Local Colleges and Universities Athletics Association (LCUAA) 2024 at Ayala Malls Southpark, Muntinlupa City today.

In her winning answer, the LCUAA queen highlighted the importance of strength, resiliency, and passion in achieving one’s dream.

Meanwhile, GC also stood out as Singson and Barrera both took home the Best in S
label: singson-reigns-as-ms-lcuaa-2024-barrera-finishes-5th

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/singson-reigns-as-ms-lcuaa-2024-barrera-finishes-5th/]
eving one’s dream.

Meanwhile, GC also stood out as Singson and Barrera both took home the Best in Swimwear and Best in Formal Wear awards.

On the other hand, City College of Angeles was hailed as Mr. LCUAA 2024.

These are the complete list of winners:

MR. LCUAA

• John Michael Padua – CITY COLLEGE OF ANGELES – MR. LCUAA 2024

• Kyle Jan Badilla – KOLEHIYO NG LUNGSOD NG DASMARIÑAS (KLD) – 1st Runner-Up

• Marc Engel Santos – PATEROS TECHNOLOGICAL COLLEGE (PTC) – 2nd Runner-Up

• Raizan Tumbaga – CALACA CITY GLOBAL COLLEGE (CCGC) – 3rd Runner-Up

• Carl Wilkin Barrera – GORDON COLLEGE (GC) –

a – CALACA CITY GLOBAL COLLEGE (CCGC) – 3rd Runner-Up

• Carl Wilkin Barrera – GORDON COLLEGE (GC) – 4th Runner-Up

MS. LCUAA

• Francesca Singson – GORDON COLLEGE (GC) – Ms. LCUAA 2024

• Angelica Sarte – CITY COLLEGE OF ANGELES (CCGC) – 1st Runner-Up

• Ym Azuela – CITY COLLEGE OF SAN JOSE DEL MONTE (CCSJDM) – 2nd Runner-Up

• Maria Kisha Bustillo – TAGUIC CITY UNIVERSITY (TCU) – 3rd Runner-Up

• Shanne Panganiban – CALACA CITY GLOBAL COLLEGE (CCGC) – 4th Runner-Up

# Gordon College CCS Bags 2nd Runner Up

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, September 16, 2023**

On September 15, 2023, a significant moment unfolded at Holy Angel University in Angeles City, Pampanga. It was during this event that the Gordon College – College of Computer Studies (CCS) led by Kim Padua, Mark Thaddeus Manuel, Karl Bastian Lacap, and Adrian Gallano, demonstrated their exceptional skills and unwavering dedication in the highly competitive Hack4Gov competition. Under the experienced guidance of Mr. Loudel Manaloto and Mr. Chris Allen Pineda, they
label: gordon-college-ccs-bags-2nd-runner-up

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-ccs-bags-2nd-runner-up/]
competition. Under the experienced guidance of Mr. Loudel Manaloto and Mr. Chris Allen Pineda, they embarked on a journey to push the boundaries of innovation and problem-solving.

Their technical prowess and creativity were on full display as Gordon College CCS emerged as dominant forces, securing the prestigious position of 2ND RUNNER UP in the Hack4Gov competition. This remarkable achievement not only reflects their outstanding performance but also underscores their commitment to excellence.

The CCS Student Council extends its warmest congratulations to our aforementioned students for this
label: gordon-college-ccs-bags-2nd-runner-up

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-ccs-bags-2nd-runner-up/]

The CCS Student Council extends its warmest congratulations to our aforementioned students for this exceptional achievement. Their success not only brings pride to our college but also serves as an inspiration to current and future students, encouraging them to strive for excellence and innovation in their academic pursuits.

Illustration by: Lovelyn Perez | Vice President for Internal Affairs

# Grguric on Nationwide Earthquake Drill: “The faster the better.”

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, September 7, 2023**

Gordon College conducted an earthquake drill following the 3rd Nationwide Earthquake Drill held at East Tapinac Oval, Olongapo City.

The drill kicked off at exactly 2:00 PM with the students, admins, instructors, and other offices practicing the duck, cover, and hold as the initial response for the earthquake.

GCians were guided by the Civil Security and Service Unit (CSSU) personnel and instructors to evacuate accordingly to the Oval Tr
label: grguric-on-nationwide-earthquake-drill

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/grguric-on-nationwide-earthquake-drill/]
il Security and Service Unit (CSSU) personnel and instructors to evacuate accordingly to the Oval Track.

“The purpose of this drill is to capacitate not only the students but also all occupants, personnel, and clients for safety, especially since the building is up to six floors,” said Ms. Jerica B. Grguric, Head of the GC Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Unit.

Grguric also added that Olongapo City is one of the participants nationwide, headed by the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC).

As for the record, six minutes was the exact time of the drill
label: grguric-on-nationwide-earthquake-drill

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/grguric-on-nationwide-earthquake-drill/]
on and Management Council (NDRRMC).

As for the record, six minutes was the exact time of the drill from the alarm to the exit.

The earthquake drill is a quarterly exercise to prepare the institution for an immediate and accurate response to a natural disaster. The last drill is expected to happen in November.

& : Bealyn Demanalata | Associate Editor

# Gintong indayog pabalik sa Gapo

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 26, 2024**

“Excellent!” ang isang salitang mailalarawan ng kanilang Coach nang makamit ng GC Wariors ang Overall Champion sa Dance Sports nitong ika-13th LCUAA National Games, na ginanap sa Ayala Malls Southpark – Muntinlupa City.

Nagdamot lang naman ng spotlight sina Jieann Nicole E. Cahanding at Prinz Anjelo Franco R. Aranassa sa kategoryang Latin-American, daan upang angkinin ang ginto at maisama sa medal haul ng dalubhasaan.

“Our dedication sa pagsasanay and pagmamahal sa aming
label: gintong-indayog-pabalik-sa-gapo

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gintong-indayog-pabalik-sa-gapo/]
into at maisama sa medal haul ng dalubhasaan.

“Our dedication sa pagsasanay and pagmamahal sa aming talent ang naging susi sa pagkamit ng aming tagumpay,” saad ng mananayaw matapos maideklara na maiuuwi nila ang karangalan.

Samantala, hindi rin nagpatinag ang tambalang Renz Joshua T. Mariano at Angela D. Francisco nang araruhin ang pilak sa kategoryang Modern Standard.

“Through intense training, nag-aral po kami ng new movements, expressions, and techniques, and ofcourse, ang tiwala po namin sa isa’t isa,” boses ng buong grupo.

Dagdag pa nila, limitado lamang ang oras nang kanilang paghaha
label: gintong-indayog-pabalik-sa-gapo

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gintong-indayog-pabalik-sa-gapo/]
sa isa’t isa,” boses ng buong grupo.

Dagdag pa nila, limitado lamang ang oras nang kanilang paghahanda, ngunit hindi sila nagpatinag upang magkaroon ng bagong bihis ang routine at magtarak ng oras sa pagsasanay upang gumawa ng matingkad na impresyon, at manatiling “center of attraction.”

Makasaysayan ang pagbabalik sa Dance Floor dahil naiukit din noong 12th LCUAA National Games ang Overall Champion sa hatawan.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

: John Patrick Mateo | Managing Editor

:Emar Aranda | Opinion-Editorial Correspondent

# TFF conducts Ceremonial Turnover

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, August 16, 2023**

The official student publication of Gordon College, The Forefront, held its first-ever Ceremonial Turnover, yesterday, in line with the opening of classes for the academic year 2023-2024.

The said event was hosted by John Christian Rocero and Mitch Kyvin Bayno, both Radio Broadcaster of the publication.

Also, the initiative was attended by the incoming editorial board and staffers, along with the executive committee last academic year, particularly, the outgoing Editor-
label: tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover/]
 staffers, along with the executive committee last academic year, particularly, the outgoing Editor-in-Chief (EIC), Mr. Reggie Boy Vargas, and Mr. Earvin Jon Arsua, Managing Editor and former EIC of the publication.

Moreover, the publication’s esteemed adviser, Mr. Guiller Marila showed his support for the said initiative, along with Dr. Imelda DP Soriano, the College Administrator, and Ms. Lovelyn P. Ceralde, Vice President for Student Welfare and Services, who extended their best wishes and special message and to the member of The Forefront who will take the lead this academic year.

Meanwh
label: tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover/]
pecial message and to the member of The Forefront who will take the lead this academic year.

Meanwhile, the recognition of the hard work of the editorial board and staffers for the academic year 2022-2023 was also conducted during the turnover ceremony.

On the other hand, the passing of pencil led by the outgoing EIC, Mr. Vargas, and accepted by the new EIC, Mr. John Ian G. Marquez, marks the beginning of a new journey and responsibility for student journalists as the new editorial board and staffers took the Journalist’s Creed as their oath of fulfilling the responsibilities of continuously
label: tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tff-conducts-ceremonial-turnover/]
taffers took the Journalist’s Creed as their oath of fulfilling the responsibilities of continuously informing and empowering the masses.

The event was concluded through the closing remarks delivered by Mr. Marquez, extending his gratitude and message of motivation to this year’s members of The Forefront.

: Alejandrian Rodavia | Photojournalist

# GC Taekwondo Team rules LCUAA 2023: Uy, Santos Grab Golds

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 13, 2023**

𝗟𝗔𝗚𝗨𝗡𝗔, 𝗣𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗽𝗽𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀— 𝙁𝙊𝙐𝙂𝙃𝙏 𝙁𝙊𝙍 𝙂𝙇𝙊𝙍𝙔.

Battling with big names in the Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA), the Gordon College (GC) Taekwondo Team sealed the 12th LCUAA National Games with a victorious finish, collecting medals for the honor of GC on October 10 at Gov. Felicisimo T. San Luis Memorial National High School Covered Court.

Jayben Lancelot Uy conquered the ring, causing pity to his opponents and securing his sec
label: gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds/]
ed Court.

Jayben Lancelot Uy conquered the ring, causing pity to his opponents and securing his second gold medal in LCUAA.

This is the fourth time that Uy is competing in the LCUAA National Games, and he described his gold medal finish in Kyorugi Light Senior Men this year as his redemption from his bronze finish last season.

“Iba po ‘yung enjoynment at excitement ko this season. Nag-gold na po ako dati tapos [naging] bronze nung last, kaya po iba po ‘yung enjyonment ko this season,” Uy said.

Meanwhile, Nathania Santos’ supremacy in the women’s division is evident as she clinched the Kyor
label: gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds/]
.

Meanwhile, Nathania Santos’ supremacy in the women’s division is evident as she clinched the Kyorugi Light Senior Women gold medal and became the Most Valuable Player of the Taekwondo Senior Women.

“Sobrang saya, training at positive vibes lang talaga kahit maraming negativities, go pa rin,” said Santos.

Santos won over her opponent from Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa, in a sudden death round.

On the other hand, Beasia Lourdes Morrison, the GC Taekwondo Team’s coach, expressed his pride in having her players heading into this year’s LCUAA.

“I feel pride sa mga players ko kasi I kno
label: gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds/]
de in having her players heading into this year’s LCUAA.

“I feel pride sa mga players ko kasi I know they did their best. And as a coach, masaya ‘yun sa feeling na nakita mo ‘yung mga players [mong] ginawa nila lahat para manalo, they did a good fight,” said Coach Morrison.

Moreover, John Vincent Manaluz in the Fly Senior Men Division, John Symon Punsaran in the Senior Men Bantam Division, Vanjoe Santos in the Feather Senior Men Division, and Brian Villanueva in the Welter Senior Men Division snatched bronzes in their respective divisions.

Furthermore, Unica Angela Hayah Fadiri secured the
label: gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-taekwondo-team-rules-lcuaa-2023-uy-santos-grab-golds/]
snatched bronzes in their respective divisions.

Furthermore, Unica Angela Hayah Fadiri secured the silver medal in Feather Senior Women Division; Andrea Divino in Fin Senior Women Division, Gedily Barrientos in Fly Senior Women Division, and Chrishelle Mae Agustin in Bantam Senior Women Division bagged bronzes.

# NLE 2023: 100 out of 110 pass; Fabian ranks 10

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, June 11, 2023**

It is a continuous good performance for Gordon College (GC), as 100 out of 110 passed the May 2023 Philippine Nursing Licensure Examination (NLE 2023).

Moreover, James Ross Fabian ranked 10 out of 10,764 (74.94%) passers.

Fabian made a commendable performance as he passed with 89.60%.

Overall, GC outperformed the national passing rate with 90.91%.

# Tagumpay sa dulo ng karera

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, October 27, 2024**

Sumelyo ng tatlong ginto, dalawang pilak at apat na tanso ang Gordon College (GC) athletics team sa katatapos lamang na 13th Local Colleges and Universities Athletics Association National Games nitong ika-20 hanggang ika-26 ng Oktubre sa Muntinlupa City.

Nagkamit ng siyam na medalya sa kabuuan ang mga atleta mula sa GC na naging daan upang masungkit ng koponan ang 3rd place sa overall ranking sa naturang larangan sa kabila ng pagkansela ng ilan pang athletics event dahil sa sama
label: tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera/]
rall ranking sa naturang larangan sa kabila ng pagkansela ng ilan pang athletics event dahil sa sama ng panahon.

Nag-uwi ng gintong medalya ang mga atleta na sina JB John Piodo para sa 5km run, Benedict Maarat sa 100m at John Paul Punzal para naman sa shotput event.

Samantala, napasakamay nina Adeline Padilla sa javelin throw at Gilbert Ebilane sa discus throw ang pilak na medalya.

Nasungkit naman ang tansong medalya nina Mica Faustino sa 5k run, Jeian Espiritu sa 400m at 800m run, Sophia Valencia para sa 400m run at Adeline Padilla sa discus throw.

Puspusan ang naging paghahanda ng kopona
label: tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera/]
encia para sa 400m run at Adeline Padilla sa discus throw.

Puspusan ang naging paghahanda ng koponan ayon kay GC Athletics Coach Roy De Vera pagkatapos pa lang ng sportsfest, “Ilang days lang, nagbalik agad kami sa training, as I always say to my athletes, this is just the beginning. We need to prepare [for] something more challenging, the [LCUAA] National Games.”

Bukod sa kanilang paghahanda, binigyang diin din ni De Vera ang pagkakaroon ng “disiplina sa lahat ng bagay” ng kaniyang mga atleta na isa sa mga naging susi sa tagumpay ng koponan sa athletics events ng LCUAA.

“Overall, masaya ak
label: tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tagumpay-sa-dulo-ng-karera/]
 na isa sa mga naging susi sa tagumpay ng koponan sa athletics events ng LCUAA.

“Overall, masaya ako sa naging result and I know to myself that GC athletics has more to offer [sa] next LCUAA 2025,” aniya.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

: Stephanie O. Estrella | Opinion-Editorial Correspondent

: John Christian Rocero | Broadcaster

# PROUD TO BE AN OLONGAPEÑO AND PROUD TO BE A GCIAN

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, May 1, 2023**

The week-long celebration of Zambales’ Dinamulag Festival ended with the Binibining Zambales Coronation Night.  
  
Due to the strong heavy rain, the organizers moved the program to 11:00 P.M. from the original plan of 8:00 P.M. Despite this, they managed to proclaim the new queen of Zambales.

Candidate #19 Christal Jean J. Dela Cruz, Miss Olongapo, is a 1st year Bachelor of Arts in Communications (BACOMM) student at Gordon College and was crowned Binibining Za
label: proud-to-be-an-olongapeno-and-proud-to-be-a-gcian

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/proud-to-be-an-olongapeno-and-proud-to-be-a-gcian/]
 Bachelor of Arts in Communications (BACOMM) student at Gordon College and was crowned Binibining Zambales 2023. She will bring home a crown, a sash, and a PHP 200,000 cash prize.  
  
3rd year student Bachelor of Arts in Communications (BACOMM) at Gordon College, candidate #6, Gianne Pearl A. Leuterio, Miss Olongapo, also joined the said competition.  
  
The newly crowned Binibining Zambales will represent the province for the upcoming National Beauty Pageant.

# PADYAK GC 2.0

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, March 3, 2023**

A life with time doesn’t have a brake and could be challenging, just like unceasingly riding a bicycle with two wheels. Nevertheless, there’s always a route to success.

PADYAK GC 2.0 has finally happened earlier today, February 19th, for the second time, as it is part of the GC Founding Anniversary celebration this year. The said event was organized and operated by the officers of the GC-Supreme Student Council as well as the Alumni Associat
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
anized and operated by the officers of the GC-Supreme Student Council as well as the Alumni Association of Gordon College (GCAAI). Also, a big thanks to all the sponsors who made this event more special and exciting.

In an interview with the Forefront, GC-SSC Chairman Richelle Joy Galaraga said that they aim to advocate for sustainability within the campus. Last year’s PADYAK project was participated in by numerous cyclists, which urged them to continue the project and promote eco-friendliness through cycling. “We aim for this project to promote sustainability within our school since alam nam
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
through cycling. “We aim for this project to promote sustainability within our school since alam naman po natin, from the previous project of PADYAK, maraming mga students ang nag participate, so we want to continue promoting sustainability inside our school and to promote a healthy or an environmentally friendly way of transportation papunta po sa institution natin,” she said. Galaraga emphasized that they also aim to support the project of GCSSC, which is the Gordon College Bike Park. Galaraga said that the event is inclusive of all cyclists in Olongapo City and is free of registration. “Ope
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
 said that the event is inclusive of all cyclists in Olongapo City and is free of registration. “Open po ang PADYAK sa lahat ng rider or bikers po sa Olongapo City basta willing po sila to support the agenda of this activity, and it is also free of registration. “We are promoting a donation drive through this activity,” she added.

The cyclists put in a lot of effort to pedal, showing their participation and enjoyment all throughout their ride. Along with it, they managed to go to their routes, from Gordon College to SM through 10th Street, Magsaysay Drive, Rizal Avenue straight to Ulo ng Apo
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
from Gordon College to SM through 10th Street, Magsaysay Drive, Rizal Avenue straight to Ulo ng Apo Rotonda, then Mabayuan via Mabayuan Bridge, straight to Balic Balic, turning left to Tabacuhan, and turning right to Memorial. Going back, they again passed Rizal Avenue, Ulo ng Apo, left to St. Joseph, and then finally Harris to GC.

Thankfully, no harm or accidents happened during their ride trip as marshals guided and protected them.

What was even more exciting about this event was that there were prepared raffles and awards for all potential winners, including Ganda and Poging Siklista.  
M
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
were prepared raffles and awards for all potential winners, including Ganda and Poging Siklista.  
Maan Rivera, a participant who won the Gandang Siklista Award, said she joined the event to encourage the younger generation to indulge themselves in cycling. Rivera felt delighted since the cycling event gave her the opportunity to meet the cyclists around Olongapo City. “Supportive kami sa mga ganitong events. Maglolook forward tayo sa mga ganiyang mga na events na dadating pa,” she added. Meanwhile, Nathaniel Alta, a participant who won the Poging Siklista Award, said he enjoyed the event. He
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
ile, Nathaniel Alta, a participant who won the Poging Siklista Award, said he enjoyed the event. He said that winning the award was unexpected, although he felt honored. He is also looking forward to the event in the future.

Additionally, Galaraga is also looking forward to the event and is optimistic about having more cyclists participate in the succeeding years. “We hope for this to continue po sa mga next year po ng anniversary ng institution natin, so hopefully, since we are on the second year, hindi na siya mag stop at magtuloy-tuloy and mas mag grow po siya into [having] more participan
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
ear, hindi na siya mag stop at magtuloy-tuloy and mas mag grow po siya into [having] more participants. We also aim to collaborate with different municipalities po na malapit saatin sa Olongapo,” Galaraga said. Nicole B. Marcial, PIO of GCAAI, said, “This year’s Cycling Motorcade has been a great representation of our community’s passion for sustainable transportation and the active lifestyle that cycling promotes. “It is a reminder that we are not the only individuals who are part of a larger community working toward a common goal.”

Besides, Olongapo City Mayor Rolen Paulino Jr. and Olongapo
label: padyak-gc-2-0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/padyak-gc-2-0/]
ommunity working toward a common goal.”

Besides, Olongapo City Mayor Rolen Paulino Jr. and Olongapo City Councilor Tata Paulino were present to give their support for the PADYAK event.

✍️ : Desiree Capitulo | Features Correspondent  
: Isiah Aquino | News Correspondent

# EARTHQUAKE DRILL AT GORDON COLLEGE

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 16, 2023**

Today, Gordon College thru its GC DRRMU Commmitte-Members conducted an earthquake drill under the monitoring and observation of the Olongapo City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to prepare students, faculty, and admin personnel for potential earthquakes and other natural disasters.

The drill began at 2:00 PM. During the drill, personnel and students demonstrated “Duck, Cover, and Hold” and evacuated from the school building when the shaking stops.

The objectiv
label: earthquake-drill-at-gordon-college

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/earthquake-drill-at-gordon-college/]
“Duck, Cover, and Hold” and evacuated from the school building when the shaking stops.

The objective of the drill is to ensure that all personnel and students are aware of the proper protocols in the event of an earthquake or other emergency situation.

Overall, the earthquake drill was deemed a success by organizers and participants alike.

With continued practice and preparedness, the hope is that the Gordon College community will be equipped to handle any emergency that may possibly occur.

# GC Honors Excellence at 2025 Awards and Recognition Ceremony

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 6, 2025**

OLONGAPO CITY—Gordon College (GC) celebrated the dedication and achievements of its faculty and staff at the 2025 Awards and Recognition Ceremony held last February 22.

The event opened with an invocation, followed by the Philippine National Anthem and the Gordon College Hymn.

Gordon College President Dr. Imelda P. Sorianodelivered an inspiring opening message, emphasizing the institution’s c
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony/]
esident Dr. Imelda P. Sorianodelivered an inspiring opening message, emphasizing the institution’s commitment to academic excellence and service.

“Binabati ko lahat ng ating pararangalan, ang inyong sipag at sakripisyo ay patuloy na nagbibigay inspirasyon upang ipagpatuloy natin ang ating misyon—ang paghubog ng isang dekalidad at mahusay na mag aaral” she stated.

She also expressed that this day should stand as a reminder of the spirit of unity at GC, an institution that not only strives for academic excellence but also aims to use that knowledge to bring meaningful change in the lives of Ol
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

content:

cademic excellence but also aims to use that knowledge to bring meaningful change in the lives of Olongapo’s students.

Hon. Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., Chairperson of the Board of Trustees and City Mayor of Olongapo, received an exemplary leadership award and graced the event with a special message, recognizing the invaluable contributions of the faculty and staff.

“Ang lifeblood po ng Gordon College ay ang mga teachers natin, nakuha natin ang recognition ng Gordon College, ‘yung record sa lahat ng Boards, dahil sa inyo, dahil sa faculty, sa mga teaching staff natin at s’yempre dahil sa non-
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

content:

at ng Boards, dahil sa inyo, dahil sa faculty, sa mga teaching staff natin at s’yempre dahil sa non-teaching (staff)” Paulino added.

Among the highlights of the ceremony was the awarding of service milestones, acknowledging individuals who have dedicated 5, 10, 20, and 25 years of service to the institution.

For five years of service, the awardees were:

Ms. Sheena T. Bolaños

Ms. Sherrine Angel Malong

Ms. Patricia Anne A. Paras

Reaching ten years of service, the honorees include Ms. Evangeline R. Incocencio and Mr. Ronnie D. Luy.

Meanwhile, those who have dedicated fifteen years to the i
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

ine R. Incocencio and Mr. Ronnie D. Luy.

Mr. Jason M. Bagason

Ms. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza

Ms. Anne Mayelle P. Nieves

Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr.

Mr. Virgilio M. Managbanag was among the recipients of the 15 Years of Service Award who unfortunately passed away before the ceremony. But to his honor, the institution played a tribute video, celebrating his contributions and remembering his lasting impact on the community.

The notable honorees included Ms. Catherine M. Mobo, who served the institution for20 years; and Mr.
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

On the other hand, the Career Development Awards recognized 32 faculty and staff members for their professional growth and contributions.

Added to this, the Honesto Awards honored individuals for their integrity and commitment, including Mr. Ranny C. Amployo, Mr. Ervin B. Andrade, and Mr. Joseph A. Bacla.

Recognizing exceptional performance, Mr. Vince Richard Q. Gomez received the Outstanding
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

 A. Bacla.

Recognizing exceptional performance, Mr. Vince Richard Q. Gomez received the Outstanding Performance Faculty Award, while Cornelio V. Sobero was named Outstanding Administrative Personnel.

Meanwhile, the Outstanding Department/Unit Awards were presented to the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM), the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA), and the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS), highlighting their excellence in academic and administrative contributions.

The event concluded with closing remarks from Dr. Erlinda Casela-Abarintos, Vice President for Admi
label: gc-honors-excellence-at-2025-awards-and-recognition-ceremony

“Maraming maraming salamat po sa inyong lahat for trusting our institution to be your source of energy, to be your source of inspiration, to be your source of motivation and to be your service to our community,”she concluded.

# GC Hosts Graduate Studies Academic Convocation

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, December 7, 2024**

Gordon College held a Graduate Studies Academic Convocation on December 7, 2024, with the theme “Crossing Borders in Graduate Education.” The event was organized by the Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs Office, through its International Affairs Unit, in collaboration with the Institute of Graduate Studies. Hosted via Zoom, the convocation brought together academic leaders, faculty, and both undergraduate and graduate students to engag
label: gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation/]
on brought together academic leaders, faculty, and both undergraduate and graduate students to engage in meaningful discussions on the evolving landscape of graduate education.

The event began with a welcoming remark from College President, Dr. Imelda Soriano, who emphasized the importance of pushing academic boundaries to create meaningful change in education. Her speech set the tone for the day, highlighting the critical role that innovation and collaboration play in shaping the future of graduate studies.

Following Dr. Soriano’s remarks, the convocation featured a lecture by Dr. Aida Hani
label: gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation/]
duate studies.

Following Dr. Soriano’s remarks, the convocation featured a lecture by Dr. Aida Hanim A. Hamid, Senior Lecturer in Educational Management at the National University of Malaysia . Dr. Hamid’s presentation focused on the importance of crossing geographic, disciplinary, institutional, and cultural borders in higher education. She highlighted how these efforts foster collaboration, inclusivity, and innovation in academic settings, inspiring attendees to think beyond traditional boundaries in their own work.

The lecture was followed by an open forum, providing attendees the opportu
label: gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation/]
aries in their own work.

The lecture was followed by an open forum, providing attendees the opportunity to engage directly with Dr. Hamid. Participants asked insightful questions and shared ideas, deepening the conversation on how graduate education can address global challenges and create positive change.

As the event ended, Prof. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Vice President for Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs, delivered the closing remarks. She shared the commitment of the institution for future internationalization engagements through cross-border collaboration and con
label: gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-hosts-graduate-studies-academic-convocation/]
e institution for future internationalization engagements through cross-border collaboration and continuous learning.

The convocation concluded with the distribution of certificates of participation and a photo opportunity, marking the successful gathering of scholars committed to advancing graduate education.

# A Whiplash of Victory: Men and women’s futsal team soar high

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, October 31, 2024**

With grit and glory, GC is at its peak.

The Gordon College (GC) futsal team kicked their way for the gold after the men kept their crown as back-to-back champions while the women earned their golden title from last year’s bronze in the 13th LCUAA National Games held at Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa (PLMun) covered court last Tuesday, October 22.

As the timer struck, contagious roars of energy filled the covered court between the GC men’
label: a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high/]
er 22.

As the timer struck, contagious roars of energy filled the covered court between the GC men’s team and PLMun who battled for gold, but the green warriors kept their net secured and finished the game with a score of 2-1, making the defending champions shut down their rival’s hopes of winning for the second time since last year’s finals game, all even in the absence of one of the team’s pillar and MVP in the previous year, Mon Ivan Mendoza.

The golden crown was then followed by John Eric Martin, who also bagged the best goalkeeper award for the men’s category, while Alexander Nash Alviz
label: a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high/]
Martin, who also bagged the best goalkeeper award for the men’s category, while Alexander Nash Alvizo took the MVP award with a total of 11 goals from their six games.

The men’s MVP expressed his gratitude for receiving the title since it was out of his expectations, “Sa totoo lang unexpected po talaga ‘yong special award sa’kin na MVP dahil ang goal ko lang po talaga is ma-defend namin ‘yung pagiging champion.”

On the other hand, the women’s team prevailed over Kolehiyo ng Lungsod ng Lipa (KLL) in the final game of the competition with a 1-1 tie, GC however had the upper hand with a 3-0 sta
label: a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high/]
L) in the final game of the competition with a 1-1 tie, GC however had the upper hand with a 3-0 standing prior to the finals, while KLL had 2-1, making GC women take the victory.

Aside from the championship title, the women’s team received special awards with Odeza Marie Yap being the best goalkeeper, while Therese Frances Lamigos was awarded the MVP after a flawless run in the tournament.

Despite the short preparation time and a massive lineup adjustment, the men’s futsal team managed to maintain their momentum and remain undefeated.

“Sobrang hirap and sobrang sarap sa feeling na kinaya n
label: a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/a-whiplash-of-victory-men-and-womens-futsal-team-soar-high/]
ntain their momentum and remain undefeated.

“Sobrang hirap and sobrang sarap sa feeling na kinaya namin, basta sinabi ko lang sa team na hindi namin puwedeng biguin lahat ng nag-e-expect ng gold para sa’min, lalo na name ng school ‘yong dala namin,” the men’s team captain JD Jimenez stated.

Anticipation builds for next year’s event as Gordon College celebrates its championship victory. A more thrilling prospect is yet to come, followed by a stronger-than-ever set of rosters.

3. Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration

4. Bachelor of Science in Business Administration, Major in Financial Management

5. Bachelor of Science in Business Administration, Major in Human Resource Management

This prestigious recognition was formally awarded to the Gordon College Accreditation Team by Dr. Raymundo P. Arcega, President of the Association of Local Colleges and Universities (ALCU) and Commission on Accreditation for
label: today-we-are-celebrating-excellence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/today-we-are-celebrating-excellence/]
ent of the Association of Local Colleges and Universities (ALCU) and Commission on Accreditation for Local Colleges and Universities (ALCUCOA). It is a testament to our institution’s commitment to academic excellence, innovation, and continuous growth.

We extend our deepest gratitude to Olongapo City Mayor, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino, Committee on Education Chairperson and Olongapo City Councilor, Hon. Cristabelle Marie Paulino, Commission on Higher Education Regional Office III Director, Dr. Lora L. Yusi, Director IV, the Board of Trustees, the Executive Council Members, College Officials, Manage
label: today-we-are-celebrating-excellence

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/today-we-are-celebrating-excellence/]
. Yusi, Director IV, the Board of Trustees, the Executive Council Members, College Officials, Management Council Members, Faculty, Students, Alumni, Administrative Personnel, and all stakeholders who made this historic achievement possible.

This success belongs to everyone—a victory for the entire Gordon College community! Together, we soar higher!

Congratulations to all!

# Badminton Singles B event, pinagharian ng GC

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 11, 2023**

Nagmistulang “scoring machine” ang dalawang pamato ng Gordon College Men’s at Women’s badminton team matapos ikadena ang dalawang gintong medalya sa naganap na championship match ng Badminton Single B event, ika-10 ng Oktubre.

Bitbit ang tapang at bilis, naungusan ni Rose Ann Mesias ang katunggali mula sa Taguig City University upang tawirin ang unang set ng laro sa iskor na 22-20.

Nagpaulan naman ng magkakasunod na power hit ang TCU dahilan para silatin an
label: %f0%9d%98%bd%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%99%f0%9d%99%a2%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%98%bd%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%99%f0%9d%99%a2%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8/]
aro sa iskor na 22-20.

Nagpaulan naman ng magkakasunod na power hit ang TCU dahilan para silatin ang lamang sa kalahating oras ng laro, ngunit kaagad na inapura ni Mesias ang laban at mabilis na sinelyuhan ang tiket patungo sa ginto, 21-12.

Sa kasabay na oras, sinalanta naman ni Glenn Ian Amancio ang hari ng Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa matapos itong magbitaw ng malalakas na smash at nakalilitong drop upang dagitin ang unang set ng laro sa Men’s division.

Umangkin pa rin ng magkakasunod na puntos si Amancio upang diktahan ang kalaban at agawin ang bentahe ng laro sa ikalawang set dah
label: %f0%9d%98%bd%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%99%f0%9d%99%a2%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%98%bd%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%99%f0%9d%99%a2%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9e%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8/]
d na puntos si Amancio upang diktahan ang kalaban at agawin ang bentahe ng laro sa ikalawang set dahilan para makamit ang kaniyang inaasam na ginto.

“Puro training po ang ginawa naming paghahanda at nanalangin. Naging inspirasyon ko rin po ‘yung teammate ko na dapat siya ang lalaro sa Single B women’s division, pero hindi natuloy dahil sa health emergency”, ani Mesias matapos ang laban.

# ALCUAA conducts Solidarity Meeting: GC eyes on bringing glory to the institution

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, October 8, 2023**

As the 12th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) 2023 kicks off, the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (ALCUAA) held a Solidarity Meeting with coordinators, coaches, and officials from 22 local colleges around the country at Laguna Sports Complex Gymnasium, Laguna University.

Meanwhile, Gordon College (GC) is steadfast in bringing pride and glory to the institution.

Mr
label: alcuaa-conducts-solidarity-meeting-gc-eyes-on-bringing-glory-to-the-institution

content:

ty.

Meanwhile, Gordon College (GC) is steadfast in bringing pride and glory to the institution.

Mr. Joel A. Duclayan, the Sports Development Unit Coordinator of GC and the current Vice Chairperson of the National Board of LCUAA, said that the players and athletes are in the momentum of representing the institution well.

“With our [teams], confident ako. Ine-aim natin sa mga players natin [to] enjoy, just enjoy the game,” said Duclayan.

After the solidarity meeting, the Mass has been conducted in lieu of peaceful games and sports events.

# Gordon College goes 25th

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, February 20, 2024**

East Tapinac – Gordon College marks the beginning of the institution’s week-long celebration of the 25th Founding anniversary with an opening ceremony.

The ceremony started with a performance from Olongapo City Marching Band followed by the Flash Mob performed of Gordon College Performing Arts Student Ensemble (Pase Ballo) and an invocation led by Pase Voci.

Prof. Arlida Pame, College President of Gordon College gave an opening remarks by acknowledging different college and cit
label: gordon-college-goes-25th

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-goes-25th/]
llege President of Gordon College gave an opening remarks by acknowledging different college and city officials who are part of the Institution’s success.

Meanwhile, Dr. Erlinda G. Crispin, Gordon College Consultant and former Olongapo City Councilor gives an inspirational message and talks about how Gordon College was established from hundreds of students before to thousand enrollees up to this day.

On the other hand, the Gordon College Performing Arts Student Ensemble, Pase Teatro unveils the GC Warrior, a performance giving life to the Alamat ng Ulo ng Apo with addition to a story of the
label: gordon-college-goes-25th

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-goes-25th/]
e GC Warrior, a performance giving life to the Alamat ng Ulo ng Apo with addition to a story of the hardships of Gordon College students and their challenges for the next years.

Also, Atty. Rolen C. Paulino, City Mayor and Chairman of Gordon College board of trustees along with Cristabelle C. Paulino, City Councilor and member of board of trustees, was also present at the ceremony to congratulate the institution on it’s 25th Anniversary.

Lastly, Dr. Abigail C. Legaspi lead the unveiling of Gordon College Building Marker with Mayor Paulino.

: Carlos Agustin | News Correspondent

: John Patri

# Amidst the force of nature: GCian performers owned the stage of GC Got Talent

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, February 27, 2025**

GCians sure know how to end the first day on a high note as 14 performers from different programs showcased their spectacular performance in the GC Got Talent on Monday, February 24 at the East Tapinac Oval Grounds.

This journey was concluded with a sweet victory of the following:

Jade Marie B. Cayosa claimed the championship title by showcasing mastery with “Sino Ang Bal
label: amidst-the-force-of-nature-gcian-performers-owned-the-stage-of-gc-got-talent

owing:

Jade Marie B. Cayosa claimed the championship title by showcasing mastery with “Sino Ang Baliw” by Kuh Ledesma. The soulful singer is also known to be a former contestant of Showtime’s segment, Tawag ng Tanghalan.

Florence May A. Mendoza brought a heartwarming performance that touched the hearts of the crowd with “Ako Naman Muna” by Angela Ken, grabbing a sweet spot for second runner-up.

And Soichi Castillo Tanaka beatboxed his way to the crowd that podium-finished as this year’s first runner-up.

This segment is still in line with the celebration of the Gordon College’s 26th Foundin

**Monday, December 9, 2024**

This year’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Unit organized an orientation on Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) awareness with the aim of ending the stigma associated with HIV and also serving as an awareness among student leaders held at GC Function Hall, December 3rd.

Such orientation is in line with the recently concluded World AIDS Day celebrated every first day of December.

James L recorded it. Gordon Memorial Hospital (JLGMH) is the 481 number of Persons Living with
label: ending-the-stigma-of-red-ribbon

ber.

James L recorded it. Gordon Memorial Hospital (JLGMH) is the 481 number of Persons Living with HIV (PLHIV) in Olongapo City including 48 who have died and 404 of the number are the only ones registered for Antiretroviral Therapy (ART).

However, resource speaker Mr. Jeffrey T also shed some light. Rodeo, RN, besides awareness, can also reduce the spread of such a virus by using pre-exposure prophylaxis.

According to the recorded data of the Department of Health (DOH) in September year 2024 there are 131,335 laboratory-confirmed HIV cases all over the Philippines and 88,544 are currently

On the other hand, the National Capital Region (NCR) tops the list with the most active cases of HIV cases followed by CALABARZON while Central Luzon ranked third.

Meanwhile, Gordon College had free HIV testing and free consultation for those who wish to undertake PreP on December 4 as part of the Regional HIV Testing Month held every last month of the year.

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, October 31, 2024**

LCUAA 2024 was a different story to the GC men’s volleyball team—“Masakit sa ’min ‘yong nangyari.”

After sustaining two losses in bracket B, the Gordon College (GC) Warriors ran short in the men’s volleyball tournament of the 13th Local Colleges and Universities Athletics Association (LCUAA) National Games at Muntinlupa City last October 20-26.

“‘Yong pagiging consistent sa loob ng court, ang daming lapses pagdating sa floor defense and service,” sa
label: gc-mens-volley-team-vows-to-reclaim-tourneys-podium

 pagiging consistent sa loob ng court, ang daming lapses pagdating sa floor defense and service,” said last year’s Best Middle Blocker Mar Angelo De Lara on the men’s challenges in their stunt in the tournament.

“At saka ‘yong eagerness and [willingness] manalo,” the middle blocker added.

Last year, GC podiumed in the men’s volleyball tournament after a silver finish to tick a mark for the first time in the medal tally since the institution’s participation in the LCUAA National Games.

Moreover, the 6’4” athlete underscored that it was a bitter experience for the Warriors but also a learning
label: gc-mens-volley-team-vows-to-reclaim-tourneys-podium

r, the 6’4” athlete underscored that it was a bitter experience for the Warriors but also a learning one for them, “Hindi dapat makampante.”

Now, the team shifts their focus to resharpening their swords and mending their lapses on the court through stricter training, eyeing a hungrier and stronger bounce back next LCUAA year.

“Lahat naman nakukuha sa ensayo. Magpe-prepare kami nang mas maaga para mapunan ‘yong lapses namin sa loob ng court and come back stronger ne

# GC Strengthens its Academic Community

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, January 22, 2024**

City College of San Jose Del Monte (CCSJDM) benchmarks Gordon College (GC) led by Dr. Roger S. Ambida, College President of CCSJDM, and Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College Administrator and Quality Assurance for Accreditation Director of GC this January 19.

In her welcoming remarks, Dr. Soriano expressed the college’s eagerness to collaborate with CCSJDM in achieving accreditation success.

“Welcome, and hopefully, we can help you in your endeavor for accreditation. We ar
label: gc-strengthens-its-academic-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-strengthens-its-academic-community/]
itation success.

“Welcome, and hopefully, we can help you in your endeavor for accreditation. We are very much willing to lend a hand for the success of your accreditation because we are one in Region 3, and our goal is for you to have the accreditation,” the GC College Administrator revealed.

Furthermore, Dr. Ambida emphasized the significance of choosing Gordon College as their benchmark. “Why Gordon College as our first benchmark? Because I believe in how you do things here. History tells us you have track records, you have credibility. Salute to your programs some of your programs are at
label: gc-strengthens-its-academic-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-strengthens-its-academic-community/]
s you have track records, you have credibility. Salute to your programs some of your programs are at level 3, and I can’t keep my envy to you,” the CCSJDM College President noted.

Moreover, he sees GC as a model institution and expresses the desire to know more about its best practices and enunciates that the experience of the institution would be vital as they continue to march in their quest for living to the mantra that excellence should be the way of life.

This benchmarking activity signifies a commitment to knowledge exchange and continuous improvement within the academic community.

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, April 5, 2024**

Olongapo City– Gordon College started its second day of accreditation as the GC Community Extension Services Unit and Association of Local Colleges and University Commission on Accreditation (ALCU-COA) conducted a site visit at Sitio Mampueng, Old Cabalan on April 4, 2023 to observe and assess the community.

The site visit was headed by the Community Extension Services Unit of Gordon College along with faculty members of CEAS,
label: %f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%8b%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%94-%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%80-%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%94%f0%9d%90%92-%f0%9d%90%93%f0%9d%90%8e%f0%9d%90%94

Along with this, the BSEd-SOC and BACOMM students conducted a literacy activity by facilitating the Alpabasa to the children of Mampueng and prepared a mini-game for them to enjoy. Also, the community of Mampueng gave a dance performance of their tribe to the

e for them to enjoy. Also, the community of Mampueng gave a dance performance of their tribe to the Gordon College and ALCU-COA accreditors during the site visit.

According to Mr. Dante P. Sardina, the coordinator for Gordon College’s Community Extension Unit, the accreditors aim to observe and assess the impact of the institution’s services to the community of Mampueng.

In addition, Dr. Ediric D. Gadia, Vice President for Research Development and Community Extension Services (RDCES) was also present at the site visit and underscored that the initiative also aims to observe the performance o

# Let’s take it from the EIC

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 7, 2024**

Bealyn P. Demanalata, Editor-in-Chief, O.I.C. Head Photojournalist of The Forefront – Gordon College and elected Young Journalist Association of Region 3 (YJAR3) Olongapo Coordinator, shared her views regarding the Women’s Month Celebration 2024.

In an interview with The Forefront, Demanalata expressed her perspective in the phrase “Women Can’t Lead.”

“Leadership has no manual, as it demands commitment and self-development. As a woman, I am determined to be an example to challen
label: lets-take-it-from-the-eic

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/lets-take-it-from-the-eic/]
 it demands commitment and self-development. As a woman, I am determined to be an example to challenge the stereotypes and biases of gender roles and leadership,” the Editor-in-Chief stated.

Furthermore, the O.I.C Head Photojournalist also discussed that this societal belief was her least prority compared on establishing a system of leading with inclusivity, diversity and press freedom.

Moreover, the YJAR3 Olongapo Coordinator talked on what she had considered as being a leader of the publication.

“I am not a leader because somebody said so. I did not accept the position because I had to pr
label: lets-take-it-from-the-eic

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/lets-take-it-from-the-eic/]
ion.

“I am not a leader because somebody said so. I did not accept the position because I had to prove to the society that women could lead. Not for popularity or because of peer pressure. I take the lead because I can,” the student leader said.

Lastly, she showned dedication, braveness and responsiveness over her leadership career and ended with a stong statement, “𝗜 𝗮𝗺 𝗮 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗜 𝗮𝗺 𝗮 𝘄𝗼𝗺𝗮𝗻.”

On the other hand, the Philippines celebrated the said event by the theme, “Lipunang Patas sa Bagong Pilipinas: Kakayahan ng Kababaihan, Papatunayan!”

The Women’s Month Celebration was commemor
label: lets-take-it-from-the-eic

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/lets-take-it-from-the-eic/]
Bagong Pilipinas: Kakayahan ng Kababaihan, Papatunayan!”

The Women’s Month Celebration was commemorated every March of the year.

# Dr. Soriano on GC’s overall champ feat: After 13 years, we got this!

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 26, 2024**

Gordon College athletes were showered gold as they were declared the Overall Champion, hoarding 41 gold, 16 silver, and 31 bronze medals at the awarding ceremony of the 13th Local Colleges and Universities-Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games at Muntinlupa City today.

Warriors rise above their last year’s placement from being the 12th LCUAA’s 1st runner-up to climb this year’s pinnacle among 24 other local colleges and universit
label: dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this/]
12th LCUAA’s 1st runner-up to climb this year’s pinnacle among 24 other local colleges and universities across the country.

In an interview with Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, the OIC President of GC, assessed the next moves for the athletes and coaches alike.

“Ang key pala rito ay whole year round training, kasi before we are not doing that and hopefully by next year we will maintain ‘yong [pagiging] overall champ,” the OIC President asserted.

On the other hand, Mr. Roy De Vera, GC Sports Coordinator, shares what matters more than the gold this competition brought upon GC athletes.

“We’re so gla
label: dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this/]
or, shares what matters more than the gold this competition brought upon GC athletes.

“We’re so glad being the overall champion, napakasaya natin sa tagumpay na nakamit ng ating paaralan, subalit di pa tayo dito nagtatapos, dahil higit sa ensayo ay ang disiplina. Disiplina sa pag-aaral, disiplina sa larangan ng palakasan at pangangalaga sa katawan,” said the Sports Coordinator.

Moreover, Ms. Lovelyn Ceralde, the Vice President of the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) highlighted the struggles LCUAA goers had overcome before achieving their now worn crown.

“All of your hard work
label: dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/dr-soriano-on-gcs-overall-champ-feat-after-13-years-we-got-this/]
e struggles LCUAA goers had overcome before achieving their now worn crown.

“All of your hard work has finally paid off. Alam ko maraming obstacles, marami tayong pinagdaanan from preparation, training, [and] screening but amidst all of these, all of you gave your 100% dedication and resiliency during the 13th LCUAA National Games,” Ms. Ceralde expressed her appreciation.

The 13th LCUAA champions will now be home-bound as their sports uniform will then be replaced with that of Gordon College’s, ready again to face what they left behind in Olongapo City’s classroom.

**Friday, February 28, 2025**

MARIKIT PARK—Students from GC’s College of Computer Studies (CCS) launched their first-ever Tech Fest to inform its participants of the dangers and capabilities of cyber technicalities and to showcase the talents of the participating attendees last February 22, Saturday.

When asked, Kurt Jeehan Tejado, one of the Events Chairman stressed out that this is a fund-raising activity that aims to allocate its earnings to the CCS students.

“May plan na magkaroon ng iMac lab para sa mga CCS students. And then, ‘yong purpose nito [fund-raising] is makatulong sa pag-upgrade no’ng iMac lab na ‘yon,” Tejado assured.

𝐅𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐚𝐥 𝐨𝐟 𝐬𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐭𝐚𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬

In addition to that, the festival includes three portions of educational talk with Mr. Domingo J. Chico in his “CyberSecurity 101” lecture; Mr. Wilson Censon on “How AI is shaping our future: Opportunities for students”; and Mr. Marvin Calingacion teaching “Pillars of information security.”

On the other hand, the Tech Fest also hosted multiple talent showcases, namely; cosplay runway, esports highlights, film screening, and digital arts presentation from its participants.

Moreso, there are numerous booths within the premises o
label: gc-ccs-tech-fest-on-debut-eyes-annual-celebration

al arts presentation from its participants.

Moreso, there are numerous booths within the premises offering food and other products.

𝐓𝐞𝐜𝐡 𝐅𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐓𝐞𝐚𝐦’𝐬 𝐡𝐮𝐫𝐝𝐥𝐞𝐬

In an interview with The Forefront, Events Chairman Mark Labucca expressed how difficult it was for them to push this event through.

“I can say, ang pinaka naging malaking challenge talagang kinaharap namin is ‘yong pag-gather ng participants.”

Furthermore, Labucca, when asked why the event’s date was moved from January 15 to February 22, stated how the participant invitation forced them to postpone the former date.

“January 15 kasi
label: gc-ccs-tech-fest-on-debut-eyes-annual-celebration

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ccs-tech-fest-on-debut-eyes-annual-celebration/]
2, stated how the participant invitation forced them to postpone the former date.

“January 15 kasi hindi pa siya start ng semester…mas lalong naging hindrance ‘yong hindi pa start ‘yong semester sa pag-gather namin ng participants,” he furthered.

𝐄𝐲𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐓𝐞𝐜𝐡 𝐅𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔

GC CCS Tech Fest team will aim to continue this program as a legacy of their college to launch the same event next year with the goal of further widening its scope of attendees, its contents, and solidifying its purpose.

# End of a Chapter: GC 22nd Commencement Exercises

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, July 16, 2024**

Gordon College 22nd Commencement Exercises with the theme “GCIAN GRADUATES: Embracing Transformative Innovations Beyond Barriers,” held at Subic Bay Exhibition and Convention Center (SBECC) on July 11, 2024.

Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, OIC-College President, graced the event with her heartfelt opening remarks.

“It is a dedication to make a difference, not only in the Olongapo City, but in the entire global community. The willingness to put others before oneself,
label: end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises/]
the Olongapo City, but in the entire global community. The willingness to put others before oneself, and understanding that the greatest fulfillment is found from helping those around us,” the OIC-College President reminded.

Furthermore, Mr. Rowel Y. Castillo, College Registrar, presented a total of 645 students consisting of graduates from College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS), College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS), and College of Computer Studies (CCS).

Moreover, Olongapo City Mayor and Chairperson for the Board of Trustees Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. also delivered his special m
label: end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises/]
or and Chairperson for the Board of Trustees Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. also delivered his special message, along with accepting and confirming the graduates.

He highlighted in his message the expansion of the Gordon College Building near Kalaklan Bridge, and the plans of changing its name from Gordon College to Unibersidad ng Lungsod ng Olongapo.

“Gusto ko lang maging proud kayo sa school niyo, sa city — maging proud kayo sa Olongapo. And kapag nasa field na kayo, kapag successful na kayo, proud “ULO” kayo,” the mayor stated.

Meanwhile, Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., CAHS Dean took the platform
label: end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises

content:

oud “ULO” kayo,” the mayor stated.

Meanwhile, Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., CAHS Dean took the platform to introduce Dr. Lora L. Yusi, Director IV of Commission on Higher Education (CHED) of Region III Office, as the commencement speaker of the ceremony and emphasized the importance of gratitude to the graduates.

“You have reasons to complain, but I hope you will choose to be grateful. It’s an acknowledgement of the good things that have happened to you,” Dr. Yusi said.

“If it is good, it’s wonderful; if it is bad, it’s experience — meaning, whatever happens to you, you still have to thank God
label: end-of-a-chapter-gc-22nd-commencement-exercises

rful; if it is bad, it’s experience — meaning, whatever happens to you, you still have to thank God for that. Because it is the good and the bad that will make us the best versions of ourselves very soon,” CHED Region III Director IV also added.

In addition, Ms. Clarissa Mae M. Berja, Magna Cum Laude from Bachelor of Arts in Communication gave her Graduation Speech, followed by the Pledge of Loyalty officiated by Monica L. Estrada, President of Graduating Class 2024, and the Alumni Oath, led by Abraham B. Dionson Jr., President of Gordon College Alumni Association.

**Sunday, February 18, 2024**

To mark the start of the foundation week, the Gordon College Facutly Association (GCFAs) conducted a fun run activity entitled, “FUNdation RUN 2024: 25 Years of Silver Success”, at Gordon College, Tapinac Oval Sports Complex, February 18, 2024.

# GCian from BSED-E Goes Viral for his Miniature Buses

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, May 4, 2023**

Exhibiting brilliance, a third-year Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in English (BSED-E) student at Gordon College, John Khristopher Bugarin, stunned the netizens with a Facebook post showing his artistic talent in making miniature buses made of vellum board, markers, and pens.  
  
He uses acetate film for its windows and yellow textiles for its curtains. The miniature buses also have ring interior lights that alternate with orange and green, just lik
label: gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses/]
s. The miniature buses also have ring interior lights that alternate with orange and green, just like a real Victory Liner Bus model.  
  
John Kristopher’s love for buses started when he was six (6) years old, and he has been riding the same bus company whenever they travel home from Umingan, Pangasinan. According to John, he began collecting his bus tickets in February 2011, when his oldest ticket was dated.  
  
“Siguro, in the future, continue lang po on bus spotting on my free time since nagiging happy na po ako basta may nacacapture akong bus. Isa rin po yan talaga sa mga nagpapasaya sak
label: gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses/]
giging happy na po ako basta may nacacapture akong bus. Isa rin po yan talaga sa mga nagpapasaya sakin pag stressed ako. Dahil po sa pagiging bus enthusiast ko madami na rin po akong bagong kaibigan na mga bus enthusiast din, akala ko po kasi noon ako lang yung may ganitong hobby,” he said in an interview with 93.5 Brigada News FM Olongapo.  
  
He stated that once he improves his miniature buses, he will then start accepting commissions from those who are interested in buying his artwork.  
  
Aside from making model buses, the young artist also enjoys photography, where he mostly features bu
label: gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcian-from-bsed-e-goes-viral-for-his-miniature-buses/]
Aside from making model buses, the young artist also enjoys photography, where he mostly features buses from Victory Liner, Inc. John Kristopher has his own Facebook page (JK Archives), where you can follow him and support his work.

✍️ : Camille Joy Lacanilao | News Correspondent

# Most Outstanding Customs Administration Students from Gordon College

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, August 31, 2023**

The PSCAS National Leader’s Summit and Research Conference, in collaboration with the Philippine Association of Customs Brokers in Education Inc., granted five Gordon College students from the Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration (BSCA) with the title “Most Outstanding Customs Administration Students in the Philippines” and 2nd Best Research Poster on August 29, 2023, at Teacher’s Camp, Baguio City.

Ivy Claire Tan, former VP-op
label: most-outstanding-customs-administration-students-from-gordon-college

st Research Poster on August 29, 2023, at Teacher’s Camp, Baguio City.

Ivy Claire Tan, former VP-operations of the PSCAS Gordon College Chapter, was ranked as the 4th Outstanding Customs Administration Student of the Philippines.

Meanwhile, Former VP-Audit of PSCAS Gordon College Chapter Kim Ramos Erandio was ranked 5th.

Moreover, Loren Gallardo, Sheilla Reguindin, and Christine Carumba were awarded the 2nd Best Research Poster.

# GC greets ALCUCOA accreditors as programs eye for Level 3 status

[ALCUCOA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alcucoa/) [Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [CBA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/cba/) [CEAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ceas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, February 12, 2025**

𝐈𝐍 𝐏𝐇𝐎𝐓𝐎𝐒 | GC greets ALCUCOA accreditors as programs eye for Level 3 status

Newly seated College President Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano and Gordon College (GC) admins welcomed the ALCUCOA accreditation team including ALCUCOA President Dr.
label: gc-greets-alcucoa-accreditors-as-programs-eye-for-level-3-status

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-greets-alcucoa-accreditors-as-programs-eye-for-level-3-status/]
d Gordon College (GC) admins welcomed the ALCUCOA accreditation team including ALCUCOA President Dr. Raymundo P. Arcega and Supervising Accreditor Dr. Dalisay Brawner at the Center for Quality Assurance and Accreditation office earlier.

This day marks the 3-day accreditation period of five programs offered by the institution, eyeing Level 3, including BS Customs Administration, BS Business Administration-HRM, BSBA-FM under the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and BSEd-Social Studies, and BSEd-Mathematics under the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS).

Along with the vying
label: gc-greets-alcucoa-accreditors-as-programs-eye-for-level-3-status

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-greets-alcucoa-accreditors-as-programs-eye-for-level-3-status/]
nd BSEd-Mathematics under the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS).

Along with the vying programs, the now-known Cosme Y. Sering Library will also be accredited by the ALCUCOA body for its role in providing instruction materials for the programs.

Stay tuned for the latest updates here at the official page of The Forefront!

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

# City of Olongapo honors TF’s performance in RHEPC21

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, June 14, 2023**

The City of Olongapo recognized the delegates of The Forefront (TF), the official publication unit of Gordon College through the resolution proposed by Hon. Sarah Lugerna Lipumano- Garcia, City Councilor and the Chairperson of the Committee on Organizational Development and Personnel Matters, June 13 at Sangguniang Panlungsod, Session Hall, Olongapo City Complex.

Recently, the TF bagged 19 awards from the 21st Regional Higher Education Press Conference (
label: city-of-olongapo-honors-tfs-performance-in-rhepc21

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/city-of-olongapo-honors-tfs-performance-in-rhepc21/]
mplex.

Recently, the TF bagged 19 awards from the 21st Regional Higher Education Press Conference (RHEPC21) and brought honor and pride to the city.

The winners of the said event have been personally congratulated by the city councilors and the city vice mayor with an approved resolution, “A Resolution Congratulating the Editorial Board and Staff of Gordon College-The Forefront for Bagging 19 Awards in the 21st Regional Higher Education Press Conference held last May 17-19, at San Fernando City, Pampanga”.

In addition to that, the city councilors also asked the scribes of the publication to
label: city-of-olongapo-honors-tfs-performance-in-rhepc21

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/city-of-olongapo-honors-tfs-performance-in-rhepc21/]
y, Pampanga”.

In addition to that, the city councilors also asked the scribes of the publication to share their experiences from the competition.

Mr. Reggie Boy M. Vargas, the editor-in-chief of the TF led the discussion in the committee meeting headed by Hon. Lipumano.

Moreover, Vargas shared his immense gratitude for recognizing the performance of his team.

This was the first time the publication had joined the RHEPC and been recognized by the city through the legislation of a resolution.

# Results of Accreditation: BSN is Now Level III

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, June 13, 2024**

With great honor and pride, we are proud to inform you that Gordon College has received the results of the accreditation visit conducted by the Commission on Accreditation for Local Colleges and Universities (ALCUCOA) in April 2024.

Our Bachelor of Science in Nursing Program received Level III accreditation, making GC the FIRST Local College in Region III and SECOND in the Philippines.

Showcasing excellence, twelve (12) academic programs have also secured the
label: results-of-accreditation-bsn-is-now-level-iii

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/results-of-accreditation-bsn-is-now-level-iii/]
COND in the Philippines.

Showcasing excellence, twelve (12) academic programs have also secured their Levels I and II accreditations.

These were made possible with the help and great contribution of our City Mayor Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr, City Councilor Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, Board of Trustees Members, College Officials, Faculty Members, Administrative Personnel, Students, Alumni, Industry Partners, Parents and other GC stakeholders.

Again, we thank all the people behind this success and may we continue to be partners in fulfilling the mission and realizing the vision of the Co
label: results-of-accreditation-bsn-is-now-level-iii

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/results-of-accreditation-bsn-is-now-level-iii/]
cess and may we continue to be partners in fulfilling the mission and realizing the vision of the College. Together, let us celebrate GC’s significant milestone in its quest for quality tertiary education and as a catalyst of change to the lives of its learners.

# A magnitude 5.9 earthquake hits

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, December 5, 2023**

A magnitude 5.9 earthquake from Lubang Town, Occidental Mindoro at exactly 4:23 PM was also felt in Olongapo City causing forced evacuation of students inside the Gordon College Building.

Furthermore, the institution suspended the face-to-face classes from 4:30 PM onwards.

Keep Safe GCians!

# Gender and Development Juana Komiks Contest: SBMA hails TF as the ‘champions’

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, October 9, 2023**

BAWAL BOBO[NG] DITO, the official entry of The Forefront – Gordon College for the Content Creating Contest of the Subic Bay Metropolitan Authority Gender and Development reeked creativity and authenticity which led to its victory today.

The contest was part of the annual Subic Bay Freeport Zone M.O.V.E – “Men Opposed VAW (Violence Against Women) Everywhere” Campaign in which supports the national quest for a VAW-free Philippines
label: gender-and-development-juana-komiks-contest-sbma-hails-tf-as-the-champions

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gender-and-development-juana-komiks-contest-sbma-hails-tf-as-the-champions/]
Against Women) Everywhere” Campaign in which supports the national quest for a VAW-free Philippines and to establish that everyone has the right to live in a gender-fair and violence-free society.

The Juana Komiks Magazine is the official magazine of the SBMA Gender and Development which includes their programs and activities and the content creation competition aimed to make use of comic strips to help understand stories about Gender and Development advocacies and how it can be addressed through this platform.

Eight members from The Forefront – Gordon College emerged victorious against the
label: gender-and-development-juana-komiks-contest-sbma-hails-tf-as-the-champions

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gender-and-development-juana-komiks-contest-sbma-hails-tf-as-the-champions/]
gh this platform.

Eight members from The Forefront – Gordon College emerged victorious against the Olongapo City National High School who also participated in the contest hosted by the SBMA GAD, the members are as follows:

Jez Anne S. Radam – Project Head & Content Writer

John Patrick Mateo & Shermel Carlos – Storyline Writers

Desiree Capitulo & Michaela Cortezano – Content Writers

Lance Isaac Leon, Dol Rich Jay Mangin, Liquid Aeron Raines – Artists

Guiller T. Marila – Adviser

: Jez Anne S. Radam | Managing Editor

: Angelique Jose | Graphics Editor

Lance Isaac Leon | Multimedia Editor

# GC’s Denise Mendoza Secures Impressive 2nd Place Victory in Tourism Quiz Challenge

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, September 26, 2023**

Denise Mendoza, a dedicated 4th-year Tourism Management student at Gordon College, showcased her expertise by clinching 2nd place in the highly competitive “Tourism Quiz Challenge” held on September 26th at Ayala Malls Harbor Point.

This event was part of the grand celebration of World Tourism Day 2023, revolving around the theme “Tourism & Green Investment,” and organized by the Subic Bay Metropolitan Authority (SBMA) a
label: gcs-denise-mendoza-secures-impressive-2nd-place-victory-in-tourism-quiz-challenge

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-denise-mendoza-secures-impressive-2nd-place-victory-in-tourism-quiz-challenge/]
e theme “Tourism & Green Investment,” and organized by the Subic Bay Metropolitan Authority (SBMA) as part of National Tourism Week 2023.

The competition brought together talented students from various schools around Region 3, with the enticing prize of 10,000 pesos reserved for the triumphant participant.

Gordon College had two standout representatives, Denise Paola Erin R. Mendoza, a 4th-year student, and Mark Louis Macachor, a 3rd-year student, both are from the Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management. Their journey to success was expertly guided by Ma’am Lizette Ann Sia, their dedicate
label: gcs-denise-mendoza-secures-impressive-2nd-place-victory-in-tourism-quiz-challenge

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-denise-mendoza-secures-impressive-2nd-place-victory-in-tourism-quiz-challenge/]
sm Management. Their journey to success was expertly guided by Ma’am Lizette Ann Sia, their dedicated program coordinator.

The title of the competition went to Marione Kyle W. Garcia from Bataan Peninsula State University, with France Erriele M. Sawi from Philippine Women’s University CDCEC Bataan securing third place.

# Empowering the Students—New Hope of this Nation!

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, September 23, 2023**

Pagtatalaga Re-echo year III with the theme “Palihan 2023: Forging Greater Leaders. Crafting ALCU students as the New Hope of this Nation.” concluded yesterday at the 3rd floor function hall.

Aiming to empower its students, the said event was led by The Office of Student’s Welfare and Services (OSWS) in collaboration with Ad Meliora, an all-student management organization.

“[The purpose of this event was to] Re-echo or share what other leaders learned
label: empowering-the-students-new-hope-of-this-nation

ment organization.

“[The purpose of this event was to] Re-echo or share what other leaders learned from the seminar they attended in Pampanga,” said Kristine Dominico, the event manager of Ad Mileora.

On the other hand, the flow of the program’s information dissemination regarding “Re-echoing” was divided into six Modules, discussed by the leaders that attended said seminar in Pampanga.
