# 𝐏𝐚𝐠𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐠𝐚 𝐑𝐞-𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐨 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐈𝐕: 𝐀𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐠𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐲𝐨𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐥𝐢𝐝𝐞𝐫-𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐲𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐮𝐧𝐠𝐨 𝐬𝐚 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐬𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, November 21, 2024**

Muling naglayag ang “student leaders” tungo sa transpormatibong pamumuno sa ika-apat na “Pagtatalaga Re-Echo,” na pinangunahan ng mga piling miyembro ng student organizations and council na ginanap sa GC Function Hall, ika-19 ng Nobyembre.

Ang naturang programa ay naglalayon na ibahagi ang mga natutunang kaalaman ng ating mga GCian student leader delegates sa nagdaang Association of Local Colleges
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1/]
nang kaalaman ng ating mga GCian student leader delegates sa nagdaang Association of Local Colleges and Universities (ALCU) Intensive Course on Student Leadership Development noong nakaraang Hunyo sa San Fernando, Pampanga sa kanilang mga kapwa lider-estudyante.

Pinasimulan ni Office of the Student Welfare and Services Vice President Ms. Lovelyn P. Ceralde sa kaniyang opening remarks at sinundan ni Ms. Frances Mobo, GC-SSC Chairman; at Mr. Kharl Nofies, Samahang Filipino (SamFil) President, bilang mga unang speaker na siyang nagbahagi ng unang paksa tungkol sa “Transformational Student Leader
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1/]
ang mga unang speaker na siyang nagbahagi ng unang paksa tungkol sa “Transformational Student Leadership Metanoia.”

Samantala, sinundan naman ito nina Mr. Gimuel Aguimatang, Vice President of Junior People Management Association of the Philippines (JPMAP); at Ms. Alexis Penuliar, President of Nursing Student Organization (NSO), na siyang nagbukas ng diskusyon ng “Fundamentals of Student Leadership.”

Sa pagtatapos ng pang-umagang sesyon, binigyang buhay ng mga student leaders ang programa sa pamamagitan ng isang workshop at pagbuo ng kani-kanilang koreograpiya ng isang sayaw para sa dance com
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1/]
mamagitan ng isang workshop at pagbuo ng kani-kanilang koreograpiya ng isang sayaw para sa dance competition na siyang parte ng pang-hapon na sesyon.

Sa kabilang banda, muling binuksan ang pagtatalakay ng panghuling modyul patungkol sa paksang “Digital Citizenship as a tool for Student Leadership” na binahagi nina Mr. Aaron Perez, President of College of Computer Studies Council (CCS-SC); at Mr. Lorenzo Cayabyab, President of League of Tourism Student Council of the Philippines (LTSP).

“Every time, lagi kong sinasabi how important it is to practice communication. Always listen to comprehend
label: %f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8f%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%9a-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%9e-%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%a1/]
time, lagi kong sinasabi how important it is to practice communication. Always listen to comprehend not listen to reply,” saad ni Ms. Kimberly Recitis, Coordinator of Student Organizations Unit sa kaniyang closing remarks.

Dagdag pa niya, sa kabila ng hamon ng pagkakaroon ng limitadong kapasidad sa venue, nasa natatanging student leaders ang responsibiliad upang i-re-echo ang kanilang mga natutunan sa kanilang kapwa lider-estudyante.

# FOI at Gordon College

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, September 18, 2023**

As part of the Transparency Roadshow in Olongapo City, the Presidential Communications Office, through its Freedom of Information – Program Management Office, organized a Campus Caravan at Gordon College.

The Campus Caravan, an integral part of the 2023 Transparency Roadshow, aims to cultivate a culture of transparency and accountability within educational institutions like [Gordon College](https://www.facebook.com/GordonCollegeOfficial?__cft__[0]=AZVWNjKvkyjlKNlE4ul0Hta9K4wQXTYw6U

By acquainting students and faculty members with the Freedom of Information (FOI) mechanism, our objective is to empower them to access and utilize vital information for their academic pursuits.

ormation for their academic pursuits.

Likewise, Atty. Evangeline Q. de Leon, Assistant Secretary for Operation of PCO, has underscored the significance of information accessibility in fostering a more engaged and discerning student body who will wholeheartedly champion FOI’s noble causes.

The Campus Caravan features a comprehensive discussion on FOI led by Mr. John Wilmer Jimenez, the Communications Lead

# GC CONDUCTS 12TH COMMENCEMENT EXERCISES FOR IGS

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, September 1, 2023**

Gordon College’s Institute of Graduate Studies (IGS) 12th Commencement Exercises with the theme “Mastering the Art of Adaptability: Equipping Graduates with the Skills to Thrive in a Vuca World,” held at Subic Bay Travellers Hotel and Event Center, August 4.

As presented by Mr. Rowell Y. Castillo, College Registrar, there were 52 graduates from the Master of Arts in Education, Master of Arts in Nursing, Master of Business Management, and Master of Public Ad
label: gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs/]
Arts in Education, Master of Arts in Nursing, Master of Business Management, and Master of Public Administration.

The processional started at 4:00 PM with the flag and colors, the candidates for graduation, the faculty members, college officials, the board of trustees, and lastly, this year’s commencement speaker, Dr. Richard G. Daenos, Director, Regional Office III, Department of Tourism.

Meanwhile, Dr. Imelda DP Soriano, College Administrator and Dean of the Institute of Graduate Studies delivered the opening remarks on behalf of the College President, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, and her special
label: gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs/]
ivered the opening remarks on behalf of the College President, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, and her special message afterward.

Hon. Atty. Rollen C. Paulino Jr., Olongapo City Mayor, and the Chairman of the Board of Trustees delivered his inspirational message along with accepting and confirming the graduates.

In addition to that, Mayor Paulino Jr. also awarded the plaque and token of appreciation to the commencement speaker along with Dr. Soriano.

Everyone acknowledged the arrival of former mayor Rollen Paulino during the ceremony and also gave his congratulatory message to the graduates.

Moreove
label: gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs/]
llen Paulino during the ceremony and also gave his congratulatory message to the graduates.

Moreover, Mayor Paulino Jr., Dr. Soriano, and Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs, led the graduate school hooding ceremony, diploma distribution, academic honors granting, and presentation of special awards.

The program coordinators—Drs. Ediric D. Gadia, Master of Arts in Education; Victor C. Quimen, Master of Arts in Nursing; Mark Alvin V. Lazaro, Master of Business Management; and Dr. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Master of Public Administration—presented the reading of the titles.
label: gc-conducts-12th-commencement-exercises-for-igs

content:

nd Dr. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Master of Public Administration—presented the reading of the titles.

Furthermore, Ms. Christina D. Tamayo, Master in Public Administration with High Distinction, gave her heartwarming speech. Ms. Lovely P. Ceralde, Master of Business Management, President of the IGS Graduating Class 2023, led the pledge of loyalty.

The graduates also took their alumni oath, presented by Mr. Michael P. Tuyay, President of the GC Alumni Association, Inc.

The program concluded with the closing remarks of Dr. Paguio and the closing prayer of Dr. Luckyhansel Cerezo, IGS Faculty.

# Accreditation 2024: A Path to Greater Recognition

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, April 4, 2024**

Olongapo City – Gordon College began its much awaited Accreditation 2024 in an opening ceremony today, April 3, 2024 at the SMX Convention Center, signaling a significant turning point in the college’s path to greater academic recognition.

The accreditation, facilitated by the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commision on Accreditation (ALCUCOA), is scheduled to take place from April 3rd to 5th.

The Opening Day began with a vibrant atmosphere
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
, is scheduled to take place from April 3rd to 5th.

The Opening Day began with a vibrant atmosphere as the institution headed by college President Prof. Arlida Pame and College Administrator Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano together with the BOT Chairman and City Mayor Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. welcomed the esteemed accreditors.

Afterwards, the Pase Voci began with a heartfelt invocation followed by singing of the Philippine National Anthem, the Olongapo City Hymn, the Gordon College Hymn, and the ALCU COA Hymn.

The college president, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, gave the welcoming remarks as she thanked ev
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
COA Hymn.

The college president, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, gave the welcoming remarks as she thanked everyone in attendance and emphasized the commitment of the technical working group, which was headed by Dr. Soriano.

In addition, she said, “our collective commitment to upholding the highest standard of education and academic excellence is not a mere duty but a sacred responsibility.”

Afterwards, an audio-visual presentation from the ALCU-COA that offered insights into the accreditation process was presented and later on, Mayor Paulino gave a speech outlining his plans for Gordon College and r
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
was presented and later on, Mayor Paulino gave a speech outlining his plans for Gordon College and reaffirmed the school’s goal of becoming a university, demonstrating the city’s dedication to improving education.

Dr. Elena Presnedi, supervising lead accreditor, commended Gordon College as a frontrunner in accreditation, noting its pursuit of Level 3 status, similar to Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa. Expressing hope for Gordon College’s attainment of such recognition. Following her remarks, Dr. Presnedi presented the ALCU-COA Brief, a guide for the accreditation process, underscoring the
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
, Dr. Presnedi presented the ALCU-COA Brief, a guide for the accreditation process, underscoring the rigorous standards and criteria essential for accreditation. With the guidance of experienced professionals.

Dr. Imelda DP Soriano, the College Administrator and CQAA Director, acknowledged the diligent efforts of the Gordon College accreditation team, paving the way for a successful evaluation. In addition she also mentioned school deans, faculty, and students, emphasizing their indispensable role in Gordon College’s journey. She acknowledged their collective dedication, stating, “Without you
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
ole in Gordon College’s journey. She acknowledged their collective dedication, stating, “Without you, we cannot make this.”

The highlight of the program was the introduction of the 11 ALCU-COA ACCREDITORS by ALCU-COA Supervising Assistant and Technical Assistant, Prof. Alain Anuevo signifying their readiness to assess and uphold educational standards at Gordon College.

Lastly, Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs and Officer-in-Charge of the College of Education in Arts and Sciences, gave final remarks to wrap up the proceedings. Dr. Paguio reaffirmed the college’s commi
label: accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/accreditation-2024-a-path-to-greater-recognition/]
d Sciences, gave final remarks to wrap up the proceedings. Dr. Paguio reaffirmed the college’s commitment, he stated that “we believe in the power of experiential learning where theory means practice.” And its confidence in meeting and surpassing the standards set by ALCU-COA accreditation.

In the next few days, the accreditation process will take place, and Gordon College is on track to reach another significant milestone in its pursuit of institutional expansion and academic excellence.

# GCians triumphed with awards on a Start-up Challenge

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, October 9, 2023**

Gordon College’s students from the College of Computer Studies (CCS) claimed two titles from joining the Philippine Start-up Challenge 8 Regional Pitching Competition at Bataan Peninsula State University on October 4, 2023.

The College of Computer Studies embarked the Pyro Logic Team into the competition, mentored by Mr. Arvin Shelby H. De Leon, and consists of three participants, namely Mark Janeil R. Villasis, Kanji Takahashi, and John Carlo D. Ramos.
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
ts of three participants, namely Mark Janeil R. Villasis, Kanji Takahashi, and John Carlo D. Ramos. The team launched their start-up entry called “REMEDIO,” a gamified system that improves medication and supplements adherence by making it engaging and enjoyable, which aided them in claiming the Region 3 Championship title.

In an interview with The Forefront, 4th-year CCS Student Mark Janeil Villasis said it was a time-stopping moment when his team was announced as the Region 3 Champion.

“What we feel is a mix of happiness, surprise, nervousness, and most importantly, gratitude. Hindi talaga
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
we feel is a mix of happiness, surprise, nervousness, and most importantly, gratitude. Hindi talaga kami makapaniwala that time kasi yung kaba namin before kami magpresent and after namin magpitch is ramdam parin namin. Nawala lang lahat ng nasa isip namin nung tinawag na kami,” he said.

In line with this, the team obtained the Best Presenters Award for their performance throughout the presentation of their start-up, adding to the significance of the challenges they faced.

According to Villasis, “One of the challenges we faced in creating our startup pitch was how we can compress all necessa
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
s, “One of the challenges we faced in creating our startup pitch was how we can compress all necessary information about ‘REMEDIO’ into an appealing 5-minute pitch.” To address the problem, Villasis and his team conducted a trial-and-error with several practices to ensure the feasibility of presenting concise information in 5 minutes.

Prior to the competition, Villasis and his team proposed several project proposals to Mr. De Leon, their mentor and project adviser, until they decided to submit REMEDIO as their entry. The concept is motivated by the medication adherence of an elderly relative
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
REMEDIO as their entry. The concept is motivated by the medication adherence of an elderly relative of Villasis.

“Napansin ko kasi na pag halimbawa may reward na inaano [ibinibigay] kay “papsie” (Elder), sumusunod agad siya or should I say na parang mas naeenjoy nya pag ganun,” he said. Villasis also added that while researching medication adherence, they found out that it is one of the most critical issues in today’s healthcare landscape, leading to the creation of REMEDIO, which was designed to make proper medication adherence recreational.

In terms of the process, the team spent the month
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
to make proper medication adherence recreational.

In terms of the process, the team spent the months of August and September researching the issues underlying the non-adherence to medication and painstakingly researched numerous related literature as the foundation of their work. After months of locating related studies and establishing their start-up idea, they consulted their instructor for approval by conducting initial pitch presentations.

During the competition, as first-timers, the team felt unease as they stepped out of their comfort zone and saw other representatives of the different
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
felt unease as they stepped out of their comfort zone and saw other representatives of the different campuses who passionately presented their groundbreaking entries, but they thought it was exciting and crucial for their growth, so they confronted the challenge.

“Ang nasa isip lang namin nun is i-grab yung opportunity and to do our best for the team, mentors, and the school,” Villasis said.

Villasis also said that the remarkable honors they took home for Gordon College and the entire CCS family are all backed by the guidance of God.

Moreover, the whole of Gordon College was granted the tit
label: %f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ae%f0%9d%90%a6%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%a1%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%9d-%f0%9d%90%b0/]
ly are all backed by the guidance of God.

Moreover, the whole of Gordon College was granted the title of Most Number of Qualified Entries for submitting 30 entries in the competition. The competition acknowledges the following runner-ups:

# Gordon College Welcomes Accreditation Team

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, April 3, 2024**

Gordon College (GC) kicked off its school accreditation process today with a warm welcome for the visiting team from the accreditation body. GC faculty, staff, and students are eager to showcase the institution’s best practices in teaching, learning, and overall academic environment.

The first day of accreditation process began as the accreditation team commenced a comprehensive evaluation, meticulously observing educational programs and visiting classrooms first
label: gordon-college-welcomes-accreditation-team

 comprehensive evaluation, meticulously observing educational programs and visiting classrooms firsthand. This examination will continue over the next few days, providing a thorough assessment of Gordon College’s academic excellence and ensuring it meets the high standards of educational quality.

# Empowering Youth Echoes Through Women’s Month

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, March 11, 2024**

Francesca Simone Singson, Ms. GC 2024 and a student from the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN), revisits her advocacy on child exploitation awareness in honor of Women’s Month 2024.

In an interview with The Forefront, Singson tackled her winning advocacy regarding Child Exploitation Awareness as she placed emphasis on the necessity of educating children about consent, boundaries, respect, and honesty to ensure their safety from the dangers of exploitation.

Furthermore, she elaborated her advocacy by stating that safeguarding the welfare of children is a responsibility that everyone must be aware of.

“Children hold the power for the future of our society. They symbolize innocence and hope,” she said.

Moreover, relating her advocacy in this year’s celebration of Women’s Month, the nursing student expressed her sentiments in acknowledging the contributions of mothers, elder sisters, grandmothers, and educators for their dedication to protecting the

“Let us all continue fighting for what is right, let us continue to show that women can do anything that they put their hearts and minds into,” Ms. GC 2024 stated.

On top of this as part of her duty to promote awareness and urge people for a collective movement through preventative and supportive means, she outlines several measures to address the issues drawn from child exploitation.

# GC Gives Back to the Community By Altruism Activity 2023

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, January 9, 2024**

Gordon College Research, Development, and Community Extension Services (RDCES), together with the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS), orchestrated the Altruism activity 2023 with the theme, “Gift of Love: Spreading Joy and Kindness this Holiday Season” for the residents of Sitio Mampueng on December 22, 2023.

The donation drive was participated by different student organizations, faculty members, volunteers, and administrative personnels.
label: gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023/]
ated by different student organizations, faculty members, volunteers, and administrative personnels. Additionally, its impact transcended mere material donations and participants went beyond to demonstrate their unwavering support.

Consequently, student organizations not only contributed goods but also facilitated engaging games and programs for the community.

Furthermore, in an interview with The Forefront, Mr. Dante Sardina, RDCES Coordinator, highlighted the remarkable surge in support within the GC community. He further note the escalating popularity of contributing to various causes amo
label: gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023/]
in the GC community. He further note the escalating popularity of contributing to various causes among GCians.

“Indicating a shift towards more meaningful and significant expressions of gratitude. Increase in the number of volunteers from students, faculty members, and administrative personnel,” he also stated.

Moreover, Mr. Sardina mentioned that the future plans for this activity have not yet been disclosed or determined. Nevertheless, they remain confident that this significant occasion will continue to be successful and persist.

“Tasks were delegated to community extension coordinators,
label: gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-gives-back-to-the-community-by-altruism-activity-2023/]
l continue to be successful and persist.

“Tasks were delegated to community extension coordinators, unit or department representatives, and student volunteers. Clear schedules for receiving donations, repackaging goods, and distributing them were established. Effective communication and coordination among all those involved were ensured for the successful execution of the planned activities,” he added.

# GC Celebrates the Investiture Ceremony of Dr. Soriano

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, March 25, 2025**

Gordon College proudly celebrates the Investiture Ceremony of Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano as the new College President, held on March 25, 2025, at SMX Olongapo City.

An investiture ceremony is a formal event that signifies the official installation of a new president into office. It is a time-honored academic tradition that symbolizes the transfer of leadership, trust, and responsibility to the newly appo
label: gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano/]
ic tradition that symbolizes the transfer of leadership, trust, and responsibility to the newly appointed leader. This milestone event highlights the institution’s vision for the future and reaffirms its commitment to academic excellence, innovation, and service to the community.

The ceremony was graced by esteemed guests, including Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., Olongapo City Mayor; Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, Olongapo City Councilor; Dr. Lora L. Yusi, CHEDROIII Director; Dr. Raymundo P. Arcega, ALCU and ALCUCOA President; and Dr. Richard Daenos, Department of Tourism Region 3 Director. A
label: gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano/]
cega, ALCU and ALCUCOA President; and Dr. Richard Daenos, Department of Tourism Region 3 Director. Also in attendance were City Officials, Department Heads, Board of Trustees (BOT) Members, Presidents of Higher Education Institutions (HEIs), Industry Partners, College Officials, Faculty, Students, Alumni, Administrative Personnel, the family of Dr. Soriano, and other valued stakeholders of Gordon College.

The investiture of Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano marks the beginning of a new era for Gordon College—one that is driven by transformative leadership, strengthened academic programs, and a deeper co
label: gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-celebrates-the-investiture-ceremony-of-dr-soriano/]
ege—one that is driven by transformative leadership, strengthened academic programs, and a deeper commitment to student success and community development.

We extend our heartfelt congratulations to Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano and look forward to her guidance in shaping a brighter and more promising future for Gordon College!

# GORDON COLLEGE: A CHAMPION OF EXCELLENCE IN QUALITY ASSURANCE FOR TEACHER EDUCATION

[CHED](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ched/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, April 2, 2025**

We are proud and honored to share that Gordon College has been recognized by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) as one of the awardees at the “Excellence in Quality Assurance in Teacher Education (EQUATE) Awards.”

This prestigious recognition highlights our institution’s unwavering commitment to pre-service teacher education, program accreditation, and excellence in
label: gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education/]
’s unwavering commitment to pre-service teacher education, program accreditation, and excellence in the Board of Licensure Examination for Professional Teachers (BLEPT).

The awarding ceremony took place on April 2, 2025 (Wednesday) at The Manila Hotel. The ceremony was attended by Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College President; Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs and OIC-Dean of the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS); Dr. Ediric D. Gadia, Vice President for Research Development and Community Extension Services and Former Dean of CEAS; and Ms. Anna Theresa M. Amar
label: gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education/]
h Development and Community Extension Services and Former Dean of CEAS; and Ms. Anna Theresa M. Amarille, Assistant Dean of CEAS.

We extend our special thanks to Hon. Rolen C. Paulino, City Mayor; Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, Committee on Education Chairperson; Board of Trustees Members, College Officials, and other stakeholders of the College for their continuous support.

A special mention goes to all current and previous Faculty Members, Alumni, Industry Partners, Students, and other stakeholders whose dedication and hard work have contributed to this achievement.

This recognition i
label: gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-a-champion-of-excellence-in-quality-assurance-for-teacher-education/]
takeholders whose dedication and hard work have contributed to this achievement.

This recognition is a testament to the excellence and commitment of the entire Gordon College community in upholding the highest standards in teacher education. Congratulations to us all!

# GC Men’s Sepak Takraw, inasinta ang tanso

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 11, 2023**

Tiniyak ng Gordon College team na hindi hadlang ang pagkakaroon ng baguhang manlalaro sa kanilang koponan matapos magpakita ng gilas at ma-ibulsa ang tansong medalya sa katatapos lamang na Men’s Sepak Takraw round-robin match na ginanap sa Laguna Sports Complex Multifunction hall, Ika-10 ng Oktubre.

Namayagpag sa unang laro ang GC at ibinida ang kanilang matatalim na spike upang pulbusin ang Kolehiyo Ng Lungsod ng Lipa (KLL) sa Regu team A match sa iskor na 21-
label: %f0%9d%99%82%f0%9d%98%be-%f0%9d%99%88%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a8-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0-%f0%9d%99%8f%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%a7%f0%9d%99%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%99%82%f0%9d%98%be-%f0%9d%99%88%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a8-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0-%f0%9d%99%8f%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%a7%f0%9d%99%96/]
m na spike upang pulbusin ang Kolehiyo Ng Lungsod ng Lipa (KLL) sa Regu team A match sa iskor na 21-15, 21-12.

Sa sumunod na sagupaan, pinayukod pa rin ng GC Regu team B ang kaparehong kalaban para angkinin ang 2-0 na kabuuang puntos sa iskor na 21-19, 21-17.

Upang dagdagan ang lumalagablab na init ng labanan, sinubukang sindakin ng GC ang Taguig City University (TCU) sa kasunod na laro ngunit nabigo ang koponan na patumbahin ito matapos pahintuin ang kanilang bilis at listo at makuha ang 2-0 standings ng laro.

Mai-dura lamang ang nalasap na pagkatalo, nagwaldas ng matitinik na atake ang pa
label: %f0%9d%99%82%f0%9d%98%be-%f0%9d%99%88%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a8-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0-%f0%9d%99%8f%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%a7%f0%9d%99%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%99%82%f0%9d%98%be-%f0%9d%99%88%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%a8-%f0%9d%99%8e%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0-%f0%9d%99%8f%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%a7%f0%9d%99%96/]
standings ng laro.

Mai-dura lamang ang nalasap na pagkatalo, nagwaldas ng matitinik na atake ang parehong Regu team A at B sa huling gitgitan, dahilan para mawalan ng porma ang iba pang katunggali at masungkit ang ikatlong pwesto.

“Big learning experience po ito dahil mas nakilala namin ang sarili namin. We will continue to work on this po kasi baguhan pa lamang ‘yung iba sa team. Pagkatapos ng LCUAA, as soon as possible, training pa rin para kahit saan kami sumali, kaya namin manalo”, saad ni Jasper Silvido, team captain ng koponan.

# Gordon College Marks its 25th Founding Anniversary with Thanksgiving Mass

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, February 19, 2024**

The Gordon College commemorated its 25th anniversary with a poignant thanksgiving mass that takes place at the 3rd Floor.

Students, instructors, staff, and administrators came together to show their solidarity and gratitude for GC’s journey to excellent performance, which has brought them to this historic event. Reverend Roque Villanueva, Parish Priest of St. Joseph, officiated over the mass, allowing attendees to pause and reflect

# SIKLAB BLEEDS BLUE: 3-peat Overall Champs

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, September 30, 2024**

𝑨𝒔𝒖𝒍 𝒂𝒏𝒈 𝒌𝒖𝒍𝒂𝒚 𝒏𝒈 𝒌𝒂𝒎𝒑𝒚𝒆𝒐𝒏.

The CEAS Mentors garnered a total of 32 gold, 17 silver, and 22 bronze medals to stand atop the podium for the third time as SIKLAB 2024 puts off its fire in the closing ceremony held at Rizal Triangle, on September 27.

Loud cheers resonated within the event’s area as CEAS students, faculty, and other departments cheered loudly for the successful defending of the Mentors in their envied crown.

Moreover, the CBA Wizards with 23 golds
label: siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs/]
 successful defending of the Mentors in their envied crown.

Moreover, the CBA Wizards with 23 golds, 23 silvers, and 28 bronzes; and the CHTM Sharks with 17 golds, 31 silvers and 27 bronzes stood beside the overall champs as 1st and 2nd runner-ups in a tight match to win the victory.

While the CCS Phoenixes came in as 3rd runner-up with 15 golds, 13 silvers, and 16 bronzes; followed by the CAHS Tigers hauling a total of 11 golds, 13 silvers, and 17 bronzes.

𝒈𝒉𝒕 𝒕𝒊𝒎𝒆

The three-peat champs received the golden beams of the sun again in this year’s Gordon College’s sportsfest.

In an interview, Mr. Christian Loid Valenzuela, CEAS’ table tennis coach, points out that their victory, despite the anticipation, weighed heavily on their shoulders, as being the defending champ for the overall category also entails high expectations.

“Pressuring siya, ‘di lang sa coaches, lalo na sa players, given na marami rin sa amin ang hindi na nakalaro because it’s either they graduated last year or disallowed due to being LCUAA players. Given that, mahirap ding adjus
label: siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs/]
er they graduated last year or disallowed due to being LCUAA players. Given that, mahirap ding adjustment sa amin sa CEAS to scout new and promising players,” Mr. Valenzuela stressed out.

Furthermore, the table tennis coach also expressed how the Mentors proved true to the high expectations as they went over the heights of their flight last year.

“With what transpired yesterday, it only proves that CEAS Mentors know no bounds. We’re limitless for as long as our desire to enjoy and win games are there,” the coach said.

Lastly, to send a message to the other departments whom the Mentors battl
label: siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs/]
e there,” the coach said.

Lastly, to send a message to the other departments whom the Mentors battled this SIKLAB, Mr. Valenzuela sent out a message in the hope that everyone will adhere to this year’s sportsfest’s theme.

“Congratulations din sa ibang departments natin, as they all proved that they also have what it takes to become a home of their own champions. May this year’s sports festival ignite us all towards strength despite diversity,” he added.

𝑼𝒏𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒈𝒊𝒗𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑭𝒍𝒂𝒎𝒆𝒔 𝒐𝒇 𝑺𝒑𝒐𝒓𝒕𝒔𝒎𝒂𝒏𝒔𝒉𝒊𝒑: 𝑺𝑰𝑲𝑳𝑨𝑩 2024’𝒔 𝑺𝒖𝒄𝒄𝒆𝒔𝒔

label: siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs

𝑲𝑳𝑨𝑩 2024’𝒔 𝑺𝒖𝒄𝒄𝒆𝒔𝒔

In addition, SIKLAB 2024 was a huge success in advocating its theme as per Mr. Roy De Vera, SIKLAB 2024 Coordinator. He also expressed his joy as this year’s event fostered a healthy competition.

“Masaya dahil nakita natin na nagco-compete tayo with a healthy competition, so hindi siya ‘yong competition na sobrang bigat para sa iba’t ibang department. Naging healthy competition siya dahil sa bawat competition kahit ‘yung ibang department, nagse-celebrate sila for the win nung isang particular department,” the sportsfest coordinator celebrated.

Besides SIKLAB’s success, M
label: siklab-bleeds-blue-3-peat-overall-champs

ng isang particular department,” the sportsfest coordinator celebrated.

Besides SIKLAB’s success, Mr. De Vera looked forward to a longer preparation day for the succeeding sportsfest of Gordon College for a much more triumphant one next year.

“Siguro we’re replanning na mas marami ‘yong bilang ng araw o linggo or event months to prepare ng bawat department para at least mas maging mas maganda ang sportsfest by 2025. Also, we’re planning by 2025 na gano’n pa rin, na makapaghanda tayo nang maaga sa sportsfest para sa preparation for our national,” he said with hope.

# Visit of the Commission on Higher Education Regional Office III at Gordon College’s Cosme Y. Sering Library

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, February 7, 2025**

𝗜𝗡 𝗣𝗛𝗢𝗧𝗢𝗦 | Showcasing its commitment to academic excellence and innovation, Gordon College with its partners from the private sector, upgraded and introduced state of the art library.

The Cosme Y. Sering Library with modern architectural design and innovative layout, fosters a conducive learning environment by seamlessly integrating comfort, functionality, aesthetics to support students’ learning.
label: visit-of-the-commission-on-higher-education-regional-office-iii-at-gordon-colleges-cosme-y-sering-library

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/visit-of-the-commission-on-higher-education-regional-office-iii-at-gordon-colleges-cosme-y-sering-library/]
ronment by seamlessly integrating comfort, functionality, aesthetics to support students’ learning.

Dr. Imelda Soriano, College President, highlighted the library’s non-traditional interior, its creative approach to consolidating student research materials, and the institution’s e-library, which grants all students to a wealth of digital resources.

Dr. Lora Yusi, CHEDRO III, Director IV and Ms. Evangerline Tamayo, Project Technical Staff I, were in Gordon College on February 6, 2025.

# Mga aspiring freshie, humarap sa init ng pagsusulit

[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, March 6, 2025**

Hindi alintana ng mga Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT) examinee ang lagay ng panahon matapos suungin ang hamon ng pagsusulit kahapon, ika-5 ng Marso.

Tuloy-tuloy ang pagpapapasok ng Gordon College (GC) sa mga naghahangad na mapabilang sa mga iskolar ng dalubhasaan simula noong Lunes.

Ito ay sa kabila ng sunod-sunod na pagkansela ng mga klase sa mga karatig na lungsod dahil sa mataas na heat index
label: mga-aspiring-freshie-humarap-sa-init-ng-pagsusulit

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mga-aspiring-freshie-humarap-sa-init-ng-pagsusulit/]
a ng sunod-sunod na pagkansela ng mga klase sa mga karatig na lungsod dahil sa mataas na heat index habang nagtamo naman ang lungsod ng Olongapo ng index na tumuntong sa 40°C, isang araw bago ang pagsusulit.

Ang GCAT ay isa sa mga pangunahing hakbang upang makapasok sa institusyon. Ito ay binubuo ng dalawang parte: Otis-Lennon School Ability Test (OLSAT) at RAVEN Standard Progressive Matrices (SPM).

Tumatanggap ng 120 na mga examinee ang dalubhasaan tuwing araw ng pagsusulit na may dalawang sesyon: umaga at hapon. Tinataya namang 6,000 na ang nagparehistro sa GC Admission.

Inaasahang magpap
label: mga-aspiring-freshie-humarap-sa-init-ng-pagsusulit

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mga-aspiring-freshie-humarap-sa-init-ng-pagsusulit/]
syon: umaga at hapon. Tinataya namang 6,000 na ang nagparehistro sa GC Admission.

Inaasahang magpapatuloy ang pagtanggap ng institusyon ng mga GCAT taker tuwing Lunes, Miyerkules, at Biyernes sa susunod na mga araw.

# Gordon College Shortlisted for Times Higher Education Awards Asia 2025

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Thursday, January 23, 2025**

Gordon College has been named a finalist in the prestigious Times Higher Education (THE) Awards Asia 2025 under the Technological or Digital Innovation of the Year category. The shortlist, published on January 21, 2025, highlights Gordon College as the only local college from the Philippines to be recognized across in this category, standing out among major universities across Asia.

With its innovative entry, the Gordon College Learn
label: gordon-college-shortlisted-for-times-higher-education-awards-asia-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-shortlisted-for-times-higher-education-awards-asia-2025/]
nding out among major universities across Asia.

With its innovative entry, the Gordon College Learners’ Academic Management Portal (GCLAMP), the college joins seven other institutions from countries such as Thailand, Turkey, Singapore, Vietnam, and Hong Kong. Gordon College continues to trailblaze in innovation and excellence, bringing pride to the Philippines.

The winners will be announced during an awards ceremony in Macao on April 22, the opening day of THE’s Asia Universities Summit. This milestone not only underscores Gordon College’s commitment to driving transformative technological a
label: gordon-college-shortlisted-for-times-higher-education-awards-asia-2025

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-shortlisted-for-times-higher-education-awards-asia-2025/]
milestone not only underscores Gordon College’s commitment to driving transformative technological advancements in education but also reflects its vision to be a globally recognized local institution

# Level II BSN Students partake in the 24th GC’s Ceremony Rights

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, December 3, 2023**

The College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) held its annual 24th Capping, Badging, and Candle Lighting Ceremony for Level 2 Students under the Bachelor of Science in Nursing with the theme, “GC Nurses: Promoting Equity and Diversity in Nursing towards Universal Health Care,” on November 29, 2023, at Subic Bay Travellers Hotel.

Ms. Noreen A. Espedido, Chief Nurse of James L. Gordon Memorial Hospital and the keynote speaker of the event said, “M
label: level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights/]
dido, Chief Nurse of James L. Gordon Memorial Hospital and the keynote speaker of the event said, “My dear fellow registered nurses, we should envision as someone who renders quality care to different kinds of clients, regardless of their social situation and without having discrimination. Upon receiving, you are accepting the burden of the sick.” Receiving the cap and pin and lighting their candles is a symbol of their official initiation into entering the world of nursing.

Furthermore, as part of the ceremony, the nurses pledged themselves to the Florence Nightingale Pledge that signifies t
label: level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights/]
t of the ceremony, the nurses pledged themselves to the Florence Nightingale Pledge that signifies their devotion to the field of nursing officiated by Ms. Angelica Donasco, a clinical instructor portraying the mother of modern nursing.

Ms. Shantelle Resurrection, a Level 2 student, expressed her appreciation for the parents who attended. “We wouldn’t be here as official student nurses if it wasn’t for your unending love and support. Cheers to all of our hard work that pays off tonight!” she uttered.

Dr. Quimen presented 37 male and 90 female student nurses, summing up to 127 student nurses,
label: level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights/]
tered.

Dr. Quimen presented 37 male and 90 female student nurses, summing up to 127 student nurses, and was accepted by Prof. Pame, on behalf of the BOT Chairman and City Mayor, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. making them official student nurses who are ready to partake in clinical duties and settings.

The event was attended by the College Officials, led by the College President, Prof. Arlida M. Pame, the Board of Trustees (BOT), and City Officials. Along with them are the CAHS faculty members, part-time and full-time clinical instructors, led by Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., CAHS Dean. The candidates
label: level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/level-ii-bsn-students-partake-in-the-24th-gcs-ceremony-rights/]
-time and full-time clinical instructors, led by Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., CAHS Dean. The candidates, together with their parents and guardians, were warmly welcomed by Ms. Sheila Oconer, on behalf of the Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin Paguio.

As the event concluded, Ms. Anita Manalo, the Level 2 Coordinator, congratulated the candidates along with their parents and guardians for their efforts in making the event successful, including the student volunteers.

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, October 8, 2023**

𝘾𝙊𝙈𝙀𝘽𝘼𝘾𝙆 𝙄𝙎 𝙍𝙀𝘼𝙇—after a four-year hiatus, the 12th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games 2023 kicked off with the theme “Comeback stronger, dream higher,” at Laguna University (LU), Sta. Cruz, Laguna.

LU gives warm welcomes to student-athletes, coaches, and other participants from 22 local colleges and universities around the country.

Furthermore, a parade took place at the LU Grandstand, followed by the opening p

# In Solidarity with the LGBTQIA+ Community: Gordon College Joins the Pride March 2023

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, July 2, 2023**

JUNE 30, 2023—GCians proudly walked in the latest “Pride March, a culminating event for this year’s pride month, held in Olongapo City.

To them, this is not just an annual celebration but ultimately a movement against gender injustice and discrimination. Events such as the Pride March promote gender equality and inclusivity, which also serve as an avenue for the LGBTQIA+ community to be more recognized in society.

Last frida
label: in-solidarity-with-the-lgbtqia-community-gordon-college-joins-the-pride-march-2023

ich also serve as an avenue for the LGBTQIA+ community to be more recognized in society.

Last friday, GCians proved once again that they can make a difference in the lives of others by encouraging the youth to provoke alienation from people outside the LGBTQIA+ community.

This agenda rooted in philanthropic causes strongly supports the idea of Gordon College, ensuring solidarity within the LGBTQIA+ community by staying present, showing up, and spotlighting the unwavering spirit and incredible resiliency as shown by today’s LGBTQIA+ youth and allies in the institution.

Having that said, the

To our new Bachelor of Science in Information and Technology students! On behalf of the Empowered League of Information and Technology Education Students, ELITES community, welcome! All your journey here marks the start of something extraordinary. Get ready to learn, grow, and create lasting memories. Embrace every opportunity, connect with fellow students, and let your curiosity thrive!

# 𝐄𝐥𝐞𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐕𝐨𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬: 𝐖𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐌𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐖𝐚𝐯𝐞𝐬

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Monday, March 25, 2024**

In celebration of Women’s Month, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College Administrator, shared her insights on influential women, debunked stereotypes, and offered advice to aspiring young women.

To tell the tale as she reflects on her journey, Dr. Soriano highlighted two (2) women as pivotal figures who significantly impacted her life.

“The person I admire the most is Senator Miriam Defensor Santiago. I admire the braveness [and] the courage to speak kasi, ako rin, I speak out
label: %f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96/]
riam Defensor Santiago. I admire the braveness [and] the courage to speak kasi, ako rin, I speak out my voice as a public servant and administrator practitioner,” she said.

In addition, she also expressed her admiration for the selflessness of Mother Teresa and her urge to help who are in need.

“I want to help other people and it’s an open book that I also advocate helping yung mga oppressed and poor. As you grow older, you’re on the side of helping other people, especially when you are already in your mid-life,” Dr. Soriano added.

Meanwhile, regarding the misconception that ‘Women are weak
label: %f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96/]
y in your mid-life,” Dr. Soriano added.

Meanwhile, regarding the misconception that ‘Women are weak leaders’, she stressed that both men and women have equal ability when it comes to leadership.

“Of course, no. For example, ako — as a college administrator — I am a woman but I know that the capacity of men and women are the same [and] equal. It depends on the conviction of the leader to lead an organization and we have a women [college] president because we empowered ourselves,” the College Administrator stated.

Lastly, she gave an advice for young women and men who are embarking on a journ
label: %f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%84%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%af%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%95%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9c%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%96/]
ministrator stated.

Lastly, she gave an advice for young women and men who are embarking on a journey towards their goals in life, “First is they should know their goals: where to go and what do they want to achieve. [Second,] work hard and have your own principle and philosophy. If you have that, young women like us [and] also young men, if they have their own vision in achieving their goals and working hard on it, they can achieve [it].”

Dr. Soriano underlined that one’s socioeconomic background should never stop them from pursuing achievements because she also came from modest origins.

ound should never stop them from pursuing achievements because she also came from modest origins.

In an interview with the Forefront, Ceralde emphasized that she has not encountered discrimination throughout her leadership.

# CLINICAL GRADUATES CELEBRATE HARDWORK DURING THE AWARDING OF OUTSTANDING STUDENTS

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, August 9, 2023**

The awarding of outstanding students stunned the candidates for clinical graduates and immediately captured genuine reactions during the 21st Clinical Commencement of the Bachelor of Science in Nursing held on July 7, 2023 at Subic Bay Travelers Hotel & Event Center Inc., SBFZ.

Moreover, the awards were only announced on the same day and time allotted for the awarding, resulting to surprised faces and joyful tears from both
label: vision-mission-and-goals

same day and time allotted for the awarding, resulting to surprised faces and joyful tears from both students and parents. The purposive action to keep the awards confidential until its final announcement paid off with sincere expressions of gratitude. It was a fitting moment to celebrate hardwork.

Here are some of the following awards and awardees:

Pre-board Examination Ranking:

* 1st placer – Christian Glenn B. Laxa
* 2nd placer – Maricar L. Maggay
* 3rd placer – Herbert D. Ocampo Jr.
* 4th placer – Kristine Mae E. Damaso
* 5th placer – Princess G. Melchor

**Saturday, July 20, 2024**

We would like to congratulate the following individuals for their exceptional contributions to sustainable solutions and academic excellence at the recently conducted 2nd Nemesio Prudente International Research Conference, themed “Partners for Progress: Perspectives from the Academe, Industry, and Government for Sustainable Solutions.”

Ms. Jenny Mae Lagman (Faculty, CHTM-BSTM) was awarded Best

# GCians Stand-out in the Recent Conducted ALCUR3 2023

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, June 11, 2023**

Marko Aratea, a 4th year Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in English and a Literary/Editorial Correspondent of our publication, and Vince Sherna Cayabyab, a 4th year Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management, conquered the ALCUR3 Student Research Conference 2023 as he won the Outstanding Research Presenter award.

In an online interview with The Forefront, Aratea shared that the research was all about the relationship between vocabulary mastery and rea
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
t, Aratea shared that the research was all about the relationship between vocabulary mastery and reading comprehension among the students, which was their study when they were 3rd year students in the Language Education Research course.

He stated that having an enthusiastic presentation and confidence in their study helped him answer all questions instantly and achieve the said award.

“I was very grateful and happy, knowing that it will be such a great help for our program, now that it will be revisited for level 2 accreditation. Having this honor and trust given to me by our CEAS research c
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
 revisited for level 2 accreditation. Having this honor and trust given to me by our CEAS research coordinator and English program coordinator, I was very exhilarated that I was able to bring them and our institution the award and honor,” Aretea answered on how he felt after the awarding ceremony.

He also expressed his deep gratitude to his co-researcher, Ms. Cheryl Ara Placido, who helped him prepare for the competition. Also, the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Research Coordinator, Mr. Kristoffer Obispo, the English Program Coordinator, Ms. Catherine Mobo, and the Gordon Co
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
nator, Mr. Kristoffer Obispo, the English Program Coordinator, Ms. Catherine Mobo, and the Gordon College Research Development and Community Extension Services (GC RDCES) for the moral support and trust they gave them.

“The great advice that I can give to them is to know their research in every aspect of it and just enjoy the moment. You have nothing to be afraid of and you will not be nervous if you know your study even if there are lots of questions thrown at you. Also, be confident and enthusiastic. These will truly show that you know what you are talking about and you are happy to share t
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
siastic. These will truly show that you know what you are talking about and you are happy to share the fruitful result of your research study with everyone,” he added.

Meanwhile, Cayabyab presented a study called “Tourism Stakeholders’ Level of Acceptance towards Kalaklan Cemetery as a Dark Tourism Site.”

“Siguro, it’s the confidence and ‘yung direct to the point na presentation and pagsagot. I personally think na if you know your study well, you would be able to present it confidently, and since may time limit ang presentation and q&a dapat direct to the point lang. walang halo halo na stat
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
nce may time limit ang presentation and q&a dapat direct to the point lang. walang halo halo na statement,” she stated.

Cayabyab was very thankful to Sir Vincent De La Cruz for giving her the opportunity, to Ma’am Lovelyn Ceralde and Ma’am Kimberly Recitis, to those panels that shared some suggestions for our study during the Gordon College Research Forum, to her family, friends, groupmates, and to her partner for their support and help.

“The advice that I could give is to take your study seriously and “isapuso.” Hindi madali ang research. Trial and error s’ya. sobrang daming revisions, sobr
label: gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcians-stand-out-in-the-recent-conducted-alcur3-2023/]
ously and “isapuso.” Hindi madali ang research. Trial and error s’ya. sobrang daming revisions, sobrang daming suggestions, and sobrang daming kailangan “i-search.” Pero as long as you took everything seriously and tinake into consideration mo lahat, you will be able to present your study well. Be confident din, may times na mags-stutter ka talaga pero shake it off agad and proceed lang. Kahit may times na mag “uhm” ka proceed lang and be direct to the point,” Cayab advice for the incoming students that will represent Gordon College.

# The Forefront bags 19 awards; Secure tickets for LHEPC 2024

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, January 14, 2024**

The Forefront— Gordon College landed on 22nd Regional Higher Education Press Conference with the theme: “AI & Ethics: Shaping the Future of Campus Journalism in the Digital Age” as they victoriously pocketed several awards from multiple categories on January 11-13, 2024, at Arayat, Pampanga.

The Official Student Publication Unit of Gordon College dispatched 19 delegates to participate in the event and competed against 474 contenders from 34 campu
label: the-forefront-bags-19-awards-secure-tickets-for-lhepc-2024

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/the-forefront-bags-19-awards-secure-tickets-for-lhepc-2024/]
ispatched 19 delegates to participate in the event and competed against 474 contenders from 34 campus publications across the Region III. The delegates participated in 14 categories.

Before the 3-day event, Bealyn Demanalata, Editor-in-Chief (EIC) of The Forefront and the Newly Elected YJAR 3 Olongapo City Coordinator, said that bringing the delegates to the competition was quite a challenge as she took a lengthy period to process documents required to join the competition, which clashed with personal errands that resulted in time constraints.

Due to the obstacle, Demanalata was skeptical ab
label: the-forefront-bags-19-awards-secure-tickets-for-lhepc-2024

ersonal errands that resulted in time constraints.

Due to the obstacle, Demanalata was skeptical about the feasibility of joining the competition.

“Pagsubok lang ‘yan pero kinaya dahil sa suppport ng Office of the Student Welfare and Services, Ma’am Lovelyn, our finance, and our college president sa Forefront,” she said.

Furthermore, the editor-in-chief said that the competitive spirit among the delegates from different campuses was apparent since everyone was dedicated to “winning the gold,” which added to the challenges.

The Forefront also earned the 9th-highest pointer for individual contests,

Meanwhile, Demanalata expressed her delight for another milestone of the publication after presenting themselves on a bigger platform.

“Every time na tinatawag yung pangalan nila talagang tumatalon ako sa tuwa since we prayed, trained, and worked hard for th
label: the-forefront-bags-19-awards-secure-tickets-for-lhepc-2024

g yung pangalan nila talagang tumatalon ako sa tuwa since we prayed, trained, and worked hard for this,” the YJAR 3 Olongapo Coordinator stated.

On the other hand, she also added that the competition was an avenue to showcase what they can do and a testament that the publication can excel in every situation they will face.

Moreover, all delegates that won the 1st to 5th placements for each category are anticipated to compete at the Luzonwide Higher Education Press Conference in March 2024, At Naga City, Bicol Region.

# GC Arnis team, namayagpag: 21 medalya, nasungkit

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 13, 2023**

Hinirang bilang overall 1st runner up ang Gordon College Arnis team matapos magtala ng 7 gold, 9 silver, at 5 bronze sa katatapos lamang na LCUAA Arnis events na ginanap sa Gov. Felicisimo T. San Luis Memorial National High School Covered Court kahapon.

Gamit ang mapanindak na abilidad, nauwi ni Jherwin Estocado ang silver sa men’s middleweight division, habang tinanghal naman bilang gold medalist sa men’s superweight division si Emigdio Dacky Dacayana mata
label: gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit/]
ng tinanghal naman bilang gold medalist sa men’s superweight division si Emigdio Dacky Dacayana matapos pulbusin ang kalahok ng Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng San Pablo.

Upang dagdagan ang panalo, apat na magkakasunod na ginto ang nasungkit nina Ann Klein Ebilane, Aira Wilsa Bugayong, Mylyn Quiambao, at Jasmine Alliah Silvido para sa Bantamweight division, Featherweight division, Lightweight division, at Welterweight division, habang nakamit naman nina Crizel Biazon, at Issha Marie Ballonado ang silver sa women’s flyweight division, at cruiserweight division.

Naibulsa naman ng koponan ang ginto sa
label: gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit/]
er sa women’s flyweight division, at cruiserweight division.

Naibulsa naman ng koponan ang ginto sa Arnis Anyo women’s individual at team sword and dagger, habang pilak naman sa men’s individual double identical weapon, women’s individual single weapon, women’s individual double identical weapon, team single weapon, at team double identical weapon. Kabilang na rito si Eliza Aquino na nagkamit ng anim na medalya: gold para sa women’s individual at synchronized sword and dagger weapon, at silver para sa individual solo weapon, double identical weapon, synchronized solo, at double identical weap
label: gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-arnis-team-namayagpag-21-medalya-nasungkit/]
para sa individual solo weapon, double identical weapon, synchronized solo, at double identical weapon.

Wagi rin ng tansong medalya ang GC team sa men’s individual single weapon, men’s team single weapon, team double identical weapon, at team sword and dagger.

Samantala, naghari bilang overall champion ang Taguig City University sa naturang event matapos kumamada ng 15 gold, 4 silver, at 6 na bronze, habang pumangatlo naman ang Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa na nakakuha ng 15 kabuuang medalya.

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, May 5, 2024**

TINDIG Partylist muling nakuha ang mayorya sa ika-apat na sunod-sunod na taon sa kakatapos na Gordon College – Supreme Student Council (GC-SSC) Elections nitong Mayo 3, 2024.

Hindi gaya ng mga nakalipas na taon, hindi nila nakamit ang lahat ng posisyong inaasam sapagkat may dalawang nagtagumpay mula sa katunggaling T YO Partylist.

Narito ang naging resulta ng nasabing botohan:

**CHAIRPERSON:**

1. Mobo (TINDIG) – 1533

2. Ching (TÂYO) – 1288

**VICE CHAIRPERSON**

1. Ibasco (TÂYO)

Itinalaga namang **CCS REPRESENTATIVE** si Aaron Clark Perez dahil walang kandidato mula sa dalawang partido sa pwestong ito.

Sa kabuuan, nagtala naman ng 52.54% na boto mas mataas mula sa nakaraang taon na nagkaroon ng 51.94%.

Sa kabila nito, patuloy namang magbabantay ang The Forefront – Gordon College sa bagong administrasyon ng GC-SSC sa nalalapit na akademikong taong 2024-2025.

# GC-SSC VIRTUAL MDA 2024: Aspirants Emphasize Platforms

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, May 1, 2024**

Supreme Student Council (SSC) candidates from Tindig and Tayo Partylists persuade the Gordon College community through the Miting De Avance (MDA) held via Zoom and Facebook Live Stream on Tuesday, April 30, 2024.

“They will be your leaders. They will be your voice. They are the ones who are going to be on your side when you need them.” Ms. Lovelyn P. Ceralde, Vice President for the Office of the Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) mentioned in her openi
label: gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms/]
lde, Vice President for the Office of the Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) mentioned in her opening remarks.

Moreover, she added advice for the students of Gordon College (GC) to be wise and choose the candidates that they can trust to hold office in the student council.

As the program progressed, the Chairman of both parties gave their opening statements as they introduced their respective slate.

Furthermore, each aspirant was allowed to present and reiterate their platforms and plans for the institution. Hence, to encourage the GCians to vote for them.

Withal, the program moved forwar
label: gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms/]
 the institution. Hence, to encourage the GCians to vote for them.

Withal, the program moved forward to the open forum segment wherein questions from the GC students were gathered by the OSWS and College Student Electoral Board (CSEB) through the Google form link.

The questions put the candidates on a hot seat as they answered carefully in just two minutes.

The MDA lasted for three hours, ending with the closing remarks of outgoing SSC Chairperson Clarissa Mae Berja and the introduction of the election system developed by the Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT) students wit
label: gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ssc-virtual-mda-2024-aspirants-emphasize-platforms/]
e election system developed by the Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT) students with the lead developer Mr. Melner Balce.

The Forefront – Gordon College will continue to serve as a watchdog of every GCian in monitoring and scrutinizing the candidates in the upcoming GC-SSC Elections.

On the other hand, the election period is set for May 03, 2024, from 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM.

# Gordon College Participates in Central Luzon Higher Education Internationalization Conference

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, July 5, 2024**

Clark, Pampanga – Commission on Higher Education III (CHEDRO3) led by Dr. Lora L. Yusi, Director IV of CHEDRO3, spearheaded the Central Luzon Higher Education Internationalization Conference on June 27, 2024 at Hotel Seoul. The event centered on the theme “Higher Education Internationalization: Breaking Barriers Towards Fostering Quality Education in Central Luzon.” Gordon College OIC – College President, Dr. Imelda D
label: gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference/]
 Fostering Quality Education in Central Luzon.” Gordon College OIC – College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano and Ms. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Director for Institutional Planning, Development and Linkages participated in this important undertaking along with educators and administrators from across the region.

The morning session, titled “Setting the International Stage: Navigating Standards and Unveiling Global Horizons,” began with Dr. Rhodora I. Dela Rosa, Head of the University Graduate Program Office of Central Luzon State University. Dr. Dela Rosa gave an in-depth analysis of the standa
label: gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference/]
gram Office of Central Luzon State University. Dr. Dela Rosa gave an in-depth analysis of the standards and requirements set by international higher education analytics firms, emphasizing the importance of aligning local institutions with global benchmarks. Following her presentation, Dr. Parsons Noble Hail, Director of the International Affairs Office of Central Luzon State University, led a talk on effectively packaging applications for international rankings. His talk underscored the importance of strategic communication and presentation in securing a place in the global academic field.

In
label: gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference/]
ce of strategic communication and presentation in securing a place in the global academic field.

In the afternoon, the conference transitioned to “Exemplifying Best Practices from Success Stories and Building Pathways for Internationalization.” Representatives from Mabalacat City College, Tarlac State University, Bulacan State University, Nueva Ecija University of Science and Technology, and Tarlac Agricultural University shared their experiences and innovative approaches to internationalization. Their presentations offered valuable insights and practical strategies, highlighting the signific
label: gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-participates-in-central-luzon-higher-education-internationalization-conference/]
n. Their presentations offered valuable insights and practical strategies, highlighting the significance of learning from successful initiatives within the region. The conference concluded with Closing Message from Dr. John Wesley S. Calagui, Education Supervisor II at CHEDRO3.

Overall, the conference marked a pivotal step towards improving the global competitiveness of higher education institutions in Central Luzon. In line with the region’s commitment to advancing educational quality, the event was able to create a collaborative atmosphere where educators exchanged knowledge, experiences, a

# Pilak na Tipa ni Elefane

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, October 29, 2024**

MUNTINLUPA CITY–Tinarak ng Gordon College (GC) Warriors ang kanilang kampilan sa table tennis matapos makamit ang pilak ni X’tian Orrick Elefane sa men’s singles sa 13th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games nitong ika-23 ng Oktubre sa Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Muntinlupa (PLMun) Emerald Hall.

Namayagpag si Elefane sa konklusyon ng elimination rounds laban sa iba’t ibang kolehiyo at unibersidad. Matapos niyang umabante sa semifinals at ipamala
label: pilak-na-tipa-ni-elefane

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pilak-na-tipa-ni-elefane/]
ounds laban sa iba’t ibang kolehiyo at unibersidad. Matapos niyang umabante sa semifinals at ipamalas ang kaniyang galing at timpla, nilupig ni Elefane ang pambato ng City College of San Jose Del Monte (CCSJDM) na nagkaloob sa kaniya ng tiket tungong finals.

Samantala, nagulantang ang Emerald Hall sa bagsik ng dalawang manlalaro at dikit na labanan sa final round. Parehong nagaasam ng pagkawagi, ang dalawang atleta ay palitang nagpakawala ng atake hanggang sa naagaw ng Kolehiyo ng Lungsod ng Dasmariñas (KLD) ang ikaapat na set at naggawad kay Elefane ng silver medal sa nasabing kategorya.

Ba
label: pilak-na-tipa-ni-elefane

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/pilak-na-tipa-ni-elefane/]
smariñas (KLD) ang ikaapat na set at naggawad kay Elefane ng silver medal sa nasabing kategorya.

Bagamat baguhan sa kaniya ang paglalaro sa men’s singles ngayong taon, pinatunayan ni Elefane ang walang mintis na liksi at galing niya nang itanghal sa ikalawang puwesto sa bago ngunit pamilyar na larangan.

Sa kabilang banda, itinirik naman ng mandirigmang GC ang luntiang bandila sa lupain ng women’s division, mixed doubles, at men’s doubles patungong semis qualifying match.

————————
