# GC dons gold in LCUAA women’s volleyball

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, October 26, 2024**

“Last year, blessing na naging silver kami kasi we just prepared for just four days, (while) this year, mas mahaba ‘yong training program namin. ‘Yon lang naging difference ngayon kaya mas well-prepared ‘yong mga bata,” emphasized GC team coach Arthur Advincula on last year’s preparation for their silver finish to this year’s gold.

The Gordon College women’s volleyball team pulled off a big upset win against Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Pasig (PLP), 3-1, securing the
label: gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball/]
ball team pulled off a big upset win against Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Pasig (PLP), 3-1, securing the queen’s crown of the tournament of the 13th LCUAA National Games at Muntinlupa National High School (MNHS) gymnasium today.

GC team ruled first and second set after a dominating 25-21, 25-17 win led by 2023 LCUAA Best Middle Blocker Ronavelle Bersabe’s 8 markers in the sets on top of two blocks and Janah Mikyla Santos’ all around performance.

“Very matured na si Rona, alam niya na ‘yong responsibilities niya (as) one of the best middles last year, alam niya na walang kayang pumigil sa kaniya,
label: gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball/]
ibilities niya (as) one of the best middles last year, alam niya na walang kayang pumigil sa kaniya,” mentioned Advincula on Bersabe’s performance in the finals.

Although PLP stained GC’s sweep in the third set of the finals, humbling them a bit, 17-25, and edging them after a 0-6 run in the latter part of the fourth set, the 2023 LCUAA first-runners proved themselves to be champion materials and showed GC’s greener than PLP in the finals, 25-22.

Advincula also underscored Roxzeth Dizon’s leadership to the team as the captain and one of the gunners of the team.

“Last year, may nagma-manage
label: gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball/]
ership to the team as the captain and one of the gunners of the team.

“Last year, may nagma-manage para sa team. Ngayon, siya ‘yong team captain, naiintindihan niya ‘yong ginawa naming set-up sa team na maglaro lang siya kasi may katulong na (siya),” the coach said.

Going into the finals, the ladies in green had a flawless 2-0 in the elims, topping group B, and secured the finals ticket after winning against Taguig City University (TCU) in the semifinals.

Although it was flawless, it was not all picture perfect as the team had to deal with bad weather condition and limited practice time.

“
label: gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-dons-gold-in-lcuaa-womens-volleyball/]
all picture perfect as the team had to deal with bad weather condition and limited practice time.

“Challenging ‘yong weather, apektado ‘yong mga bata. Na-postpone sila na umabot ng two days, gusto kong magpa-ensayo pero lagi kaming nauunahan sa court, hindi sila nakapagpapawis ng dalawang araw,” he said.

On the other hand, PLP cut off last year’s champion’s Tangub City Global College (TCGC) run in the semis of the women’s volleyball tournament.

Fourteen local colleges and universities all over the country participated in this year’s national tournament.

# GC CCS Acquaintance Day 2023: A Celebration of Talents and Achievements

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 6, 2023**

Gordon College effectuated an acquaintance fete referred to as “GCCCS Acquaintance Day 2023” with a theme “KiLiTi ng CCS: Kislap. Likha. Tindig,” where students from the College of Computer Studies (CCS) gathered and celebrated with merriment as they collected mementos and engaged with booths, programs, and activities on September 29, 2023, at Marikit Park, Olongapo City.

Students took delight in a celebration that exhibited every aspe
label: gc-ccs-acquaintance-day-2023-a-celebration-of-talents-and-achievements

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ccs-acquaintance-day-2023-a-celebration-of-talents-and-achievements/]
3, at Marikit Park, Olongapo City.

Students took delight in a celebration that exhibited every aspect of their distinct abilities. A dance competition, fashion show, karaoke, and cosplay competition were some of the features of the said event. Also, students got the chance to participate in the excitement by playing the “Bring Me” game.

Meanwhile, the event also awarded the top ten CCS students on the Dean’s List to validate their outstanding accomplishments and academic excellence.

This acquaintance day was made possible by the integration of the CCS programs, including Bachelor of Science
label: gc-ccs-acquaintance-day-2023-a-celebration-of-talents-and-achievements

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ccs-acquaintance-day-2023-a-celebration-of-talents-and-achievements/]
aintance day was made possible by the integration of the CCS programs, including Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (BSCS), Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT), and Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Multimedia Computing (BSEMC). The Programs also collaborated with GCCCS Specs, GCCCS Images, GCCCS Elites, CCS Student Council, and EMC studio booths such as Queuebeta Ent, Caballeros Studio, Quaja, and Millenium Flames Studio.

# GC’s 21ST COMMENCEMENT EXERCISES AND THE 2ND FACE-TO-FACE GRADUATION SINCE 2020

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, September 1, 2023**

Gordon College’s 21st Commencement Exercises with the theme “Imbuiding Mindful Intelligence and Gratitude Mindset for Greater Success,” held on July 14, 2023, at the Subic Bay Exhibition and Convention Center (SBFZ), is the 2nd in-person graduation ceremony since the pandemic started.

The event started with the procession of flags and colors, the entrance of the candidates for graduation, faculty members, college officials, t
label: gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020/]
ags and colors, the entrance of the candidates for graduation, faculty members, college officials, the board of trustees, and lastly, the commencement speaker.

Followed by a prayer led by Ms. Lizette Ann P. Sia, Program Coordinator of BSTM, the singing of the National Anthem, the Olongapo Hymn, and the Gordon College Hymn.

Prof. Arlida M. Pame, College President, gave her sincere opening remarks, while Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College Administrator, also graced the floor as she delivered a heartfelt special message to the graduates.

The event continued with the presentation of a total of 1,3
label: gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020/]
tfelt special message to the graduates.

The event continued with the presentation of a total of 1,359 graduates, led by Mr. Rowel Y. Castillo, College Registrar. Then followed by the acceptance and confirmation, and an inspirational message by Hon. Atty. Rolen C. Paulino, City Mayor and Chairman of the Board of Trustees.

Followed by the introduction of the commencement speaker, Atty. Ryan L. Estevez, DPA, Executive Director of UNIFAST, was officiated by Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., Dean of the College of Allied Health Studies. Then the awarding of a plaque and token to the guest speaker was ass
label: gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020/]
llege of Allied Health Studies. Then the awarding of a plaque and token to the guest speaker was assigned to Hon. Paulino Jr., Prof. Pame, and Dr. Soriano.

The distribution of diplomas, awarding of academic honors, and special awards were led by Hon. Paulino, Prof. Pame, Dr. Soriano, Dr. Paguio, and college deans, while the reading of the graduates was designated to the program coordinators.

Graduation Speech by Ms. Shaula Mae P. Estocado, BACOMM, Magna Cum Laude, Pledge of Loyalty officiated by Ms. Everly Stecel R. Bayog, President of the Graduating Class 2023, and the Alumni Oath led by Mr
label: gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcs-21st-commencement-exercises-and-the-2nd-face-to-face-graduation-since-2020/]
by Ms. Everly Stecel R. Bayog, President of the Graduating Class 2023, and the Alumni Oath led by Mr. Michael P. Tuyay, President of Gordon College Alumni Association.

Graduates then enjoyed their graduation song while looking back on their journey through a visual presentation, followed by the closing remarks by Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and lastly, a closing prayer by Ms. Rowenalyn P. Sagun, Program Coordinator of BSED-Filipino. The event was concluded by a recessional ceremony.

**Thursday, February 22, 2024**

Gordon College, situated in the heart of the bustling city, marked a significant milestone with a vibrant celebration honoring 25 years of academic excellence and community spirit.

The institution’s founding anniversary event transformed the tranquil campus into an energetic hub, where students, faculty, alumni, and guests gathered beneath the starlit sky to revel in the festivities.

The night commenced with a palpabl

As the crowd eagerly anticipated the pinnacle of the celebration, December Avenue, renowned icons of Original Pinoy Music (OPM), took center stage. With a captivating repertoire; “Kahit Di Mo Alam”, “Bulong”, “Dahan”, “Eroplanong Papel”, “Huling Sandali”, “Kung ‘Di Rin Lang Ikaw”, ” Saksi ang Langit”, and “Sa Ngalan Ng P

planong Papel”, “Huling Sandali”, “Kung ‘Di Rin Lang Ikaw”, ” Saksi ang Langit”, and “Sa Ngalan Ng Pagibig” the band guided the audience on a poignant musical journey, evoking a range of emotions and connecting across generations.

December Avenue’s performance went beyond a typical concert; it served as a powerful declaration of resilience, showcasing the indomitable spirit embedded in Filipino culture.

# Gravanzo at Cambi, Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Buwan ng Wika

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, September 3, 2024**

Kinoronahan ang mga bagong Lakan at Lakambini nitong ika-30 ng Agosto kasabay ng panapos na selebrasyon ng Buwan ng Wika 2024 na may temang “Wikang Filipino: Wikang Mapagpalaya” na idinaos sa Gordon College Function Hall sa pangunguna ng Samahang Filipino (SamFil).

Matapos ang masusing pagsusuri ng mga hurado, kinoronahan ang mga sumusunod:

Para sa Lakan at Lakambini ng Buwan ng Wika 2024, nagwagi sina G. Ivan Tristan Gravanzo mula sa Kolehi

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika/]
ra sa Lakan at Lakambini ng Buwan ng Wika 2024, nagwagi sina G. Ivan Tristan Gravanzo mula sa Kolehiyo ng Edukasyon, Sining, at Agham (CEAS) at Bb. Christel C. Cambi ng Kolehiyo ng Turismo at Pagkalinga (CHTM).

Nanalo naman bilang Lakan at Lakambini ng Wika sina G. Ira Abrigo mula sa Kolehiyo ng Kawaksing Pag-aaral sa Kompyuter (CCS) at si Bb. Antonette Tirona mula sa Kolehiyo ng Kawaksing Pag-aaral sa Medisina (CAHS).

Namayagpag din bilang Lakan at Lakambini ng Panitik sina G. Justin Orlanda ng Kolehiyo ng Kawaksing Pag-aaral sa Medisina (CAHS) at si Bb. Jemarie Sangcap mula sa Kolehiyo ng
label: gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika/]
ng Kolehiyo ng Kawaksing Pag-aaral sa Medisina (CAHS) at si Bb. Jemarie Sangcap mula sa Kolehiyo ng Edukasyon, Sining, at Agham (CEAS).

Nakamit naman nina G. Jeero Ceneze at Bb. Kryssel Anne Divina, parehong mula sa Kolehiyo ng Pangangalakal at Pagtutuos (CBA) ang titulo bilang Lakan at Lakambini ng Sining.

Sa huli, itinanghal bilang Lakan at Lakambini ng Samfil sina G. Andrei Sydney Albarda mula sa Kolehiyo ng Turismo at Pagkalinga (CHTM) at Bb. Jasmine Anne Espartero mula sa Kolehiyo ng Kawaksing Pag-aaral sa Kompyuter (CCS).

Ayon kay G. Roy De Vera, taga-payo ng SamFil, ang pangunahing l
label: gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika/]
ksing Pag-aaral sa Kompyuter (CCS).

Ayon kay G. Roy De Vera, taga-payo ng SamFil, ang pangunahing layunin ng Buwan ng Wika ay upang bigyang-pagkilala ang ating wika at maisulong ang paggamit nito sa iba’t ibang larangan.

“Sa pamamagitan ng pagsasagawa ng iba’t ibang gawain ay nahuhubog or nasusubok ‘yong mga kakayanan ng mga mag-aaral sa ating dalubhasaan,” aniya.

Samantala, ibinahagi naman ni G. Kharl Christopher Nofies, Pangulo ng organisasyon, ang kanyang mensahe sa mga nagwagi kung saan ay hinikayat niya ang mga ito na gamitin ang kanilang titulo upang mas mapagyabong ang wikang Filipin
label: gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika/]
y hinikayat niya ang mga ito na gamitin ang kanilang titulo upang mas mapagyabong ang wikang Filipino.

“Gamitin iyong mga katungkulan, maging ang titulo nila para mas mapagyabong o mapagyaman ang ating wikang Filipino. Kasi sa paglipas ng panahon, iyong titulo nawawala rin naman ‘yan,” pahayag niya.

Para kay G. Ivan Tristan Gravanzo, Lakan 2024, ang pagkapanalo bilang Lakan ay isang malaking sorpresa.

“Personally, hindi ko in-expect na mananalo ako. Pero, it is what it is. Unang-una, maraming salamat kay Lord na binigay niya ‘tong award na ‘to,” pahayag niya.

Samantala, ibinahagi naman ni
label: gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gravanzo-at-cambi-bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-buwan-ng-wika/]
 salamat kay Lord na binigay niya ‘tong award na ‘to,” pahayag niya.

Samantala, ibinahagi naman ni Bb. Christel C. Cambi, Lakambini 2024, hindi niya inakala na siya ang magwawagi.

“At first hindi ko talaga in-expect na mananalo kasi grabe rin ‘yung laban ng Department ng CAHS. Kaya ‘yung laban sa aming dalawa, feeling ko hindi siya gano’n kahigpit [ang laban],” saad niya.

Bukod sa Lakan at Lakambini 2024, pinarangalan din ang ilang estudyante na nanalo sa ibang patimpalak ng organisasyon tulad ng Likhawit, Tatafil, Malikhaing Pagguhit, Islogan, Husanaysay, at MakaTanghal.

: Princess Alliya

**Thursday, August 31, 2023**

# Barbeque-mikinang ng Subic Zambales: Tinatawag muli ng Tanghalan

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, November 15, 2024**

Ibahin mo si Jade Benero Cayosa sa mga konteserang nakilala mo; dahil bumaba man siya sa “It’s Showtime” stage noong 2019 na hindi hawak ang gintong-mikropono, hindi pa sintunado ang lahat upang umawit muli ng sariling rendisyon ng kaniyang himig-sikan sa ika-dalawang edisyon ng Tawag ng Tanghalan: School Showdown Season 1.

Kadalasan mong maririnig ang boses ng balladeer sa Gordon College. Bukod sa maraming organisasyon at mga patimpalak na
label: barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan/]
irinig ang boses ng balladeer sa Gordon College. Bukod sa maraming organisasyon at mga patimpalak na ang kaniyang nasalihan tulad ng Battle of the Bands, sa pangalawang pagkakatao’y hindi lamang iwa-wagayway ni Cayosa ang kaniyang apilyedo – pati na rin ang bandera ng dalubhasaan.

Kahit sa rurok nito, maaring hindi pa rin klaro ang pangalan ng mag-aaral mula BCAEd, pinuri lang naman siya ni Meme Vice ng “ang clear ng voice mo” sa unang salang niya sa TNT Stage. Higit sa lahat, saan mang panig sa Olongapo at Subic, malinaw rin na makapigil-hininga ang kaniyang pagbirit upang makamit ang iba’t
label: barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan/]
a Olongapo at Subic, malinaw rin na makapigil-hininga ang kaniyang pagbirit upang makamit ang iba’t ibang titulo sa larangan ng pagkanta.

Kaya naman noong 2019, sa kaunting kabig ng dibdib na gamitin ang Hashtag TNT Refer, inulan ng suporta ang kaniyang isang-minutong English-Tagalog pyesa na naging Golden Ticket niya upang kantahin ang “Dukha” ng Bandang Aegis sa Tawag ng Tanghalan Stage noong Ika-3 ng Pebrero, 2020.

“I work on practicing sa mga kanta na nilagay ko sa list ng kakantahin ko, and hanggang saan yung strength and weaknesses ko. In the past, kasi, wala pa akong masyadong na-aral
label: barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan/]
nd hanggang saan yung strength and weaknesses ko. In the past, kasi, wala pa akong masyadong na-aral na kanta. And, disiplina rin: I need to be careful sa mga pwedeng makasira sa vocal[s] ko,” saad ng GCian nang tinanong ukol sa kaniyang pagbabalik.

Marahil dumating man ang oras na mawalan ng boses si Jade, hindi naman mawawala sa kaniyang bar line ang mga taong pinanghuhugutan niya ng lakas upang hindi magpahinga at manatiling tahimik ang ritmo ng kaniyang talento.

“Inaalay ko ito sa mga taong hindi sumusuko na mangarap and magpatuloy na sumubok muli kahit na nalulunod tayo sa problema na k
label: barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/barbeque-mikinang-ng-subic-zambales-tinatawag-muli-ng-tanghalan/]
g hindi sumusuko na mangarap and magpatuloy na sumubok muli kahit na nalulunod tayo sa problema na kinakaharap,” palaging baon ni Jade para sa mga manonood at mapapanood siyang muli.

Mapapakinggan kung paano rumesbak ang nag-iisa at patuloy pa rin na Barbequ-mikinang ng Subic mamayang 12:00pm sa Tawag ng Tanghalan Stage ng It’s Showtime.

# R.A. 11313 and R. A 7877: Prevention of sexual abuse

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, December 3, 2024**

For the first time this year, the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Unit, in partnership with the Human Resource Management Unit, conducted a seminar discussing R.A. 11313 or the Safe Spaces Act and R.A. 7877 or the Anti-sexual Harassment Act held at the GC Function Hall, November 29.

Its aim is to raise awareness to GC personnels regarding such laws, because sexual abuse may happen within the profession that they are not aware of is already doing or ex
label: r-a-11313-and-r-a-7877-prevention-of-sexual-abuse

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/r-a-11313-and-r-a-7877-prevention-of-sexual-abuse/]
ause sexual abuse may happen within the profession that they are not aware of is already doing or experiencing.

These topics were discussed by Michelle B. Ubuta, Police Senior Master Sergeant (PSMS) from Olongapo City Police Station 3 – Women and Children Protection Desk.

In her first discussion, she highlighted acts of gender-based sexual harassment, both online and physically.

Furthermore, he also explained that sexual abuse has no age limit and sometimes it is those in charge and high positions who commit acts against such laws.

“The key for building safety in our community is respect”
label: r-a-11313-and-r-a-7877-prevention-of-sexual-abuse

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/r-a-11313-and-r-a-7877-prevention-of-sexual-abuse/]
tions who commit acts against such laws.

“The key for building safety in our community is respect” promise of Mr. Joseph A. Atencio, OIC-Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Unit, in his closing remarks.

He added, knowledge needs to be added and such a program is one of the methods they see.

: Emar Kristian Aranda | Opinion-Editorial Correspondent

# Takbo para sa Guro 2023: IHK’s Fun Run Event Sparks Passion for Discipline and Fitness

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Sunday, October 22, 2023**

Hundreds of students and teachers from across the city laced up their sneakers and took to the streets for a fun run on October 22, 2023. The event, organized by the GC-IHK faculty was a celebration of teachers’ dedication to their students and the community.

The run kicked off at Tapinac Oval at 6:17 a.m. with a warm-up zumba exercise led by various students of Gordon College. Participants then set off on 5K course, wi
label: takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness/]
zumba exercise led by various students of Gordon College. Participants then set off on 5K course, winding their way through the heart of Olongapo City Proper.

Along the way, teachers were cheered on by students, community members, and other participants. The fun run was more than just a physical challenge. It was also a chance for teachers to come together and celebrate their profession. The mood was electrifying as the runners triumphantly reached the finish line.

Mica Faustino, a spirited participant who completed the race with a remarkable time of 14 minutes and 53 seconds, emphasized the
label: takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness/]
rticipant who completed the race with a remarkable time of 14 minutes and 53 seconds, emphasized the joy and fulfillment that comes with pursuing one’s passion. “Inaalala ko lang ‘yong turo ng coach namin habang tumatakbo kami, na kailangan may disiplina ka sa lahat ng bagay,” she expressed.

On the other hand, JB Piodo, with a notable time of 11 minutes and 22 seconds, shared the challenges of balancing academic responsibilities with sports commitments. “Unang-una ‘yong sa hindi naman maiiwasan minsan yung mahirap sa school [academics], mahirap pag sabayin yung school [academics] at ‘yong spo
label: takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness/]
 minsan yung mahirap sa school [academics], mahirap pag sabayin yung school [academics] at ‘yong sports. ‘Yon ‘yung isa sa mga challenges na kinakaharap ko ngayon,” JB noted. “Masasabi ko lang syempre, unang-una disiplina. Pangalawang, sumunod sa mga training. At siyempre, pangatlo, manalangin para sa sarili at para sa pamilya. Tapos huwag pa rin kakalimutan yung pag aaral, first priority pa rin ‘yon. Second option na lang ‘yong sports pero kung kaya mong ituloy, then go lang.”

Meanwhile, Mr. Duclayan, P.E. Head Teacher at IHK, discussed the key goals of the event and stressed the significanc
label: takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness/]
uclayan, P.E. Head Teacher at IHK, discussed the key goals of the event and stressed the significance of promoting physical well-being among students. “Ang key goals ay siyempre ‘yong mga students natin to be physically fit. At the same time maging aware sila sa mga sports, and activities sa school,” he emphasized. Additionally, the fun run event, an integral part of GC-IHK’s Physical Education program, served as a stepping stone toward achieving these overarching objectives.

The resounding success of “Takbo Para sa Guro,” raised a huge amount for the recent teacher’s day celebration. But mor
label: takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/takbo-para-sa-guro-2023-ihks-fun-run-event-sparks-passion-for-discipline-and-fitness/]
cess of “Takbo Para sa Guro,” raised a huge amount for the recent teacher’s day celebration. But more importantly, this event was a reminder of the important role that teachers play in our community.

# Gordon College secures its place in LCUAA National Game 2023

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 13, 2023**

In display of athletic prowess and unwavering determination, GC has emerged with the most number of medals at the highly competitive Local Colleges and Universities-Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games 2023, which was participated in by 22 LUCs across the country!

From October 8 to 13, 2023, at the Laguna University in Sta. Cruz, Laguna, GC unleashed extraordinary performance, clinching a staggering 37 golds, 24 silvers, and 21 bronzes!
label: gordon-college-secures-its-place-in-lcuaa-national-game-2023

 unleashed extraordinary performance, clinching a staggering 37 golds, 24 silvers, and 21 bronzes!

This achievement stands as a testament to the unyielding spirit and collective dedication of the entire Gordon College community — from the athletes and coaches to the college personnel. # Ikatlong pwesto, nakopo ng GC Women’s Futsal

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Wednesday, October 11, 2023**

Bigo mang makamit ang ginto, siniguro pa rin ng Gordon College Women’s Futsal team na sa kanila pa rin isasabit ang tansong medalya matapos dispatsahin ang iba pang katunggali sa katatapos na laro kahapon, ika-10 ng Oktubre.  
Naka-ungos man ang kalaban sa unang bakbakan, hindi pa rin nagpatinag ang koponan sa pangunguna ng team captain na si Clarisse Kyla Dagoon kahit naabot na ang kaniyang “last playing year” upang silatin ang ikatlong pwesto at umani ng pa
label: %f0%9d%99%84%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c-%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%ac%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4-%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%96

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%99%84%f0%9d%99%a0%f0%9d%99%96%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a1%f0%9d%99%a4%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%9c-%f0%9d%99%a5%f0%9d%99%ac%f0%9d%99%9a%f0%9d%99%a8%f0%9d%99%a9%f0%9d%99%a4-%f0%9d%99%a3%f0%9d%99%96/]
on kahit naabot na ang kaniyang “last playing year” upang silatin ang ikatlong pwesto at umani ng parangal.  
“As a captain lagi kong mino-motivate yung team ko na kaya nila kahit na nafi-feel nilang hindi na nila kaya. Tiwala lang po ang naging preparasyon namin para makamit namin ‘yung panalo”, pahayag ni Dagoon sa panayam bitbit ang medalya at aral matapos ang laro.

# GC paddlers prevail, snatches gold, bronze in Table Tennis event

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Friday, October 13, 2023**

Table Tennis team secured more awards for Gordon College after winning 1 gold and 1 bronze medals and escaping the powerful Taguig City University at the recently held single and mixed doubles event yesterday.

𝙅𝙖𝙘𝙠 𝘾𝙤𝙨𝙩𝙖𝙡𝙚𝙨 earned his bronze for the Men’s single event, while 𝙓’𝙩𝙞𝙖𝙣 𝙀𝙡𝙚𝙛𝙖𝙣𝙚 and 𝙅𝙪𝙡𝙞𝙖𝙣𝙖 𝙋𝙖𝙮𝙪𝙢𝙤 dominated the mixed doubles event after targetting the oponent with their blazing spins and backhand shots to earn the gold medal.

# Project WHARF 2024: giving back to CHTM Alumni community

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, August 27, 2024**

Last August 21, 2024, the Alumni Association of College of Hospitality and Tourism Management – Gordon College (AAC-GC) conducts a job fair activity entitled Project WHARF 2024: Workforce Hiring and Recruitment Fair at Gordon College Function Hall, which aims to give back to the CHTM Alumni community by finding job opportunities aligned for them.

To begin with, Ms. Jenny Mae Lagman, the president of CHTM Alumni Association gave opening remarks with
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
egin with, Ms. Jenny Mae Lagman, the president of CHTM Alumni Association gave opening remarks with a warm greeting welcome the participants to the job fair.

In partnership with the I’ve Productions of CHTM, Olongapo City Public Employment Service Office (PESO), Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE), Department of Migrant Workers (DMW), and Work Abroad, around 19 participating industries have joined the workforce hiring fair.

𝗜𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝘀 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗮𝗰𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗺𝗲

Meanwhile, Mr. Mario Esquillo, the head of Olongapo City PESO, stated that they truly support this kind of school activity.

Mr. Esquill
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
ead of Olongapo City PESO, stated that they truly support this kind of school activity.

Mr. Esquillo also said that the employment will be easier because it started from the academe itself.

“Truly support lang po kami at naniniwala kami pag laging ganito po ang mga eskwelahan, madali po ang employment. Kasi directly from the academe, mula sa kanilang pagtuturo diretso na sa pag i-employ,” the head of PESO stated.

He also encouraged the attendees that they must not lose the chance to grab the opportunity to know the employers and make sure to utilize it to establish their network.

In an int
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
opportunity to know the employers and make sure to utilize it to establish their network.

In an interview with The Forefront, Ms. Patricia Ann Ecalnir, the event manager of I’ve Production, said that this event will benefit the participants especially those alumni that are looking for job opportunities including fresh graduates.

According to Ms. Ecalnir, a total of 80 participants from alumni and an estimated 120 participants from the students they invited attended the said job fair.

In another interview with the student publication, Mr. Benjamin Florencio Jr., the Labor Market Information
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
er interview with the student publication, Mr. Benjamin Florencio Jr., the Labor Market Information (LIM) officer of PESO Olongapo said that the available job vacancies include hospitality, food industry, hotel, and resource staff.

Additionally, there are offerings abroad for food service staff and skilled workers.

Moreover, the LIM officer of PESO said that the participating industries are also in need of accounting staff , an in-demand job nowadays.

The PESO officer also mentioned that some fresh graduates want a job that is aligned with their degree in order to avoid job mismatch. He als
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
 fresh graduates want a job that is aligned with their degree in order to avoid job mismatch. He also pointed out that it is one of the reasons why there is unemployment among the fresh graduates.

𝗚𝗿𝗮𝗯 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗼𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝘂𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲

Additionally, Mr. Florencio also gave advice for the first time job seekers to grab the available opportunities to have work background.

“Para magkaroon sila kahit experience man lang, [kaunting] experience. Para, ‘pag nakita nila yung position na target nila, meron na silang work experience na pwede ng i-support para dun sa [pag-apply] nila doon sa preferred
label: project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/project-wharf-2024-giving-back-to-chtm-alumni-community/]
eron na silang work experience na pwede ng i-support para dun sa [pag-apply] nila doon sa preferred position nila na related na doon sa pinag-aralan,” he stated.

Lastly, he also said that this partnership of Gordon College with PESO is a good start because their office is also planning to put up Job Placement Offices (JPO) in different schools within Olongapo, like GC, to make the schools be capacitated and be easier for students to find job opportunities after graduation.

# Mr. Ms. and Mx. ECONS: Promoting confidence and power thru English Language

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Saturday, November 25, 2023**

Students of BSEd-English gathered at Gordon College PE Hall to witness the most-aticipated Mr. Ms. and Mx. ECONS initiated by the League of English Language and Literature Students (LELLS) as part of the English Month 2023 celebration last November 18.

The event was hosted by John Christian J. Rocero and Sherie Bohol, the Public Information Officers of LELLS, and spearheaded by Aeros Morales, the Auditor of LELLS.

Candidates f
label: mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language/]
Information Officers of LELLS, and spearheaded by Aeros Morales, the Auditor of LELLS.

Candidates from the Bachelor of Secondary Education – Major in English have competed for the title of Mr. Ms. and Mx. ECONS 2023.

Mr. Lucky Uel Magrata was hailed as Mr. ECONS 2023, Ms. Crisheille Naol hailed as Ms. ECONS 2023, and Mx. Marky Doctama was hailed as Mx. ECONS 2023.

Aside from the title, there are also special awards such as Best Polaroid (Most Facebook reactions), Best Style (best outfit from the photoshoot and contest proper), Best Wonder (candidate with the biggest audience impact), and Mo
label: mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language/]
the photoshoot and contest proper), Best Wonder (candidate with the biggest audience impact), and Most Photogenic (combined scores from Best Style and Best Wonder category) for this year’s contest.

BEST POLAROID

– Mr. Marcio Sadaichi Misuhura

– Ms. Crisheille Naol

– Mx. Albert Serrano

BEST STYLE:

– Mr. Arjay Guinto

– Ms. Crisheille Naol

– Mx. Mark Anthony Doctama

BEST WONDER: Ms. Crisheille Naol

MOST PHOTOGENIC: Ms. Crisheille Naol

In an interview with the LELLS President, Gabrielle Bulda, she said that the contest aims to promote confidence and power thru English Language.

“ECONS
label: mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language/]
ulda, she said that the contest aims to promote confidence and power thru English Language.

“ECONS is actually a shortcut word for English Icons, meaning, this event’s purpose is to really encourage English Majors to be an icon they already are. It’s also to promote confidence and power thru the English language. We also have plans to make this event per college candidates next year. ” she said.

She also said that she wants the students to enjoy their stay at Gordon College by conducting activities like these.

“Amidst the academic pressure and obligations, I want the students to have a grea
label: mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language/]
ities like these.

“Amidst the academic pressure and obligations, I want the students to have a great time and enjoy their college years thru activities like these. After all, they are still learning about English as they join the different activities under English Month. As well as for the ECONS, it promotes camaraderie and teamwork. Students coming together to support their classmates and just really have a great time during their stay in Gordon College.” Bulda said.

Meanwhile, she said that she considered this event as a successful one and shared what she felt in conducting this event of t
label: mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mr-ms-and-mx-econs-promoting-confidence-and-power-thru-english-language/]
she considered this event as a successful one and shared what she felt in conducting this event of their organization.

“Not only me but the whole org knows it was successful the minute we processed the letters for this event. Unlike last year, it was still successful thru virtual means but this year was really a blast and a great development from last year’s. My heart is still full at the moment and I am still overwhelmed in a good way.” she stated.

The League of English Language and Literature Students (LELLS) is the official student organization of the Bachelor of Secondary Education – Maj

# GC-CEAS Conducts Annual Convocation Ceremony

[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)

**Tuesday, December 19, 2023**

Gordon College – College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) graduating students participate in its 16th Convocation: Pinning and Candle Lighting Ceremony held at SM Olongapo Central Convention Center on December 12, 2023, with the theme “Empowering Student Teachers: Navigating the Evolving Landscape of Education.”

Dr. Darwin Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs and the CEAS OIC-Dean, presented a total of 268 student teachers coming from different p
label: gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony/]
mic Affairs and the CEAS OIC-Dean, presented a total of 268 student teachers coming from different programs; 14 from BCAEd, 15 from BECEd, 40 from BEEd, 37 from BPEd, 44 from BSEd-English, 34 from BSEd-Filipino, 19 from BSEd-Mathematics, 21 from BSEd-Science, and 44 from BSEd-Social Studies

Meanwhile, Ms. Arlene E. Dela Rea, Senior Education Program Specialist from the Department of Education (DepEd) and the event’s keynote speaker, praised the stellar performance of Gordon College, achieving an impressive 83.95% passing rate in secondary education and 81.84% in elementary education. Ms. Dela
label: gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony/]
n impressive 83.95% passing rate in secondary education and 81.84% in elementary education. Ms. Delarea emphasized the pride and support of the Department of Education for such accomplishments, recognizing them as significant milestones, especially for first-takers.

Furthermore, she emphasized one of the critical components for the pre-service teachers, mentorship. She acknowledged and extended her gratitude to the school principals and cooperating teachers for their guidance and unwavering support to the 268 student teachers.

Moreover, Ms. Dela Rea wishes for a 100% passing rate on the PRC
label: gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony/]
port to the 268 student teachers.

Moreover, Ms. Dela Rea wishes for a 100% passing rate on the PRC license exam next year, the stage was set for these graduates to embark on their noble journey of empowering minds and shaping futures, leaving an indelible mark that transcends the confines of the classroom.

After the pinning and candle lighting, Mr. Vrixter Stanley Gatbunton, a graduating student from BSEd-Mathematics and the President of CEAS Graduating Class 2023-2024 led the Student Teacher’s Pledge.

The culmination of the event was concluded by the closing remarks from Mr. Leby Fastidio,
label: gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony

content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-ceas-conducts-annual-convocation-ceremony/]
s Pledge.

The culmination of the event was concluded by the closing remarks from Mr. Leby Fastidio, who extended heartfelt congratulations to the pre-service teachers for their dedication and hard work.

: Carlos Angelo N. Agustin | News Correspondents

John Christian J. Rocero | Radio Broadcaster

: Alejandrian Rodavia | Photojournalist

Gordon College’s 25th Anniversary celebration kicked off in grand style last February 21st, with the coronation of the new Mr. and Ms. GC 2024.

Held at the Oval Tapinac Grounds, the event saw 18 delegates representing their respective colleges vying for the coveted titles.
